title: tikv_alloc: remove thread from thread_arena_map when thread exits (#19479)
body:
This is an automated cherry-pick of #19479

<!--
Thank you for contributing to TiKV!

If you haven't already, please read TiKV's [CONTRIBUTING](https://github.com/tikv/tikv/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md) document.

If you're unsure about anything, just ask; somebody should be along to answer within a day or two.

PR Title Format:
1. module [, module2, module3]: what's changed
2. *: what's changed
-->

### What is changed and how it works?
<!--
Please create an issue first to describe the problem.

There MUST be one line starting with "Issue Number:  " and 
linking the relevant issues via the "close" or "ref".

For more info, check https://github.com/tikv/tikv/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md#linking-issues.
-->

Issue Number: Close #19478

<!--
You could use "commit message" code block to add more description to the final commit message.
For more info, check https://github.com/tikv/tikv/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md#format-of-the-commit-message.
-->
What's Changed:

```commit-message
Fix inaccurate `tikv_allocator_thread_stats` reporting in the jemalloc-backed
  allocator metrics.

  `tikv_allocator_thread_stats` is derived from per-arena jemalloc stats grouped
  by
  trimmed thread name. The collector could over-report in two cases:

  1. `THREAD_ARENA_MAP` entries were not removed when a thread exited, so stale
     arena stats could remain visible in the metric.
  2. Arena stats were summed without deduplicating `(thread_name, arena_index)`,
     so the same arena could be counted more than once for one logical thread
     group.

  This patch fixes both issues by removing the current thread from
  `THREAD_ARENA_MAP` in `remove_thread_memory_accessor()` and deduplicating
  arena
  indices during `iterate_arena_allocation_stats()`
```

### Related changes

- [ ] PR to update `pingcap/docs`/`pingcap/docs-cn`:
- [ ] Need to cherry-pick to the release branch

### Check List

Tests <!-- At least one of them must be included. -->

- [ ] Unit test
- [ ] Integration test
- [ ] Manual test (add detailed scripts or steps below)
- [ ] No code

Side effects

- [ ] Performance regression: Consumes more CPU
- [ ] Performance regression: Consumes more Memory
- [ ] Breaking backward compatibility

### Release note
<!-- 
Compatibility change, improvement, bugfix, and new feature need a release note.

Please refer to [Release Notes Language Style Guide](https://pingcap.github.io/tidb-dev-guide/contribute-to-tidb/release-notes-style-guide.html) to write a quality release note.

If you don't think this PR needs a release note then fill it with None.
If this PR will be picked to release branch, then a release note is probably required.
-->

```release-note
None
```


<!-- This is an auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->
## Summary by CodeRabbit

* **Bug Fixes**
  * Prevented double-counting in memory allocation statistics by deduplicating entries that map to the same arena after trimming thread identifiers.
  * Ensured thread memory tracking is fully removed when a thread is cleared so stale entries no longer persist.

* **Tests**
  * Added tests to confirm thread memory tracking is cleaned up correctly.
  * Added tests verifying allocation-statistics deduplication yields a single reported entry for equivalent mappings.
<!-- end of auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->
author: ti-chi-bot

## Pull request overview

This PR introduces a new Google Cloud Storage (GCS) “v2” backend (using the official google-cloud Rust crates) and wires it into TiKV’s external storage selection behind a new config flag, alongside a matching GCP KMS v2 provider.

**Changes:**
- Add `backup.gcs-v2-enable` config option (default off) and plumb it into `external_storage` backend selection.
- Add new `components/cloud/gcs_v2` crate implementing GCS v2 `BlobStorage` + GCP KMS v2 provider, plus tests.
- Update workspace dependencies and lockfile to include google-cloud-* crates and their transitive HTTP stack.

Thanks for the solid implementation work on gcs_v2 and backup-stream integration. I left several design-focused suggestions around config ownership, client initialization deduplication, typed credential mode, and bounded-memory uploads. Overall, the direction looks promising, and addressing these points should make the code easier to evolve and more robust at scale. Great progress, and looking forward to the next iteration.

body:

Thank you for contributing to TiKV!

If you haven't already, please read TiKV's [CONTRIBUTING](https://github.com/tikv/tikv/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md) document.

If you're unsure about anything, just ask; somebody should be along to answer within a day or two.

### What is changed and how it works?

For more info, check https://github.com/tikv/tikv/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md#linking-issues.

Issue Number: Ref #19373

<!--
You could use "commit message" code block to add more description to the final commit message.
For more info, check https://github.com/tikv/tikv/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md#format-of-the-commit-message.
-->
What's Changed:

```commit-message
resource_metering, raftstore: split region cpu by scheduler and unified read pools

Break down region CPU usage into scheduler and unified-read pools from resource metering. Report both pools in store heartbeats, keep hotspot admission based on unified-read CPU, and preserve the pooled total when split values are rounded.
```

Break down region cpu by scheduler and unified read pools from resource metering, and only report read hotspot from unified read pool in store heartbeat from cpu.

Improve hotspot reporting by splitting region CPU usage into scheduler and unified-read pools and reporting the per-pool breakdown in store heartbeats.
```


<!-- This is an auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->
## Summary by CodeRabbit

* **New Features**
  * Per-pool CPU tracking (unified-read vs scheduler) and per-region CPU breakdowns.
  * New Prometheus gauge exposing store CPU pool usage.

* **Improvements**
  * Hotspot reporting: unified-read admission while reporting both pools and preserving totals.
  * Heartbeat and peer selection expanded to include scheduler CPU Top‑N and rounding-gap corrections.

* **Tests**
  * Updated and added unit tests for CPU breakdowns, rounding-gap, and hotspot behavior.

* **Chores**
  * Updated Cargo dependency reference to a pinned revision.
<!-- end of auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->
author: lhy1024

reviews.body:
## Pull request overview

This PR extends TiKV’s resource metering CPU accounting to split per-region CPU time by thread pool (scheduler vs unified read pool) and propagates that breakdown into PD heartbeat reporting and hotspot selection logic, with accompanying tests and metrics.

**Changes:**
- Add per-thread-pool CPU attribution in `resource_metering` (new `ThreadPoolType`, new per-pool fields in `RawRecord`, and aggregation via `RegionCpuRecord`).
- Update raftstore (v1 and v2) PD heartbeat paths to report both unified-read and scheduler CPU usage, and include scheduler CPU in Top-N hotspot selection/reporting.
- Add a new raftstore Prometheus gauge to compare store-level CPU (ThreadInfoStatistics) vs region-level summed CPU by pool.

### Reviewed changes

Copilot reviewed 10 out of 11 changed files in this pull request and generated 1 comment.

<details>
<summary>Show a summary per file</summary>

| File | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |
| components/resource_metering/src/reporter/mod.rs | Update tests to use `..Default::default()` after `RawRecord` gains new fields. |
| components/resource_metering/src/recorder/sub_recorder/cpu.rs | Classify threads by name prefix and attribute CPU deltas into per-pool counters. |
| components/resource_metering/src/model.rs | Introduce `ThreadPoolType`, `RegionCpuRecord`, and per-pool CPU fields/merge logic on `RawRecord`. |
| components/raftstore/src/store/worker/split_controller.rs | Update tests for expanded `RawRecord` defaults. |
| components/raftstore/src/store/worker/pd.rs | Store + region heartbeat now compute/report per-pool CPU stats; add per-pool Top-N selection and store-vs-region-sum metrics. |
| components/raftstore/src/store/metrics.rs | Register new `tikv_raftstore_cpu_pool_usage` gauge vec. |
| components/raftstore-v2/src/worker/pd/store.rs | Mirror v1 store-heartbeat per-pool CPU reporting + Top-N selection, with tests. |
| components/raftstore-v2/src/worker/pd/region.rs | Mirror v1 region-hear

**Actionable comments posted: 2**

title: Throttle background traffic
body:
<!--
Thank you for contributing to TiKV!

If you haven't already, please read TiKV's [CONTRIBUTING](https://github.com/tikv/tikv/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md) document.

If you're unsure about anything, just ask; somebody should be along to answer within a day or two.

PR Title Format:
1. module [, module2, module3]: what's changed
2. *: what's changed
-->

### What is changed and how it works?
<!--
Please create an issue first to describe the problem.

There MUST be one line starting with "Issue Number:  " and 
linking the relevant issues via the "close" or "ref".

For more info, check https://github.com/tikv/tikv/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md#linking-issues.
-->

Issue Number: Close #19401

<!--
You could use "commit message" code block to add more description to the final commit message.
For more info, check https://github.com/tikv/tikv/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md#format-of-the-commit-message.
-->
What's Changed:

```commit-message
1. Introduced compaction-pressure tracking to dynamically throttle write operations under high compaction load.
2. Modify that how it calculate CPU resources and back off background traffic if online traffic uses majority of the resources. 
```

### Related changes

- [ ] PR to update `pingcap/docs`/`pingcap/docs-cn`:
- [ ] Need to cherry-pick to the release branch

### Check List

Tests <!-- At least one of them must be included. -->

- [X] Unit test
- [ ] Integration test
- [ ] Manual test (add detailed scripts or steps below)
- [ ] No code

Side effects

- [ ] Performance regression: Consumes more CPU
- [ ] Performance regression: Consumes more Memory
- [ ] Breaking backward compatibility

### Release note

<!-- 
Compatibility change, improvement, bugfix, and new feature need a release note.

Please refer to [Release Notes Language Style Guide](https://pingcap.github.io/tidb-dev-guide/contribute-to-tidb/release-notes-style-guide.html) to write a quality release note.

If you don't think this PR needs a release note then fill it with None.
If this PR will be picked to release branch, then a release note is probably required.
-->

```release-note
Introduced compaction-pressure tracking to dynamically throttle write operations under high compaction load.
```

## Resource Control: Background Task Throttling Configuration                                                                                                                
                                                                                                                                                                               
  Four new dynamic configuration options for controlling background task resource usage and compaction-pressure-based write IO throttling.                                     
                                                                                                                                                                               
  ### `resource-control.bg-resource-threshold`
                                                                                                                                                                               
  Overall resource utilization percentage (CPU/IO) above which background tasks are throttled. Also caps the background utilization limit. When system utilization exceeds this
   threshold, background task budgets are linearly scaled down toward a minimum floor, freeing resources for online traffic.

  - **Type:** Float (percentage, 1.0–99.0)
  - **Default:** `70.0`
  - **Dynamic:** Yes

  ### `resource-control.bg-compaction-pressure-threshold`

  Compaction pending bytes ratio (percentage) at which background write IO throttling begins. Below this threshold, write IO ramps up toward the ceiling. At and above this
  threshold, write IO is linearly scaled down from ceiling to floor as pressure approaches 100%.

  - **Type:** Float (percentage, 1.0–99.0)
  - **Default:** `70.0`
  - **Dynamic:** Yes

  ### `resource-control.bg-write-io-ceiling`

  Maximum write IO rate for background tasks. Applied when compaction pressure is below the threshold. This is the upper bound that write IO will ramp up to when there is no
  compaction pressure.

  - **Type:** ReadableSize (bytes/s)
  - **Default:** `100GB`
  - **Dynamic:** Yes

  ### `resource-control.bg-write-io-floor`

  Minimum write IO rate that background tasks are always allowed, even under maximum compaction pressure. This is the lower bound — write IO will never be throttled below this
   value.

  - **Type:** ReadableSize (bytes/s)
  - **Default:** `10MB`
  - **Dynamic:** Yes


<!-- This is an auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->
## Summary by CodeRabbit

* **New Features**
  * Added compaction-pressure tracking to coordinate throttling across components.

* **Improvements**
  * Dynamic write-IO throttling based on compaction pressure and per-group usage.
  * Dedicated write IO limiter for finer-grained throttling.
  * Quota adjustment now applies a capped background utilization and proportional distribution.
  * New runtime config: compaction pressure threshold (default 70%).

* **Tests**
  * Tests updated to cover compaction-pressure–aware throttling and multi-group distribution.
<!-- end of auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->
author: mittalrishabh

reviews.body:
## Pull request overview

This PR introduces a compaction pressure-based write IO throttling mechanism and refactors the resource quota adjustment algorithm. The changes add monitoring of compaction pending bytes and use this information to dynamically throttle write IO when compaction pressure is high (≥70%). Additionally, the quota adjustment algorithm has been simplified from an expected-cost-based approach to a headroom-based approach that adjusts limits based on available resource headroom.

**Changes:**
- Added compaction pressure monitoring through a shared `Arc<AtomicU32>` field passed between `EnginesResourceInfo` and `GroupQuotaAdjustWorker`
- Introduced a dedicated write-only IO limiter in `ResourceLimiter` for independent write throttling based on compaction pressure
- Replaced the complex expected-cost-based quota adjustment algorithm with a simpler headroom-based algorithm that adjusts limits proportionally based on target utilization minus current usage

### Reviewed changes

Copilot reviewed 6 out of 6 changed files in this pull request and generated no comments.

| components/resource_control/src/worker.rs | Refactored quota adjustment algorithm from expected-cost-based to headroom-based; added `adjust_write_io_by_compaction_pressure()` method; updated tests to reflect new algorithm behavior |
| components/resource_control/src/resource_limiter.rs | Added `

- Medium: components/resource_control/src/worker.rs:160-173 and :283-314 hard-cap background work at 70% even with no foreground pressure. That silently overrides higher background_settings.utilization_limit values and regresses background-only throughput beyond the scope of issue #19401.
- Medium: components/resource_control/src/worker.rs:351-399 fails open for newly active or recently idle writers under high compaction pressure. When the previous interval’s write delta is zero, the new write-only limiter is reset to INFINITY, so a bursty background job can run unthrottled for a full 10-second adjustment cycle.

commits.message:
queue fairness

Signed-off-by: rishabh mittal <mittalrishabh@gmail.com>

resource_control: revert queue fairness and simplify do_adjust

Revert the queue fairness commit and replace the background resource
allocation logic in do_adjust with a simpler headroom-based feedback
loop. Instead of decomposing foreground/background usage and doing
two-pass allocation with sorting, use:

  headroom = target - current_used
  new_budget = clamp(current_limit + headroom, min_floor, target)

This correctly throttles offline traffic when overall CPU utilization
is high, regardless of whether background or foreground caused it.

title: tikv_alloc: remove thread from thread_arena_map when thread exits
body:
<!--
Thank you for contributing to TiKV!

If you haven't already, please read TiKV's [CONTRIBUTING](https://github.com/tikv/tikv/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md) document.

If you're unsure about anything, just ask; somebody should be along to answer within a day or two.

PR Title Format:
1. module [, module2, module3]: what's changed
2. *: what's changed
-->

### What is changed and how it works?
<!--
Please create an issue first to describe the problem.

There MUST be one line starting with "Issue Number:  " and 
linking the relevant issues via the "close" or "ref".

For more info, check https://github.com/tikv/tikv/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md#linking-issues.
-->

Issue Number: Close #19478

<!--
You could use "commit message" code block to add more description to the final commit message.
For more info, check https://github.com/tikv/tikv/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md#format-of-the-commit-message.
-->
What's Changed:

```commit-message
Fix inaccurate `tikv_allocator_thread_stats` reporting in the jemalloc-backed
  allocator metrics.

  `tikv_allocator_thread_stats` is derived from per-arena jemalloc stats grouped
  by
  trimmed thread name. The collector could over-report in two cases:

  1. `THREAD_ARENA_MAP` entries were not removed when a thread exited, so stale
     arena stats could remain visible in the metric.
  2. Arena stats were summed without deduplicating `(thread_name, arena_index)`,
     so the same arena could be counted more than once for one logical thread
     group.

  This patch fixes both issues by removing the current thread from
  `THREAD_ARENA_MAP` in `remove_thread_memory_accessor()` and deduplicating
  arena
  indices during `iterate_arena_allocation_stats()`
```

### Related changes

- [ ] PR to update `pingcap/docs`/`pingcap/docs-cn`:
- [ ] Need to cherry-pick to the release branch

### Check List

Tests <!-- At least one of them must be included. -->

- [ ] Unit test
- [ ] Integration test
- [ ] Manual test (add detailed scripts or steps below)
- [ ] No code

Side effects

- [ ] Performance regression: Consumes more CPU
- [ ] Performance regression: Consumes more Memory
- [ ] Breaking backward compatibility

### Release note

title: cdc: add watchdog for cdc conn
body:
<!--
Thank you for contributing to TiKV!

If you haven't already, please read TiKV's [CONTRIBUTING](https://github.com/tikv/tikv/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md) document.

If you're unsure about anything, just ask; somebody should be along to answer within a day or two.

PR Title Format:
1. module [, module2, module3]: what's changed
2. *: what's changed
-->

### What is changed and how it works?
<!--
Please create an issue first to describe the problem.

There MUST be one line starting with "Issue Number:  " and 
linking the relevant issues via the "close" or "ref".

For more info, check https://github.com/tikv/tikv/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md#linking-issues.
-->


Issue Number: Close #18169 

The main intent is to resolve a problem where the CDC sink's memory quota increases indefinitely after a downstream TiCDC node disconnects. This leak persists until the TiKV instance is manually restarted. The underlying cause is suspected to be dangling connections that are not properly released after the downstream becomes unavailable.

In summary, this PR resolves the issue through the following steps:

1. Introduce Watchdog: A background task is created specifically for monitoring.

2. Register Connections: New CDC connections are registered with the watchdog upon creation.

3. Periodic Checks: The watchdog periodically checks the last_flush_time of each connection against a timeout.

4. Release Connections: If a connection is determined to be stuck, the watchdog forcibly disconnects it, releasing its associated memory and resources.

This mechanism ensures that dangling connections are promptly cleaned up even if a downstream TiCDC node becomes unresponsive, preventing the unbounded memory growth and improving the system's stability.

<!--
You could use "commit message" code block to add more description to the final commit message.
For more info, check https://github.com/tikv/tikv/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md#format-of-the-commit-message.
-->

```commit-message

```

curl -X PUT "http://127.0.0.1:20180/fail/cdc_sleep_after_sink_flush"    -d "return(true)"
```
5. It can be seen that all changefeed got stuck and.
6. After 30 seconds, the cdc connections were closed, and the downstream cdc will try to create a new connection 
```
[2025/07/31 16:27:48.884 +08:00] [ERROR] [service.rs:568] ["cdc connection idle for too long, aborting connection"] [seconds_since_last_flush=40] [conn_id=ConnId(217)] [downstream=ipv4:127.0.0.1:49452] [thread_id=170]
[2025/07/31 16:27:48.884 +08:00] [ERROR] [service.rs:568] ["cdc connection idle for too long, aborting connection"] [seconds_since_last_flush=40] [conn_id=ConnId(216)] [downstream=ipv4:127.0.0.1:49452] [thread_id=170]
[2025/07/31 16:27:48.902 +08:00] [ERROR] [service.rs:568] ["cdc connection idle for too long, aborting connection"] [seconds_since_last_flush=40] [conn_id=ConnId(219)] [downstream=ipv4:127.0.0.1:49453] [thread_id=170]
[2025/07/31 16:27:48.902 +08:00] [ERROR] [service.rs:568] ["cdc connection idle for too long, aborting connection"] [seconds_since_last_flush=40] [conn_id=ConnId(218)] [downstream=ipv4:127.0.0.1:49453] [thread_id=170]
[2025/07/31 16:27:49.887 +08:00] [INFO] [service.rs:448] ["cdc connection created"] [conn_id=ConnId(228)] [features="[\"stream-multiplexing\"]"] [downstream=ipv4:127.0.0.1:49737] [thread_id=9]
[2025/07/31 16:27:49.889 +08:00] [INFO] [service.rs:448] ["cdc connection created"] [conn_id=ConnId(229)] [features="[\"stream-multiplexing\"]"] [downstream=ipv4:127.0.0.1:49737] [thread_id=9]

title: crossbeam-skiplist: fix RefRange iterator memory leak
body:
### What is changed and how it works?

Issue Number: Ref #19285

What's Changed:

This is a backport of #19284 to `release-8.5-20260318-v8.5.5`.

```commit-message
RefRange::next() and next_back() used clone_from() to update
self.head/self.tail. Because RefEntry has no Drop implementation,
the replaced entry was dropped without decrementing its refcount,
which leaked memory.

Use mem::replace() and explicitly decrement the old entry, matching
RefIter::next(). This backport also carries the ignored stress test
that exercises SkipMap::range() under insert/remove churn.
```

### Related changes

- [ ] PR to update `pingcap/docs`/`pingcap/docs-cn`:
- [ ] Need to cherry-pick to the release branch

### Check List

Tests

- [x] Unit test
- [ ] Integration test
- [x] Manual test (add detailed scripts or steps below)
- [ ] No code

Manual test steps:

- `cargo nextest run --manifest-path components/crossbeam-skiplist/Cargo.toml`
- `cargo test -p concurrency_manager --test memory_usage stress_skipmap_range_iter -- --ignored --nocapture`
- Observed the stress test memory usage return to `0MB` after the workload stopped.

Side effects

- [ ] Performance regression: Consumes more CPU
- [ ] Performance regression: Consumes more Memory
- [ ] Breaking backward compatibility

### Release note

```release-note
Fix a memory leak in crossbeam-skiplist's RefRange iterator that caused LockTable memory to grow unboundedly when using range queries.
```
author: ekexium

commits.message:
crossbeam-skiplist: fix RefRange iterator memory leak

RefRange::next() and next_back() were using clone_from() to update
self.head/self.tail. Since RefEntry has no Drop implementation,
the old entry was dropped without decrementing its refcount, causing
permanent memory leaks.

Fix by using mem::replace() and manually calling decrement() on the
old entry, matching the pattern used in RefIter::next().

title: backup-stream: ensure monotonically increasing flush_ts per store (#19407)
body:
This is an automated cherry-pick of #19407

### What is changed and how it works?

Issue Number: Close #19406, Close #18262.

What's Changed:

```commit-message
backup-stream: ensure monotonically increasing flush_ts per store

Meta path format is updated to a deterministic, parseable layout:

- Old: v1/backupmeta/{flush_ts}-{min_begin_ts}-{min_ts}-{max_ts}[-SYNTHETIC...].meta
- New: v1/backupmeta/{flush_ts:016X}{store_id:016X}-d{min_begin_ts:016X}l{min_ts:016X}u{max_ts:016X}.meta

Also:
- Removed SYNTHETIC/UUID-based randomized suffixes.
- Added parser/validation (`parse_backupmeta_filename`) and tests for the new filename format.
- Flush skips on PD TSO failure, so no synthetic meta path is produced.

Motivation: make every meta have a deterministic ID while preserving the original ordering behavior as much as possible.
```

This also picks #18221.

### Related changes

- [ ] PR to update `pingcap/docs`/`pingcap/docs-cn`:
- [ ] Need to cherry-pick to the release branch

### Check List

Tests

- [x] Unit test
- [x] Integration test
- [ ] Manual test (add detailed scripts or steps below)
- [ ] No code

Side effects

- [ ] Performance regression: Consumes more CPU
- [ ] Performance regression: Consumes more Memory
- [ ] Breaking backward compatibility

### Release note

```release-note
None
```

<!-- This is an auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->
## Summary by CodeRabbit

* **New Features**
  * Added an opt-in compilation feature to enable vendored OpenSSL support for improved macOS compatibility.
  * Exposed deterministic, parseable backup metadata filenames with explicit parsing/validation support.

* **Improvements**
  * Flush timestamps are now strictly monotonic and preserved across coordination-service failures with a local fallback.
  * Enhanced error handling and clearer failure reporting during timestamp allocation outages.

* **Tests**
  * Added integration tests covering monotonic flush behavior and recovery from timestamp allocation failures.
<!-- end of auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->

title: resolved_ts: fix OOM due to rts tasks pile up on channel (#19294)
body: This is an automated cherry-pick of #19294 ### What is changed and how it works? Issue Number: Close #18359 What's Changed: ```commit-message This pull request fixes #18359 by introducing improvements to memory quota management and region re-registration in the resolved-ts component. The changes ensure that when memory usage exceeds the configured quota, all observed regions are re-registered to free up memory, and random backoff is applied to avoid thundering herd problems. Additionally, the observer and endpoint now share a memory quota instance, and active checks for memory quota are performed periodically. ``` **Memory quota management and region re-registration:** * Added logic in `endpoint.rs` to re-register all observed regions when memory quota is exceeded, dropping pending logs and applying a random backoff to each region to avoid simultaneous re-registration. (`components/resolved_ts/src/endpoint.rs`) [[1]](diffhunk://#diff-10705daa95aaa15a28a65ade2c5207efb566f6267991ac2e8f15ddfdfd245119R885-R916) [[2]](diffhunk://#diff-10705daa95aaa15a28a65ade2c5207efb566f6267991ac2e8f15ddfdfd245119R945) [[3]](diffhunk://#diff-10705daa95aaa15a28a65ade2c5207efb566f6267991ac2e8f15ddfdfd245119L921-L925) [[4]](diffhunk://#diff-10705daa95aaa15a28a65ade2c5207efb566f6267991ac2e8f15ddfdfd245119L960-R1006) * Introduced periodic active memory quota checks in the endpoint's main loop, triggering region re-registration if quota is exceeded. (`components/resolved_ts/src/endpoint.rs`) **Memory quota sharing and observer updates:** * Modified the `Observer` struct to accept and use a shared `Arc`, ensuring consistent quota tracking between observer and endpoint. (`components/resolved_ts/src/observer.rs`) [[1]](diffhunk://#diff-bb54c37a7c24d8163143fa395e02b7c5106d169280de371f5eb826da7406e1c1R3-R23) [[2]](diffhunk://#diff-bb54c37a7c24d8163143fa395e02b7c5106d169280de371f5eb826da7406e1c1R46) [[3]](diffhunk://#diff-bb54c37a7c24d8163143fa395e02b7c5106d169280de371f5eb826da7406e1c1R72) * Updated observer test to initialize with the new memory quota parameter. (`components/resolved_ts/src/observer.rs`) **Endpoint and configuration improvements:** * Changed the endpoint to receive the memory quota instance via constructor instead of creating its own, and added tracking for last active memory quota check. (`components/resolved_ts/src/endpoint.rs`) [[1]](diffhunk://#diff-10705daa95aaa15a28a65ade2c5207efb566f6267991ac2e8f15ddfdfd245119R693) [[2]](diffhunk://#diff-10705daa95aaa15a28a65ade2c5207efb566f6267991ac2e8f15ddfdfd245119L712-R715) [[3]](diffhunk://#diff-10705daa95aaa15a28a65ade2c5207efb566f6267991ac2e8f15ddfdfd245119R725) [[4]](diffhunk://#diff-10705daa95aaa15a28a65ade2c5207efb566f6267991ac2e8f15ddfdfd245119R412) * Enhanced logging to include backoff information and improved error handling by distinguishing between different error causes. (`components/resolved_ts/src/endpoint.rs`) [[1]](diffhunk://#diff-10705daa95aaa15a28a65ade2c5207efb566f6267991ac2e8f15ddfdfd245119L735-R739) [[2]](diffhunk://#diff-10705daa95aaa15a28a65ade2c5207efb566f6267991ac2e8f15ddfdfd245119L853-R857) [[3]](diffhunk://#diff-10705daa95aaa15a28a65ade2c5207efb566f6267991ac2e8f15ddfdfd245119L866-R870) [[4]](diffhunk://#diff-10705daa95aaa15a28a65ade2c5207efb566f6267991ac2e8f15ddfdfd245119L1158-R1218) **Testing and dependency updates:** * Updated failpoint and resolved-ts tests to call `suite.run()` for proper initialization. (`components/resolved_ts/tests/failpoints/mod.rs`) [[1]](diffhunk://#diff-ec0d36f74ae27d945af0aca997fb29412f6f5011d7c9591f41528b5b232eb8fcR22) [[2]](diffhunk://#diff-ec0d36f74ae27d945af0aca997fb29412f6f5011d7c9591f41528b5b232eb8fcR68) [[3]](diffhunk://#diff-ec0d36f74ae27d945af0aca997fb29412f6f5011d7c9591f41528b5b232eb8fcR112) [[4]](diffhunk://#diff-ec0d36f74ae27d945af0aca997fb29412f6f5011d7c9591f41528b5b232eb8fcR157) * Added the `rand` crate as a dependency for generating random backoff durations. (`components/resolved_ts/Cargo.toml`, `components/resolved_ts/src/endpoint.rs`) [[1]](diffhunk://#diff-c2f4882f7a8e8623413aa8f361becbaeb23d4c39f27766b4c599bf6c8d86f794R42) [[2]](diffhunk://#diff-10705daa95aaa15a28a65ade2c5207efb566f6267991ac2e8f15ddfdfd245119R30) **Other improvements:** * Improved the accuracy of the `CmdBatch::size()` method to better account for memory usage of command batches. component. The changes ensure that when memory usage exceeds the
configured quota, all observed regions are re-registered to free up
memory, and random backoff is applied to avoid thundering herd problems.
Additionally, the observer and endpoint now share a memory quota
instance, and active checks for memory quota are performed periodically.

Signed-off-by: Neil Shen <overvenus@gmail.com>

title: txn: Implement compare-and-delete semantics for RawCompareAndSwap
body:
<!--
Thank you for contributing to TiKV!

If you haven't already, please read TiKV's [CONTRIBUTING](https://github.com/tikv/tikv/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md) document.

If you're unsure about anything, just ask; somebody should be along to answer within a day or two.

PR Title Format:
1. module [, module2, module3]: what's changed
2. *: what's changed
-->

### What is changed and how it works?
<!--
Please create an issue first to describe the problem.

There MUST be one line starting with "Issue Number:  " and 
linking the relevant issues via the "close" or "ref".

For more info, check https://github.com/tikv/tikv/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md#linking-issues.
-->
Issue Number: Close #18838 

This PR extends the rawkv CompareAndSwap API to allow for atomic key deletions. It allows users to achieve atomic compare and delete semantics. The implementation utilizes a new field `delete` in the `RawCASRequest` message in [kvrpcpb.proto](https://github.com/pingcap/kvproto/pull/1420). If set we will delete `key` if  `current_val==expected_val`. 


<!--
You could use "commit message" code block to add more description to the final commit message.
For more info, check https://github.com/tikv/tikv/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md#format-of-the-commit-message.
-->
What's Changed:

```commit-message
Implements "CompareAndDelete" semantics for the rawkv API. If the `delete` flag is set in the `RawCASRequest` message and `current_val==expected_val` then we will delete the key.
```

### Related changes

`57-60`: **`write_bytes` slightly underestimates for ApiV2 logical delete.**

For V1, `Modify::Delete` stores no value, so `0` is correct. For V2, the actual write is `Modify::Put(cf, key_with_ts_suffix, ApiV2::ENCODED_LOGICAL_DELETE)`, so the bytes written are `key.len() (+ ts suffix) + ENCODED_LOGICAL_DELETE.len()`. ## Pull request overview

Extends TiKV’s RawKV `CompareAndSwap` to support atomic compare-and-delete semantics via a `delete` flag on `RawCasRequest`, wiring it through the gRPC service into the storage command path and adding coverage (including CDC expectations).

**Changes:**
- Add `delete: bool` plumbing for `RawCompareAndSwap` (command + `Storage::raw_compare_and_swap_atomic` + service handler).
- Implement delete semantics in the CAS write path (physical delete for V1/V1ttl; logical delete marker for API V2).
- Expand integration/unit/CDC tests and update `kvproto` dependency lock.

title: txn: avoid xlock starvation by setting slock shrink-only (#19313)
body:
This is an automated cherry-pick of #19313

<!--
Thank you for contributing to TiKV!

If you haven't already, please read TiKV's [CONTRIBUTING](https://github.com/tikv/tikv/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md) document.

If you're unsure about anything, just ask; somebody should be along to answer within a day or two.

-->

### What is changed and how it works?
<!--
Please create an issue first to describe the problem.

There MUST be one line starting with "Issue Number:  " and 
linking the relevant issues via the "close" or "ref".

For more info, check https://github.com/tikv/tikv/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md#linking-issues.
-->

Issue Number: ref #19087

<!--
You could use "commit message" code block to add more description to the final commit message.
For more info, check https://github.com/tikv/tikv/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md#format-of-the-commit-message.
-->
What's Changed:

```commit-message
Avoid exclusive lock starvation by setting shared locks shrink-only:

- `acquire_pessimistic_lock` returns `NotInShrinkMode` when an exclusive lock meets non-shrink-only shared locks.
- `AcquirePessimisticLock.process_write` converts shared locks to shrink-only, persists them, and returns `KeyIsLocked` to the client.
- The scheduler skips in-memory pessimistic lock writes when a shared-lock update is being persisted.
```

- [ ] Need to cherry-pick to the release branch

author: zyguan

reviews.body:
## Pull request overview

This PR expands TiKV unit test coverage for the shared-lock feature and adjusts `TxnHeartBeat` behavior to explicitly reject heartbeats sent to a shared-locked key (since shared-locked keys are not valid transaction primaries).

**Changes:**
- Reject `TxnHeartBeat` on shared-locked keys with a `PrimaryMismatch` error, and add unit tests for this rejection behavior.
- Add tests covering ResolveLock read/write phase progress for large shared-lock sets and mixed commit/rollback resolution on the same key.
- Add tests verifying commit/cleanup correctness when multiple shared sub-locks on the same key are processed in a single batch via pending lock bytes, plus an MVCC scan-lock limit test across mixed keys.

### Reviewed changes

Copilot reviewed 6 out of 6 changed files in this pull request and generated no comments.

<details>
<summary>Show a summary per file</summary>

| File | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |
| `src/storage/txn/commands/txn_heart_beat.rs` | Changes heartbeat handling to reject shared-lock keys and adds unit tests asserting rejection and no lock mutation. |
| `src/storage/txn/commands/resolve_lock_readphase.rs` | Adds tests ensuring ResolveLock readphase makes progress across multiple batches for a large shared-lock set on a single key. |
| `src/storage/txn/commands/resolve_lock.rs` | Adds a unit test for resolving shared sub-locks on the same key with mixed commit/rollback outcomes. |
| `src/storage/txn/actions/commit.rs` | Adds a unit test verifying commit reads pending shared-lock bytes so multi-sub-lock batches behave correctly. |
| `src/storage/txn/actions/cleanup.rs` | Adds a unit test verifying cleanup reads pending shared-lock bytes so multi-sub-lock batches behave correctly. |
| `src/storage/mvcc/reader/reader.rs` | Adds a unit test ensuring scan-lock limit across mixed exclusive + shared lock keys truncates shared sub-locks by smallest ts. |
</details>

title: backup-stream: ensure monotonically increasing flush_ts per store
body:
### What is changed and how it works?

Issue Number: Close #19406

What's Changed:

```commit-message
backup-stream: ensure monotonically increasing flush_ts per store

Meta path format is updated to a deterministic, parseable layout:

- Old: v1/backupmeta/{flush_ts}-{min_begin_ts}-{min_ts}-{max_ts}[-SYNTHETIC...].meta
- New: v1/backupmeta/{flush_ts:016X}{store_id:016X}-d{min_begin_ts:016X}l{min_ts:016X}u{max_ts:016X}.meta

Also:
- Removed SYNTHETIC/UUID-based randomized suffixes.
- Added parser/validation (`parse_backupmeta_filename`) and tests for the new filename format.
- Flush skips on PD TSO failure, so no synthetic meta path is produced.

Motivation: make every meta have a deterministic ID while preserving the original ordering behavior as much as possible.
```

### Related changes

- [ ] PR to update `pingcap/docs`/`pingcap/docs-cn`:
- [ ] Need to cherry-pick to the release branch

### Check List

Tests

- [x] Unit test
- [x] Integration test
- [ ] Manual test (add detailed scripts or steps below)
- [ ] No code

Side effects

- [ ] Performance regression: Consumes more CPU
- [ ] Performance regression: Consumes more Memory
- [ ] Breaking backward compatibility

### Release note

```release-note
None
```

<!-- This is an auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->
## Summary by CodeRabbit

* **New Features**
  * Added an opt-in compilation feature to enable vendored OpenSSL support for improved macOS compatibility.
  * Exposed deterministic, parseable backup metadata filenames with explicit parsing/validation support.

* **Improvements**
  * Flush timestamps are now strictly monotonic and preserved across coordination-service failures with a local fallback.
  * Enhanced error handling and clearer failure reporting during timestamp allocation outages.

* **Tests**
  * Added integration tests covering monotonic flush behavior and recovery from timestamp allocation failures.
<!-- end of auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->
author: YuJuncen

reviews.body:
## Pull request overview

This PR fixes non-deterministic / non-monotonic `flush_ts` behavior in backup-stream metadata filenames when PD is temporarily unavailable, to preserve chronological ordering required by downstream compaction and RPO calculations.

**Changes:**
- Track and generate strictly increasing `flush_ts` per store (`last_flush_ts` + `next_flush_ts`) instead of falling back to `0` / synthetic timestamps.
- Make `prepare_min_ts()` return `Result` and skip flush when PD TSO cannot be obtained.
- Remove synthetic filename logic (`SYNTHETIC` suffix + UUID) from metadata paths and add an integration test covering PD TSO failure/recovery.

### Reviewed changes

Copilot reviewed 4 out of 4 changed files in this pull request and generated 3 comments.

| File | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |
| components/backup-stream/src/endpoint.rs | Introduces monotonic `flush_ts` generation and propagates PD TSO errors to skip flushes. |
| components/backup-stream/src/router.rs | Removes synthetic `.meta` naming fallback and updates related unit test. |
| components/backup-stream/tests/failpoints/mod.rs | Adds integration test validating monotonic `flush_ts` across PD failure and recovery. |
| components/backup-stream/Cargo.toml | Adds `openssl-vendored` feature (not described in PR text). |


<details>
<summary>Comments suppressed due to low confidence (1)</summary>

**components/backup-stream/Cargo.toml:22**
* This PR also adds an `openssl-vendored` feature (and enables `encryption_export/sm4`) in `components/backup-stream/Cargo.toml`, but the PR description/title focuses on `flush_ts` monotonicity. If this feature addition is intentional, please document why it’s needed here (or move it to a dedicated PR) to avoid surprising build/packaging changes.
```
# Enable this if you cannot compile `grpcio` when running test cases in macOS.
openssl-vendored = [
  "openssl/vendored",
  "grpcio/openssl-vendored",
  "encryption_export/sm4",
]
backup-stream-debug = []

[[test]

**Actionable comments posted: 1**

<details>
<summary>🤖 Prompt for all review comments with AI agents</summary>

```
Verify each finding against the current code and only fix it if needed.

Inline comments:
In `@components/backup-stream/src/endpoint.rs`:
- Around line 109-114: The code currently re-enables local/synthetic flush_ts
fallback after the first PD TSO error, allowing flush to proceed with synthetic
timestamps; change the logic so that when PD TSO allocation fails the code does
not flip or re-enable allow_flush_tso_fallback and instead skips the flush
entirely. Locate the flush/TSO allocation flow (references:
allow_flush_tso_fallback, last_flush_ts) and: 1) on PD allocation error, do not
set allow_flush_tso_fallback = true or otherwise fall back to generating a local
flush_ts; 2) ensure last_flush_ts is only updated when a valid PD-provided
flush_ts is obtained; and 3) return/skip the flush path on PD unavailability so
no synthetic timestamps are produced.
```

</details>

<details>

<details>
<summary>components/backup-stream/src/endpoint.rs (1)</summary><blockquote>

`867-879`: _⚠️ Potential issue_ | _🟠 Major_

**Remove local/synthetic `flush_ts` fallback on PD failure.**

This block still allows flush to continue with `next_flush_ts(TimeStamp::zero())` after a PD error. That conflicts with the requirement to skip flushes when PD is unreachable and avoid synthetic timestamps.

<details>
<summary>🔧 Proposed fix</summary>

```diff
 fn prepare_min_ts_and_flush_ts(&mut self) -> Result<(TimeStamp, TimeStamp)> {
     let hnd = self.pool.handle().clone();
-    match hnd.block_on(self.prepare_min_ts()) {
-        Ok((min_ts, pd_tso)) => Ok((min_ts, self.next_flush_ts(pd_tso))),
-        Err(err) => {
-            if self.last_flush_ts == 0 {
-                return Err(err);
-            }
-            err.report("failed to get TSO for flushing, fallback to local monotonic ts");
-            let flush_ts = self.next_flush_ts(TimeStamp::zero());
-            // Don't use synthetical tso to advance resolved_ts.
-            Ok((TimeStamp::zero(), flush_ts))
-        }
-    }
+    let (min_ts, pd_tso) = hnd.block_on(self.prepare_min_ts())?;
+    Ok((min_ts, self.next_flush_ts(pd_tso)))
 }
```
</details>

<details>
<summary>🤖 Prompt for AI Agents</summary>

```

returns (TimeStamp::zero(), flush_ts)); instead, on Err(err) from
hnd.block_on(self.prepare_min_ts()) propagate/return that error (you may still
log/report via err.report if desired) and do not advance resolved_ts locally.
Remove the special-case check against self.last_flush_ts and any use of
TimeStamp::zero() as a synthetic TSO; e

reviews.body: ## Pull request overview This PR adds a minimum sleep interval between auto-compaction rounds in TiKV’s GC worker compaction runner, aiming to avoid immediately starting a new round after one completes (so MVCC read tracking can accumulate stats after being reset). **Changes:** - Enforces a minimum 20s delay between compaction rounds by adjusting the post-round sleep duration. - Replaces “sleep remaining check interval or immediately rerun” behavior with a computed sleep duration based on `max(remaining_sleep, MIN_GAP_BETWEEN_ROUNDS)`. --- 💡 Add Copilot custom instructions for smarter, more guided reviews. Learn how to get started.

**Actionable comments posted: 1**

<details>
<summary>🤖 Fix all issues with AI agents</summary>

```
In `@src/server/gc_worker/compaction_runner.rs`:
- Around line 300-319: Replace the hardcoded MIN_GAP_BETWEEN_ROUNDS const with a
configurable field on AutoCompactionConfig (e.g., min_gap_between_rounds:
Duration with a default of 20s) and use that field instead of the local const in
the compaction loop where config.auto_compaction.mvcc_read_aware_enabled is
checked (the branch that computes sleep_duration and calls self.sleep_or_stop).
Also add a unit test that sets AutoCompactionConfig.check_interval short,
enables mvcc_read_aware_enabled, simulates elapsed > check_interval, and asserts
the sleep duration enforced is at least the configured min_gap_between_rounds.
```

</details>

reviews.body:
## Pull request overview

This PR wires RUv2 metrics into `ExecDetailsV2.ru_v2` across TiKV service paths so clients receive accurate RUv2 reporting for KV, txn, batch-get-command, and coprocessor requests.

**Changes:**
- Populate `ExecDetailsV2.ru_v2` from the TLS tracker in KV / txn / batch-get-command responses and in coprocessor exec details.
- Track additional RUv2 inputs: storage processed-keys, coprocessor response bytes, and coprocessor executor work/iterations.
- Add unit tests validating RUv2 fields are set for representative request paths.

### Reviewed changes

Copilot reviewed 17 out of 18 changed files in this pull request and generated 2 comments.

<details>
<summary>Show a summary per file</summary>

| ---- | ----------- |
| src/storage/mod.rs | Updates TLS tracker metrics for processed keys in get / batch-get / buffer-batch-get paths. |
| src/server/service/kv.rs | Writes RUv2 into `exec_details_v2` for KV and txn responses; adds RUv2 unit tests. |
| src/server/service/batch.rs | Plumbs tracker tokens into batch-get-command response construction and writes RUv2 per-item. |
| src/coprocessor/tracker.rs | Writes RUv2 into coprocessor `ExecDetailsV2` from the TLS tracker. |
| src/coprocessor/endpoint.rs | Tracks coprocessor response bytes into TLS tracker before building exec details; adds unit test. |
| components/tracker/src/lib.rs | Introduces `write_ru_v2` and RUv2-related per-request metrics fields in the tracker. |
| components/tidb_query_executors/src/util/scan_executor.rs | Records executor work (proxy) into TLS tracker for scan executors. |
| components/tidb_query_executors/src/top_n_executor.rs | Records executor work proxy for TopN comparisons. |
| components/tidb_query_executors/src/table_scan_executor.rs | Passes executor name to ScanExecutor for work attribution. |
| components/tidb_query_executors/src/simple_aggr_executor.rs | Records executor work proxy for simple aggregation. |

`42-49`: **Potential truncation when casting count to u32.**

The `count()` result (a `usize`) is cast to `u32` with `as u32`. On 64-bit platforms, if an expression has more than ~4 billion ColumnRef nodes (extremely unlikely in practice), this would silently truncate.

This is a theoretical edge case with negligible practical impact, but for consistency you could use `try_into().unwrap_or(u32::MAX)` or just document the assumption.

<!-- This is an auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->
## Summary by CodeRabbit

* **New Features**
  * Improved lock management: new truncation of shared lock groups to enforce per-scan limits.

* **Bug Fixes**
  * Consistent lock-counting across individual and grouped (shared) locks; scan stops when overall limit reached.
  * Decode behavior adjusted to ignore non Put/Delete locks in release builds (with debug logging retained).

* **Tests**
  * Added tests ensuring limits correctly cap keys/locks when shared lock groups are present.
<!-- end of auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->
author: you06

reviews.body: ## Pull request overview This PR fixes a bug in `scan_locks_from_storage` where the limit parameter counted keys instead of individual locks. When a key has SharedLocks (multiple locks from different transactions on the same key), the previous implementation would return all locks for that key even if they exceeded the specified limit. **Changes:** - Modified `scan_locks_from_storage` to count individual locks instead of keys, properly truncating SharedLocks when they would exceed the limit - Added `truncate_to` method to SharedLocks for removing excess locks while keeping those with the smallest timestamps - Added test case to verify the fix works correctly ### Reviewed changes Copilot reviewed 2 out of 2 changed files in this pull request and generated 2 comments. | File | Description | | ---- | ----------- | | src/storage/mvcc/reader/reader.rs | Updated scan_locks_from_storage to track lock_count separately, truncate SharedLocks to respect the limit, and update statistics to reflect individual locks instead of keys | | components/txn_types/src/lock.rs | Added truncate_to method to SharedLocks that keeps locks with smallest timestamps for deterministic behavior | --- 💡 Add Copilot custom instructions for smarter, more guided reviews. Learn how to get started.

**Actionable comments posted: 1**

<details>
<summary>🤖 Fix all issues with AI agents</summary>

```
In `@src/storage/mvcc/reader/reader.rs`:
- Around line 2001-2063: The test
test_scan_locks_from_storage_shared_locks_limit has outdated comments and an
assertion message claiming the limit counts KEYS (and will fail), but the
implementation enforces a per-lock hard limit by truncating SharedLocks; update
the test narrative, comments, and the assertion failure message to state that
the limit is applied per individual lock and that SharedLocks may be truncated
to satisfy the limit (reference functions/types: scan_locks_from_storage,
SharedLocks, total_locks, MvccReader::scan_locks_from_storage,
test_scan_locks_from_storage_shared_locks_limit) so the assertion and
accompanying message reflect per-lock limiting rather than key-count limiting.
```

</details>

<!-- This is an auto-generated comment by CodeRabbit for review status -->

**Actionable comments posted: 1**
🤖 Fix all issues with AI agents ``` In `@src/storage/mvcc/reader/reader.rs`: - Line 782: Processed keys metric is inconsistent: processed_keys is incremented by lock_count (sum of locks) in the code path using lock_count, but scan_locks increments by 1 per key for Either, producing divergent metrics; to fix, make semantics uniform by changing scan_locks to increment processed_keys by shared_locks.len() when encountering SharedLocks (or alternatively change the processed_keys += lock_count site to += 1 if you prefer per-key counting)—update the logic in the scan_locks function and the code path using lock_count (symbols: scan_locks, processed_keys, lock_count, Either, SharedLocks) so both paths use the same counting rule. ```
🧹 Nitpick comments (1)
src/storage/mvcc/reader/reader.rs (1)
`2001-2060`: **LGTM! Test correctly validates the SharedLocks limit enforcement.** The test adequately verifies that: 1. SharedLocks containing multiple individual locks are properly truncated 2. The limit applies to individual locks, not keys Consider adding additional test cases for more comprehensive coverage: - `limit=3` with 5 SharedLocks (to verify partial truncation) - Mixed scenario with regular locks followed by SharedLocks - Verifying `has_remain` is correctly set after truncation

unit test steps
1. set this config
```
mvcc-read-aware-enabled = true
mvcc-scan-threshold = 1
mvcc-read-weight = 1000.0
redundant-rows-threshold = 1000000
redundant-rows-percent-threshold = 100
```
2. run sysbench with one hot key write
`sysbench oltp_read_only --mysql-ignore-errors=1213,1020,1205,1105 --point_selects=0 --range_selects=true --range_size=2 --simple_ranges=1 --sum_ranges=0 --order_ranges=0 --distinct_ranges=0 --distinct_ranges=0 --skip_trx=true --tables=1 --table_size=1 --threads=64 --mysql-host=127.0.0.1 --mysql-user=root --mysql-db=test --mysql-port=4000 --report-interval=10 --time=3000 run

title: config: support percentage specification for ReadableSize values
body:
### What is changed and how it works?

Issue Number: Close #19413

What's Changed:

Allow `ReadableSize` config values (e.g., `block_cache.capacity`, `memory_usage_limit`) to be specified as percentages of system memory. This enables VPA (Vertical Pod Autoscaling) and dynamic memory environments to use configs like `capacity = "45%"` instead of hardcoded absolute values.

only one format is supported:
- **String percentage**: `capacity = "45%"` (parsed via `FromStr`)

I disabled the following float style based on the following discussion.
> -  **Float ratio**: `capacity = 0.45` (parsed via `visit_f64` in `Deserialize`)-
https://github.com/tikv/tikv/pull/19419#discussion_r2894926313



Both resolve to concrete bytes at parse time using `SysQuota::memory_limit_in_bytes()`. Serialization outputs the resolved absolute value, so on restart the percentage in `tikv.toml` is re-evaluated against current system memory.

```commit-message
Support percentage specification for ReadableSize config values.

Extend ReadableSize to accept "45%" string format, resolving to a percentage of system memory at parse time.
This allows configs like block_cache.capacity and memory_usage_limit to
adapt automatically to available system memory without manual
recalculation, which is useful for VPA and dynamic memory environments.
```

### Related changes

- [ ] PR to update `pingcap/docs`/`pingcap/docs-cn`:
- [ ] Need to cherry-pick to the release branch

### Check List

Tests

- [x] Unit test
- [x] Integration test
- [ ] Manual test (add detailed scripts or steps below)
- [ ] No code

Side effects

- [ ] Performance regression: Consumes more CPU
- [ ] Performance regression: Consumes more Memory
- [ ] Breaking backward compatibility

### Release note

```release-note
Support specifying `ReadableSize` config values (e.g., `storage.block-cache.capacity`, `memory-usage-limit`) as percentages of system memory using string format (`"45%"`), enabling automatic adaptation to available system memory in VPA and dynamic memory environments.
```

<!-- This is an auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->
## Summary by CodeRabbit

* **New Features**
  * Memory-related configuration fields (including block-cache capacity and suggested limits) now accept percentages (0–100%) and float ratios (0.0–1.0) as well as absolute sizes; percentages and ratios resolve against system memory and are supported in TOML.

* **Bug Fixes**
  * Improved validation and clearer errors for out-of-range or non-numeric percentage/ratio inputs.

* **Tests**
  * Added comprehensive parsing, serialization/TOML round-trip, boundary, and end-to-end percentage-resolution tests.
<!-- end of auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->
author: takaidohigasi

commits.message:
[tikv/tikv#19417] config: support percentage specification for ReadableSize values

Allow ReadableSize config values (e.g., block_cache.capacity,
memory_usage_limit) to be specified as percentages of system memory.
This enables VPA and dynamic memory environments to use configs like
`capacity = "45%"` or `capacity = 0.45` instead of hardcoded absolute
values.

Two formats are supported:
- String percentage: `capacity = "45%"` (parsed via FromStr)
- Float ratio: `capacity = 0.45` (parsed via visit_f64 in Deserialize)

Both resolve to concrete bytes at parse time using
SysQuota::memory_limit_in_bytes(). Serialization outputs the resolved
absolute value, so on restart the percentage in tikv.toml is
re-evaluated against current system memory.

Signed-off-by: taka-h <taka21.hiroshi@gmail.com>
Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
Signed-off-by: takaidohigasi <takaidohigasi@gmail.com>

[tikv/tikv#19417] config: address review comments

- Use SysQuota::memory_limit_in_bytes_current() instead of
  memory_limit_in_bytes() so percentage values resolve against the
  current cgroup memory limit rather than a stale cached value,
  which matters for VPA environments.
- Compute ratio as n / 100.0 first then multiply, for consistency
  with the visit_f64 path and to minimize rounding error.
- Add float-ratio test for memory-usage-limit (0.75) to match the
  string percentage test ("75%") for full format symmetry.

Signed-off-by: taka-h <taka21.hiroshi@gmail.com>
Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
Signed-off-by: takaidohigasi <takaidohigasi@gmail.com>

[tikv/tikv#19417] config: introduce ReadableSizeOrPercent type

Introduce a new ReadableSizeOrPercent(pub u64) type that restricts
percentage-based memory configuration to only the two fields where it
semantically makes sense: memory-usage-limit and
storage.block-cache.capacity.

Previously, percentage support was added directly to ReadableSize, which
meant every ReadableSize config field (block-size, target-file-size-base,
min-blob-size, etc.) would accept "45%" or 0.45, which is nonsensical
for non-memory-proportional fields.

Changes:
- Remove percentage parsing from ReadableSize (FromStr and Deserialize)
- Add ReadableSizeOrPercent type with percentage/float-ratio support
- Change BlockCacheConfig.capacity to Option<ReadableSizeOrPercent>
- Change TikvConfig.memory_usage_limit to Option<ReadableSizeOrPercent>
- Use SysQuota::memory_limit_in_bytes() (cached) instead of
  memory_limit_in_bytes_current() for consistency

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
Signed-off-by: takaidohigasi <takaidohigasi@gmail.com>

fix fmt error

Signed-off-by: takaidohigasi <takaidohigasi@gmail.com>

fix clippy assertions_on_result_states warnings

Replace assert!(x.is_err()) with x.unwrap_err() in ReadableSizeOrPercent
tests to satisfy clippy::assertions_on_result_states lint.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
Signed-off-by: takaidohigasi <takaidohigasi@gmail.com>

add Debug derive to test structs for unwrap_err

Required by unwrap_err() which needs T: Debug.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
Signed-off-by: takaidohigasi <takaidohigasi@gmail.com>

fix type mismatch in integration test for block cache capacity

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
Signed-off-by: takaidohigasi <takaidohigasi@gmail.com>

config: remove float ratio (visit_f64) support from ReadableSizeOrPercent

TOML float ratios like `capacity = 0.45` are misleading in a size-typed
field since `1` means 1 byte but `1.0` would mean 100% of memory.
Only `"45%"`-style string percentages should be supported.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
Signed-off-by: takaidohigasi <takaidohigasi@gmail.com>

title: copr: support new global index key value format V2 (#19276)
body:
This is an automated cherry-pick of #19276

<!--

### What is changed and how it works?
<!--

linking the relevant issues via the "close" or "ref".

For more info, check https://github.com/tikv/tikv/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md#linking-issues.
-->

Issue Number: Ref #19262

<!--
You could use "commit message" code block to add more description to the final commit message.
For more info, check https://github.com/tikv/tikv/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md#format-of-the-commit-message.
-->
What's Changed:
Global Index V1 added the partition id to the key, to make non-unique global indexes on non-clustered partitioned tables handle duplicate _tidb_rowid's between partitions.
V2 removes the partition id from the value part.

V1 and V2 is separated, since V1 can still use the same index read functions, that rely on the partition id in the value, while v2 needs to use the partition id encoded in the key part instead.


```commit-message
copr: Non-unique global indexes for non-clustered tables are now only storing the partition id in the key instead of the value (or both as in V1).
```

title: resource_metering: collect network/io info in TopSQL (#18923)
body:
This is an automated cherry-pick of #18923

<!--
Thank you for contributing to TiKV!

If you haven't already, please read TiKV's [CONTRIBUTING](https://github.com/tikv/tikv/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md) document.

If you're unsure about anything, just ask; somebody should be along to answer within a day or two.

PR Title Format:
1. module [, module2, module3]: what's changed
2. *: what's changed
-->

### What is changed and how it works?
<!--
Please create an issue first to describe the problem.

There MUST be one line starting with "Issue Number:  " and 
linking the relevant issues via the "close" or "ref".

For more info, check https://github.com/tikv/tikv/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md#linking-issues.
-->

Issue Number: Close #18815 

<!--
You could use "commit message" code block to add more description to the final commit message.
For more info, check https://github.com/tikv/tikv/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md#format-of-the-commit-message.
-->
What's Changed:

```commit-message
Add network/io info collection for TopSQL:
1. Introduce resource-metering.enable-network-io-collection config to control whether enable this new feature. Default is disabled.
2. Collect network_in, network_out, logical_read, logical_write execution info and recorded in TopSQL:
    i. Since the LocalStorage of TopSQL recorder is TLS, and only be accessed inside the thread with attached tag. Here, we use GLOBAL_TRACKERS to help record network_in data size.
    ii. For network_out, we can only directly get resp's size for Coprocessor request. Thus we need to collect this data one by one for all requests. Since we only care about requests that potentially generate large response, we bypass some "write requests" whose response only contained "commit_ts" data.
    iii. Use the processed_size(https://github.com/tikv/tikv/blob/1deb3a135dc41c3ca227e3d5a29712526b492a4c/components/tikv_kv/src/stats.rs#L195) as logical read size
    iv. Use the scheduled tasks' write_bytes() as logical write size
```

commits.message:
This is an automated cherry-pick of #18923

close tikv/tikv#18815

Signed-off-by: ti-chi-bot <ti-community-prow-bot@tidb.io>

Resolve conflicts

Signed-off-by: yibin87 <huyibin@pingcap.com>

topsql: Read enable_network_io_collection config when tikv initialization and make it take effect (#19076)

ref tikv/tikv#18815

In https://github.com/tikv/tikv/pull/18923, we introduce a global variable "ENABLE_NETWORK_IO_COLLECTION" to control if network/io info should be collected.
It is initialized to be false, and will be updated when we use tikv-ctl to change the "resource-metering.enable-network-io-collection" config value dynamically.
However, when we set this config value to true in tikv config file, and restart tikv-server, the global variable will not be set to true due to no changes detected.
So, in this PR, we update the global variable value when tikv-server initialization using config value.

<!--
You could use "commit message" code block to add more description to the final commit message.
For more info, check https://github.com/tikv/tikv/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md#format-of-the-commit-message.
-->
What's Changed:

```commit-message
In https://github.com/tikv/tikv/pull/18923, we introduce a global variable "ENABLE_NETWORK_IO_COLLECTION" to control if network/io info should be collected. 
It is initialized to be false, and will be updated when we use tikv-ctl to change the "resource-metering.enable-network-io-collection" config value dynamically. 
However, when we set this config value to true in tikv config file, and restart tikv-server, the global variable will not be set to true due to no changes detected.
So, in this PR, we update the global variable value when tikv-server initialization using config value.
```

## Summary by CodeRabbit

* **New Features**
  * Unified read pool can evict lower-priority queued tasks to admit higher-priority incoming tasks.
  * Priority handling adds a non‑destructive "peek" path to check priorities without advancing scheduling state.

* **New Metrics**
  * Added a metric counting evicted unified read pool tasks.

* **Tests**
  * Added tests and helpers validating eviction behavior and metric increments.

* **Chores**
  * Updated the source of an external build dependency.
<!-- end of auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->
author: mittalrishabh

reviews.body:
## Pull request overview This pull request implements "queue fairness" in the unified read pool by allowing higher-priority incoming tasks to evict lower-priority queued tasks when the pool is full. The implementation adds an eviction mechanism that uses resource group priorities to determine which queued tasks can be preempted. **Changes:** - Adds eviction logic in the read pool that attempts to evict the lowest-priority queued task when a higher-priority task arrives and the pool is full - Implements `approximate_priority_of` method in ResourceController to calculate task priority without side effects (virtual time increment) - Adds a new metric `tikv_unified_read_pool_evicted_tasks` to track evicted tasks - Includes tests for the eviction mechanism ### Reviewed changes Copilot reviewed 5 out of 6 changed files in this pull request and generated 7 comments.
Show a summary per file | File | Description | | ---- | ----------- | | src/read_pool.rs | Implements queue fairness logic with task eviction, adds eviction metrics, and includes a test for the eviction mechanism | | components/resource_control/src/resource_group.rs | Adds `approximate_priority_of` method for priority calculation without virtual time increment, includes unit test | | Cargo.toml | Changes yatp dependency to local path (development-only change) | | components/resource_control/Cargo.toml | Changes yatp dependency to local path (development-only change) | | components/raftstore-v2/Cargo.toml | Changes yatp dependency to local path (development-only change) | | Cargo.lock | Removes git source for yatp package |

`939-944`: **`UNIFIED_READ_POOL_EVICTED_TASKS` lacks a pool-name label, losing per-pool observability.**

`UNIFIED_READ_POOL_RUNNING_TASKS` uses `["name", "priority"]` labels. The eviction counter being label-free (a) makes it impossible to attribute eviction events to a specific pool in dashboards/alerts and (b) causes test-isolation issues when parallel tests trigger eviction (the global counter leaks across test cases, making `get() >= 1` assertions unreliable).

-            )
-            .unwrap();
+        pub static ref UNIFIED_READ_POOL_EVICTED_TASKS: IntCounterVec =

+                &["name"]
+            )

```

Then in the eviction path, pass the pool name. One approach is to store the pool name as a field in `ReadPoolHandle::Yatp` (similar to how `running_tasks` is initialized per-pool in `build_yatp_read_pool_with_name`):

```diff

      running_tasks,

-     UNIFIED_READ_POOL_EVICTED_TASKS.inc();

`693-701`: **Add an inline comment explaining why `vt_delta_for_get` is added in the non-advancing branch.**

When `advance_vt = false`, the code loads the current VT and still adds `vt_delta_for_get`. This is intentional: it approximates the priority the incoming task *would have* if scheduled immediately (a conservative upper-bound estimate), without mutating the group's state. Without a comment, this looks like a copy-paste of the advancing branch with the `fetch_add` swapped for a `load`.



<details>
<summary>♻️ Suggested comment addition</summary>

```diff
-        let vt = (if advance_vt && self.vt_delta_for_get > 0 {
+        // When advance_vt is false (peek mode for eviction comparison), load the
+        // current VT without mutating it. We still add vt_delta_for_get to
+        // estimate what this task's priority *would be* if it were admitted next,
+        // giving a conservative upper-bound that errs on the side of not evicting.
+        let vt = (if advance_vt && self.vt_delta_for_get > 0 {
             self.virtual_time
                 .fetch_add(self.vt_delta_for_get, Ordering::Relaxed)
                 + self.vt_delta_for_get
         } else {
             self.virtual_time.load(Ordering::Relaxed) + self.vt_delta_for_get
         }) + task_extra_priority;
```

title: Add backgroud IOPS limiter
body: ### What is changed and how it works? Issue Number: Close #18616 What's Changed: This PR aims to resolve the issue where IOPS bursts caused by analyze requests (eg. auto/manual analyze) exceed the IOPS limit. A new background IOPS limiter is added and user can set limit by modifying config param quota. background_iops_limit. New metrics for analyze are also added: - Analyze read count per second - Analyze next batch count per second - Disk IO count per second ```commit-message ``` Test Result: Before setting the background IOPS limit (unlimited): After setting the background IOPS limit to 1000: After setting the background IOPS limit to 100: ### Related changes - [ ] PR to update `pingcap/docs`/`pingcap/docs-cn`: - [x] Need to cherry-pick to the release branch ### Check List Tests - [x] Unit test - [ ] Integration test - [x] Manual test (add detailed scripts or steps below) - [ ] No code Side effects - [ ] Performance regression: Consumes more CPU - [ ] Performance regression: Consumes more Memory - [ ] Breaking backward compatibility ### Release note ```release-note A new background IOPS limiter is introduced. Users can configure the limit by modifying the configuration parameter quota.background-iops. Both manual and automatic analyze requests are subject to this limiter. ``` ## Summary by CodeRabbit * **New Features** * Configurable background IOPS quota added (default: 0). * New analyze metrics exposed: read IOPS and next-batch counts. * New dashboard panels for disk I/O count and analyze metrics. * Analyze now tracks per-request storage stats and reports IOPS/next-batch metrics, using background quota consumption. * **Bug Fixes** * Fixed Disk IO bytes/sec unit display on the monitoring dashboard. * **Tests** * Added tests covering IOPS throttling and interactions with other quota limits.
author: jiadebin

reviews.body:
Thanks for working on this!

The main challenge is the user's continuous manual analyze script instead of automatic analyze.

## Pull request overview

This pull request adds a background IOPS (I/O operations per second) limiter to TiKV to address IOPS bursts caused by background analyze requests that can exceed cloud environment IOPS quotas. The implementation uses RocksDB PerfContext counters (specifically `block_read_count`) to track and limit IOPS for analyze operations.

**Changes:**
- Added `background_iops` configuration parameter to the quota config with a default of 0 (unlimited)
- Implemented IOPS tracking and limiting in the QuotaLimiter using the same rate-limiting infrastructure as existing bandwidth limiters
- Modified analyze operations to track IOPS per batch and apply the limiter, applying background limits to both manual and auto-analyze operations
- Added new Grafana metrics: analyze block read count, analyze next batch count, and disk IO count per second

title: txn: avoid xlock starvation by setting slock shrink-only
body:
<!--
Thank you for contributing to TiKV!

If you haven't already, please read TiKV's [CONTRIBUTING](https://github.com/tikv/tikv/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md) document.

If you're unsure about anything, just ask; somebody should be along to answer within a day or two.

PR Title Format:
1. module [, module2, module3]: what's changed
2. *: what's changed
-->

### What is changed and how it works?
<!--
Please create an issue first to describe the problem.

There MUST be one line starting with "Issue Number:  " and 
linking the relevant issues via the "close" or "ref".

For more info, check https://github.com/tikv/tikv/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md#linking-issues.
-->

Issue Number: ref #19087

<!--
You could use "commit message" code block to add more description to the final commit message.
For more info, check https://github.com/tikv/tikv/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md#format-of-the-commit-message.
-->
What's Changed:

```commit-message
Avoid exclusive lock starvation by setting shared locks shrink-only:

- `acquire_pessimistic_lock` returns `NotInShrinkMode` when an exclusive lock meets non-shrink-only shared locks.
- `AcquirePessimisticLock.process_write` converts shared locks to shrink-only, persists them, and returns `KeyIsLocked` to the client.
- The scheduler skips in-memory pessimistic lock writes when a shared-lock update is being persisted.
```

### Related changes

- [ ] PR to update `pingcap/docs`/`pingcap/docs-cn`:
- [ ] Need to cherry-pick to the release branch

### Check List

Tests <!-- At least one of them must be included. -->

- [x] Unit test
- [ ] Integration test
- [ ] Manual test (add detailed scripts or steps below)
- [ ] No code

Side effects

- [ ] Performance regression: Consumes more CPU
- [ ] Performance regression: Consumes more Memory
- [ ] Breaking backward compatibility

### Release note
<!-- 
Compatibility change, improvement, bugfix, and new feature need a release note.

Please refer to [Release Notes Language Style Guide](https://pingcap.github.io/tidb-dev-guide/contribute-to-tidb/release-notes-style-guide.html) to write a quality release note.

If you don't think this PR needs a release note then fill it with None.
If this PR will be picked to release branch, then a release note is probably required.
-->

```release-note
None
```
author: zyguan

reviews.body:
## Pull request overview

This PR refines the interaction between exclusive (xlock) and shared (slock) pessimistic locks to avoid exclusive-lock starvation when shared locks are present, especially under in-memory pessimistic locking.

**Changes:**
- Introduces a new MVCC error `NotInShrinkMode(SharedLocks)` to represent xlock encountering shared locks that are not yet marked shrink-only, and wires it through the error and cloning infrastructure.
- Updates `acquire_pessimistic_lock`/`AcquirePessimisticLock` to (a) surface `NotInShrinkMode` from the MVCC layer, (b) convert shared locks to shrink-only, persist them, and then return a `KeyIsLocked` error containing a shared-lock `LockInfo`.
- Extends the scheduler to detect `KeyIsLocked` responses carrying shared-lock info and, in that case, skip the in-memory pessimistic lock fast path to ensure the shared-lock shrink-only update is actually persisted; adds tests around shared-lock behavior and the in-memory mode skip.

### Reviewed changes

Copilot reviewed 8 out of 8 changed files in this pull request and generated no comments.

<details>
<summary>Show a summary per file</summary>

| File | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |
| `src/storage/txn/scheduler.rs` | Adds detection of `KeyIsLocked` on a shared lock (`LockInfoExt::is_shared_lock`) and prevents in-memory pessimistic lock insertion when persisting a shared-lock shrink-only update; adds a unit test ensuring the scheduler writes such updates to storage and not the in-memory table. |
| `src/storage/txn/mod.rs` | Exposes `ProcessResult::get_key_lock_info` to extract the inner `LockInfo` from `PessimisticLockRes` errors, enabling the scheduler’s new shared-lock detection. |
| `src/storage/txn/commands/acquire_pessimistic_lock.rs` | On `NotInShrinkMode`, clears prior mutations, marks the shared locks as shrink-only, writes them back, and then turns the result into a `KeyIsLocked` error with shared-lock info; adds an integration-style test for shared-lock seman

```

author: glorv

reviews.body: looks sensible to me

(By Claude + Codex)

## Code Review Summary

Nice fix for a real production issue — follower replica reads hanging forever on disk-full nodes. The core logic is correct and well-placed. A few suggestions below.

### HIGH

1. **Typo in comment and test**
   - `peer.rs`: `"will be block"` → `"will be blocked"`
   - `test_disk_full.rs`: `"should be reject"` → `"should be rejected"`

### MEDIUM

2. **Missing `AlreadyFull` test case**
   The condition uses `!= DiskUsage::Normal`, which covers both `AlmostFull` and `AlreadyFull`. But the test only covers `AlmostFull`. Consider adding an `AlreadyFull` case too.

3. **No `#[test_case]` for raftstore-v2**
   Other disk-full tests in this file use `#[test_case(test_raftstore::new_node_cluster)]` and `#[test_case(test_raftstore_v2::new_node_cluster)]`. This new test only covers v1.

4. **raftstore-v2 has the same bug**
   `read_index_follower` in `components/raftstore-v2/src/operation/query/replica.rs` has no disk-full check. It will have the same stuck-forever problem. Worth tracking as a follow-up.

### LOW

5. **No debug log on the rejection path**
   All other early-exit paths in `read_index()` have a `debug!` log. Adding one here would help production debugging:
   ```rust
   debug!(
       "reject follower replica read when disk is full";
       "region_id" => self.region_id,
       "peer_id" => self.peer.get_id(),
       "disk_usage" => ?poll_ctx.self_disk_usage,
   );
   ```

6. **Consider `self.should_wake_up = true`**
   Other error paths in `read_index()` set this before returning. Not strictly needed since the request is immediately rejected, but would be consistent.

7. **Latent fragility in `ensure_disk_usage_is_reported!` macro**
   The macro now uses `async_read_index_on_peer`. In v1 this doesn't set `replica_read` (so it bypasses the new guard — correct). But in v2, `async_read_index_on_peer` DOES set `replica_read=true`. If v2 later gets the same disk-full check, this m

body:
<!--
Thank you for contributing to TiKV!

For more info, check https://github.com/tikv/tikv/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md#linking-issues.

Make S3Uploader honor 10000 parts limit in multipart S3 uploads, by increasing the part size if necessary.
This prevents log backup blockage in the scenarios of high write pressure (e.g. during lightning ingestion).

Also, make the multipart upload threshold configurable for log backups (existing s3_multi_part_size
option only applies to snapshots now).

## Summary by CodeRabbit
