author: vldmit

reviews.body: LGTM

## Pull request overview This PR fixes a critical bug in S3 multipart uploads for log backups where uploads would fail when exceeding 10,000 parts (the AWS S3 limit). The fix dynamically adjusts the part size to ensure the file fits within the 10,000 parts limit, preventing backup failures under high write pressure. Additionally, it makes the multipart upload threshold configurable specifically for log backups through a new `s3_multi_part_size` configuration parameter. **Changes:** - Added dynamic part size adjustment in S3Uploader to respect the 10,000 parts limit - Introduced `s3_multi_part_size` configuration parameter for log backups in BackupStreamConfig - Updated configuration validation to enforce a 5GB maximum for the new parameter ### Reviewed changes Copilot reviewed 3 out of 3 changed files in this pull request and generated 2 comments. | File | Description | | ---- | ----------- | | src/config/mod.rs | Adds `s3_multi_part_size` field to BackupStreamConfig with validation for maximum 5GB | | components/cloud/aws/src/s3.rs | Implements dynamic part size adjustment logic to honor 10,000 parts limit, adds MAX_PARTS constant, updates documentation, and includes comprehensive unit tests | | components/backup-stream/src/router.rs | Propagates `s3_multi_part_size` configuration through the router to storage backends via BackendConfig, updates all test cases to include the new parameter | --- 💡 Add Copilot custom instructions for smarter, more guided reviews. Learn how to get started.

**Actionable comments posted: 1**
🧹 Nitpick comments (1)
components/cloud/aws/src/s3.rs (1)
`1443-1478`: **Test duplicates the formula rather than exercising `S3Uploader::run`.** The test re-implements the part-size adjustment logic inline instead of testing the actual code path in `run()`. If the logic in `run()` changes, this test won't detect the regression. Also, line 1445 redeclares `MAX_PARTS` locally, shadowing `super::MAX_PARTS`—prefer using the module constant directly. Consider a test that constructs an `S3Uploader` (or at least calls a factored-out helper) to verify the real code path. That said, the arithmetic sanity check is still useful as-is.
🤖 Prompt for AI Agents ``` Verify each finding against the current code and only fix it if needed. In `@components/cloud/aws/src/s3.rs` around lines 1443 - 1478, The test duplicates the multipart sizing logic instead of exercising the real implementation; update the test to call the actual code path (either invoke S3Uploader::run with a controlled est_len input or call the existing helper used by run that computes effective part size) so changes to S3Uploader's logic are caught, and stop shadowing the module constant by replacing the local MAX_PARTS with super::MAX_PARTS (and use super::MINIMUM_PART_SIZE as needed); keep the arithmetic sanity assertions but derive effective_size and estimated_parts by invoking the production function (S3Uploader::run or the factored helper) rather than re-implementing the formula. ```
🤖 Prompt for all review comments with AI agents ``` Verify each finding against the current code and only fix it if needed. Inline comments: In `@src/config/mod.rs`: - Around line 3172-3178: The code in BackupStreamConfig (the self.s3_multi_part_size check) only enforces an upper bound; add

reviews.body: ## Pull request overview This pull request implements timeout handling for snapshot retrieval in the coprocessor endpoint to prevent blocked tasks in the unified read pool. When snapshot retrieval exceeds the request deadline, the operation now times out instead of blocking indefinitely. **Changes:** - Added `async_timeout` utility function for timeout handling in yatp FuturePool contexts - Implemented `remaining_duration` method on `Deadline` to calculate time until deadline - Refactored snapshot retrieval to use `get_snapshot_with_timeout` with deadline-based timeout checking ### Reviewed changes Copilot reviewed 3 out of 3 changed files in this pull request and generated 5 comments. | File | Description | | ---- | ----------- | | src/coprocessor/endpoint.rs | Added `get_snapshot_with_timeout` method and refactored unary/stream request handlers to use timeout-aware snapshot retrieval | | components/tikv_util/src/future.rs | Added `async_timeout` function for timeout handling and comprehensive unit tests | | components/tikv_util/src/deadline.rs | Added `remaining_duration` method to calculate time remaining until deadline | --- 💡 Add Copilot custom instructions for smarter, more guided reviews. title: resolved_ts: fix OOM due to rts tasks pile up on channel
body: ### What is changed and how it works? Issue Number: Close #18359 What's Changed: ```commit-message This pull request fixes #18359 by introducing improvements to memory quota management and region re-registration in the resolved-ts component. The changes ensure that when memory usage exceeds the configured quota, all observed regions are re-registered to free up memory, and random backoff is applied to avoid thundering herd problems. Additionally, the observer and endpoint now share a memory quota instance, and active checks for memory quota are performed periodically. ``` **Memory quota management and region re-registration:** * Added logic in `endpoint.rs` to re-register all observed regions when memory quota is exceeded, dropping pending logs and applying a random backoff to each region to avoid simultaneous re-registration. (`components/resolved_ts/src/endpoint.rs`) [[1]](diffhunk://#diff-10705daa95aaa15a28a65ade2c5207efb566f6267991ac2e8f15ddfdfd245119R885-R916) [[2]](diffhunk://#diff-10705daa95aaa15a28a65ade2c5207efb566f6267991ac2e8f15ddfdfd245119R945) [[3]](diffhunk://#diff-10705daa95aaa15a28a65ade2c5207efb566f6267991ac2e8f15ddfdfd245119L921-L925) [[4]](diffhunk://#diff-10705daa95aaa15a28a65ade2c5207efb566f6267991ac2e8f15ddfdfd245119L960-R1006) * Introduced periodic active memory quota checks in the endpoint's main loop, triggering region re-registration if quota is exceeded. (`components/resolved_ts/src/endpoint.rs`) **Memory quota sharing and observer updates:** * Modified the `Observer` struct to accept and use a shared `Arc`, ensuring consistent quota tracking between observer and endpoint. (`components/resolved_ts/src/observer.rs`) [[1]](diffhunk://#diff-bb54c37a7c24d8163143fa395e02b7c5106d169280de371f5eb826da7406e1c1R3-R23) [[2]](diffhunk://#diff-bb54c37a7c24d8163143fa395e02b7c5106d169280de371f5eb826da7406e1c1R46) [[3]](diffhunk://#diff-bb54c37a7c24d8163143fa395e02b7c5106d169280de371f5eb826da7406e1c1R72) * Updated observer test to initialize with the new memory quota parameter. (`components/resolved_ts/src/observer.rs`) **Endpoint and configuration improvements:** * Changed the endpoint to receive the memory quota instance via constructor instead of creating its own, and added tracking for last active memory quota check. (`components/resolved_ts/src/endpoint.rs`) [[1]](diffhunk://#diff-10705daa95aaa15a28a65ade2c5207efb566f6267991ac2e8f15ddfdfd245119R693) [[2]](diffhunk://#diff-10705daa95aaa15a28a65ade2c5207efb566f6267991ac2e8f15ddfdfd245119L712-R715) [[3]](diffhunk://#diff-10705daa95aaa15a28a65ade2c5207efb566f6267991ac2e8f15ddfdfd245119R725) [[4]](diffhunk://#diff-10705daa95aaa15a28a65ade2c5207efb566f6267991ac2e8f15ddfdfd245119R412) * Enhanced logging to include backoff information and improved error handling by distinguishing between different error causes. (`components/resolved_ts/src/endpoint.rs`) [[1]](diffhunk://#diff-10705daa95aaa15a28a65ade2c5207efb566f6267991ac2e8f15ddfdfd245119L735-R739) [[2]](diffhunk://#diff-10705daa95aaa15a28a65ade2c5207efb566f6267991ac2e8f15ddfdfd245119L853-R857) [[3]](diffhunk://#diff-10705daa95aaa15a28a65ade2c5207efb566f6267991ac2e8f15ddfdfd245119L866-R870) [[4]](diffhunk://#diff-10705daa95aaa15a28a65ade2c5207efb566f6267991ac2e8f15ddfdfd245119L1158-R1218) **Testing and dependency updates:** * Updated failpoint and resolved-ts tests to call `suite.run()` for proper initialization. (`components/resolved_ts/tests/failpoints/mod.rs`) [[1]](diffhunk://#diff-ec0d36f74ae27d945af0aca997fb29412f6f5011d7c9591f41528b5b232eb8fcR22) [[2]](diffhunk://#diff-ec0d36f74ae27d945af0aca997fb29412f6f5011d7c9591f41528b5b232eb8fcR68) [[3]](diffhunk://#diff-ec0d36f74ae27d945af0aca997fb29412f6f5011d7c9591f41528b5b232eb8fcR112) [[4]](diffhunk://#diff-ec0d36f74ae27d945af0aca997fb29412f6f5011d7c9591f41528b5b232eb8fcR157) * Added the `rand` crate as a dependency for generating random backoff durations. (`components/resolved_ts/Cargo.toml`, `components/resolved_ts/src/endpoint.rs`) [[1]](diffhunk://#diff-c2f4882f7a8e8623413aa8f361becbaeb23d4c39f27766b4c599bf6c8d86f794R42) [[2]](diffhunk://#diff-10705daa95aaa15a28a65ade2c5207efb566f6267991ac2e8f15ddfdfd245119R30) **Other improvements:** * Improved the accuracy of the `CmdBatch::size()` method to better account for memory usage of command batches. (`components/raftstore/src/coprocessor/mod.rs`) * Added a failpoint for testing after handling change logs. (`components/resolved_ts/src/endpoint.rs`) | Without this Fix | With this Fix | |--------|--------| | | | ### Related changes - [ ] PR to update `pingcap/docs`/`pingcap/docs-cn`: - [ ] Need to cherry-pick to the release branch ### Check List Tests - [ ] Unit test - [ ] Integration test - [ ] Manual test (add detailed scripts or steps below) - [ ] No code Side effects - [ ] Performance regression: Consumes more CPU - [ ] Performance regression: Consumes more Memory - [ ] Breaking backward compatibility ### Release note ```release-note Fix an OOM issue when resolved-ts thread is busy. ```
author: overvenus

reviews.body:
## Pull request overview

This PR fixes an OOM issue in the resolved-ts component by implementing comprehensive memory quota management. When memory usage exceeds the configured quota, the system now re-registers all observed regions with random backoff to avoid thundering herd problems, drops pending logs, and shares a memory quota instance between the observer and endpoint components.

**Changes:**
- Added two new configuration parameters for active memory quota checking and backoff duration
- Implemented shared memory quota between observer and endpoint with proper allocation/deallocation tracking
- Added active memory quota checks and automatic region re-registration when quota is exceeded
- Improved CmdBatch memory size calculation to include struct sizes and delete operations
- Refactored test suite to separate cluster initialization from cluster startup

### Reviewed changes

Copilot reviewed 13 out of 14 changed files in this pull request and generated 8 comments.

<details>
<summary>Show a summary per file</summary>

| File | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |
| src/config/mod.rs | Added `memory_quota_active_check_interval` and `memory_quota_exceeded_backoff_duration` config parameters |
| tests/integrations/config/test-custom.toml | Updated test config with new memory quota parameters |
| tests/integrations/config/mod.rs | Added test assertions for new config parameters |
| components/resolved_ts/src/observer.rs | Modified to accept and use shared memory quota for tracking pending change logs |
| components/resolved_ts/src/endpoint.rs | Implemented active memory quota checks, region re-registration with backoff, and proper memory deallocation |
| components/resolved_ts/tests/mod.rs | Refactored TestSuite to separate configuration from cluster startup |
| components/resolved_ts/tests/failpoints/mod.rs | Added comprehensive test for memory quota exceeded behavior and updated existing tests |
| components/resolved_ts/Cargo.toml | Added `rand` depen

commits.message:
copr: support decoding new global index key format

Signed-off-by: tangenta <tangenta@126.com>

add test for decode_int_handle_from_key

Signed-off-by: tangenta <tangenta@126.com>

copr: add tests and documentation for global index V1 format

Based on PR#19261, with the help from Claude Code to add tests etc.
Add comprehensive test coverage and documentation for V1 global index
format, where partition ID appears in both KEY and VALUE for non-unique
global indexes on non-clustered tables.

V1 Format:
- Key: [indexed_cols][PARTITION_ID_FLAG][partition_id][handle]
- Value: [tail_len][PARTITION_ID_FLAG][partition_id][...] (unchanged)

This dual-location approach minimizes code changes during migration from
legacy format (partition ID in value only) to future V2 format (partition
ID in key only).

Changes:
- Add documentation to decode_int_handle_from_key() explaining V1 format
  and migration strategy
- Add test_non_unique_global_index_v1_partition_in_key_and_value() to
  validate end-to-end V1 key/value handling
- Add test_v1_edge_cases() to test zero and negative partition IDs
- Add test_v1_backward_compatibility() to ensure old format still works

All tests verify that:
1. Partition ID in key doesn't break handle decoding (key skips it)
2. Partition ID from value is correctly extracted (existing code path)
3. All columns are output correctly including partition ID
4. Zero and negative partition IDs work correctly (i64 full range)
5. Legacy format (no partition ID in key) continues to work

reviews.body: ## Pull request overview This PR introduces a shrink-only flag to shared locks as part of the broader effort to support shared locks for foreign key constraints (issue #19087). The shrink-only flag prevents new shared locks from being added to a `SharedLocks` instance once set, enabling specific lock management patterns needed for foreign key constraint handling. **Changes:** - Added a `SharedLocksFlags` bitflags type with a `SHRINK_ONLY` flag to control whether new shared locks can be added - Modified `put_shared_lock` method to return `Result<()>` and fail when attempting to add locks to a shrink-only instance - Added a new `SharedLocksAreShrinkOnly` error type with appropriate error handling and conversion logic - Updated serialization/deserialization to persist the flags field with forward compatibility support ### Reviewed changes Copilot reviewed 3 out of 3 changed files in this pull request and generated 2 comments. | File | Description | | ---- | ----------- | | components/txn_types/src/lock.rs | Core implementation of shrink-only flag including bitflags definition, SharedLocks struct modification, put_shared_lock API change, serialization/deserialization updates, and comprehensive unit tests | | components/txn_types/src/lib.rs | New error type definition with appropriate error code mapping and cloning support | | src/storage/mvcc/mod.rs | Error conversion logic to handle SharedLocksAreShrinkOnly as an internal error | --- 💡 Add Copilot custom instructions for smarter, more guided reviews. title: raftstore: iops aggregation adaptive optimization
body:
<!--
Thank you for contributing to TiKV!

If you haven't already, please read TiKV's [CONTRIBUTING](https://github.com/tikv/tikv/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md) document.

If you're unsure about anything, just ask; somebody should be along to answer within a day or two.

PR Title Format:
1. module [, module2, module3]: what's changed
2. *: what's changed
-->

### What is changed and how it works?
<!--
Please create an issue first to describe the problem.

There MUST be one line starting with "Issue Number:  " and 
linking the relevant issues via the "close" or "ref".

For more info, check https://github.com/tikv/tikv/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md#linking-issues.
-->

Issue Number: Close #19392

<!--
You could use "commit message" code block to add more description to the final commit message.
For more info, check https://github.com/tikv/tikv/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md#format-of-the-commit-message.
-->
What's Changed:
The v8.x has introduced an adaptive batching strategy controlled by two key parameters: wait_duration (default: 20us) and batch_size_hint (default: 8KB). It dynamically adjusts the wait_duration to maintain an optimal balance, constraining it strictly between 0.5x and 2.0x of the configured value according to the batch trend. The problem with the current strategy is that when the wait_duration is configured to a large value, performance regression and latency increase may occur due to the limited range of variation.      
The [new adaptive batching strategy](https://pingcap.feishu.cn/wiki/FgIWwaXoriexbPkzG5uclLiWn2b) dynamically adjusts the wait_duration within a larger range in raftstore write path to optimize the trade-off between write batching efficiency (reducing IOPS) and QPS throughput / request latency.    

As the [Test Report](https://pingcap.feishu.cn/wiki/LgRtw7BkUi501ZkOeLZcHph8nsb) shows, in the local SSD environment (IDC), compared with the default configuration (wait_duration=20us), the new strategy achieves IOPS reduction; compared with the old version with the same configuration, it delivers better trade-off control. In the EBS environment (Cloud), we tested the standard and plus storage types, and the new strategy has almost no IOPS reduction effect. This is because the latency of EBS itself is already relatively high, and the current version can automatically aggregate I/Os. But the new strategy achieves a dual optimization of "increased throughput (QPS) and decreased latency".     

### Related changes

- [ ] PR to update `pingcap/docs`/`pingcap/docs-cn`:
- [ ] Need to cherry-pick to the release branch

### Check List

Tests <!-- At least one of them must be included. -->

- [x] Unit test
- [x] Integration test
- [x] Manual test (add detailed scripts or steps below)
- [ ] No code

Side effects

- [ ] Performance regression: Consumes more CPU
- [ ] Performance regression: Consumes more Memory
- [ ] Breaking backward compatibility

### Release note
<!-- 
Compatibility change, improvement, bugfix, and new feature need a release note.

Please refer to [Release Notes Language Style Guide](https://pingcap.github.io/tidb-dev-guide/contribute-to-tidb/release-notes-style-guide.html) to write a quality release note.

If you don't think this PR needs a release note then fill it with None.
If this PR will be picked to release branch, then a release note is probably required.
-->

```release-note
Raftstore: add an optional adaptive write-batching feature that dynamically tunes raft write wait duration based on observed write QPS and batch-achievement ratio. This improves batching efficiency under high concurrency to reduce IOPS amplification, while backing off quickly when waiting becomes less effective under lighter traffic. The feature is controlled by `raftstore.adaptive_batch_enabled` and is disabled by default, so existing behavior remains unchanged unless explicitly enabled. 
```


<!-- This is an auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->
## Summary by CodeRabbit

* **New Features**
  * Opt-in adaptive batching for write operations (default: off).
  * New config fields to enable adaptive batching and set a high-QPS threshold.
  * Exposed an adaptive-batching gauge and two histograms to observe wait-duration behavior.

* **Improvements**
  * Runtime-tuned batching and wait-duration adjustments based on recent QPS and concurrency.
  * Smoothing, clamping and safeguards to avoid futile or extreme waits; enhanced adaptive logging.

* **Tests**
  * Expanded tests covering adaptive wait-duration logic and boundary conditions.
<!-- end of auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->
author: jiadebin

reviews.body:
## Pull request overview

This pull request implements support for shared locks in the pessimistic transaction model for TiKV. The PR adds shared lock functionality to the acquire_pessimistic_lock, prewrite, and commit schedulers/actions.

**Changes:**
- Adds `SharedLock` variant to `Mutation` enum and related type handling
- Implements shared lock acquisition logic in `acquire_pessimistic_lock` action
- Updates prewrite logic to handle shared lock mutations and merge them with existing shared locks
- Modifies commit logic to handle shared lock entries, removing locks individually while keeping remaining entries
- Adds scheduler support for tracking whether lock requests are for shared locks
- Implements `SharedLocks` type with methods for managing multiple transaction locks on the same key

### Reviewed changes

title: copr: support new global index key value format V2
body:
<!--
Thank you for contributing to TiKV!

If you haven't already, please read TiKV's [CONTRIBUTING](https://github.com/tikv/tikv/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md) document.

If you're unsure about anything, just ask; somebody should be along to answer within a day or two.

PR Title Format:

2. *: what's changed
-->

### What is changed and how it works?
<!--
Please create an issue first to describe the problem.

linking the relevant issues via the "close" or "ref".

For more info, check https://github.com/tikv/tikv/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md#linking-issues.
-->

Issue Number: Ref #19262

<!--
You could use "commit message" code block to add more description to the final commit message.
For more info, check https://github.com/tikv/tikv/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md#format-of-the-commit-message.
-->
What's Changed:
Global Index V1 added the partition id to the key, to make non-unique global indexes on non-clustered partitioned tables handle duplicate _tidb_rowid's between partitions.
V2 removes the partition id from the value part.

V1 and V2 is separated, since V1 can still use the same index read functions, that rely on the partition id in the value, while v2 needs to use the partition id encoded in the key part instead.


```commit-message
copr: Non-unique global indexes for non-clustered tables are now only storing the partition id in the key instead of the value (or both as in V1).
```

### Related changes

- [ ] PR to update `pingcap/docs`/`pingcap/docs-cn`:

reviews.body: ## Pull request overview This PR adds support for Global Index V2 format in TiKV's index scan executor. The key difference between V1 and V2 is that V2 stores the partition ID only in the key (not in the value), reducing storage overhead for non-unique global indexes on non-clustered partitioned tables. **Changes:** - Modified `decode_int_handle_from_key` to handle partition ID prefix in the key and return both handle and partition ID - Updated `process_old_collation_kv` to extract and use partition ID from key when present (V2 format) - Updated `build_operations` (new collation path) to extract partition ID from key for non-unique indexes and prefer key-based partition ID over value-based - Added comprehensive unit and integration tests for V1, V2, edge cases, and backward compatibility --- 💡 Add Copilot custom instructions for smarter, more guided reviews. Learn how to get started.

**Actionable comments posted: 1**
🤖 Fix all issues with AI agents ``` In `@components/tidb_query_executors/src/index_scan_executor.rs`: - Around line 849-862: The error branch is too aggressive because it treats physical_table_id_column_cnt > 0 the same as pid_column_cnt > 0; only pid_column_cnt implies a required partition id. Update the conditional that builds decode_pid_op in index_scan_executor.rs so it returns an error only when self.pid_column_cnt > 0 (not when physical_table_id_column_cnt > 0); keep the DecodePartitionIdOp::Nop path for cases with physical_table_id_column_cnt > 0 so downstream process_kv_general and process_physical_table_id_column(key) can handle extracting the table id. ```
🧹 Nitpick comments (1)
components/tidb_query_executors/src/index_scan_executor.rs (1)
`3988-3999`: **V2 format is only tested through the old-collation path (small values).** V2 values are always `[0u8]` (1 byte), so all V2 iterations go through `process_old_collation_kv`. The `process_kv_general` / `build_operations` path for V2 is never exercised — this is the path where `partition_id_from_key` is extracted manually and decoded via `NumberCodec::decode_i64`. Consider adding a V2 test case with a value > `MAX_OLD_ENCODED_VALUE_LEN` (e.g., including restore data) to cover the `build_operations` → `partition_id_from_key` flow. This would also catch the error condition issue at Line 855.

**Actionable comments posted: 1**
🤖 Fix all issues with AI agents ``` In `@components/tidb_query_executors/src/index_scan_executor.rs`: - Around line 76-82: Fix the typo in the comment referring to the TiDB constant name: replace `EXTRA_PHYSICAL_TAGBLE_ID_COL_ID` with the correct `EXTRA_PHYSICAL_TABLE_ID_COL_ID` in the comment near the index scan executor section (where `table::EXTRA_PHYSICAL_TAGBLE_ID_COL_ID` is mentioned) so the constant name matches the actual symbol `table::EXTRA_PHYSICAL_TABLE_ID_COL_ID`. ```
🧹 Nitpick comments (1)
components/tidb_query_executors/src/index_scan_executor.rs (1)
`4120-4253`: **Good targeted test covering the V2 new-encoding path through `build_operations`.** The value is carefully constructed to exceed `MAX_OLD_ENCODED_VALUE_LEN` and route through `process_kv_general`, with `index_version=1` and a minimal v2 restore-data row. The `0x80` codec-version byte naturally avoids collision with `INDEX_VALUE_PARTITION_ID_FLAG` (0x7E), correctly triggering the `partition_id_from_key` fallback (the V2 path). Consider also testing with `request_pid_column = true` in this test to cover the deprecated `pid_column_cnt > 0` path through `process_kv_general` under V2 new-encoding conditions, though the risk is low since that path is already covered by the old-encoding V2 test.

<details>
<summary>🧹 Nitpick comments (2)</summary><blockquote>

<details>
<summary>components/tidb_query_executors/src/index_scan_executor.rs (2)</summary><blockquote>

`528-537`: **Consider a defensive assertion for the NoDecode + global-index invariant.**

The safety of this path relies on an external invariant: TiDB always sends the handle column for IntHandle global indexes, so NoDecode is never reached for V2 global indexes. If that invariant is ever violated, `process_physical_table_id_column` would silently return the *index* table ID instead of the partition ID — a correctness issue that's hard to diagnose in production.

A `debug_assert` (or a logged warning) would provide an early signal if the invariant breaks during development or rolling upgrades, without impacting release performance.

<details>
<summary>Optional defensive check</summary>

```diff
             NoDecode => {
                 // V2 key-only partition ID only exists for IntHandle
                 // global indexes, when !PKIsHandle, and TiDB always sends
                 // the handle column for those, so this path is only reached
                 // for local indexes where decode_table_id correctly returns
                 // the partition's table ID.
+                debug_assert!(
+                    self.pid_column_cnt == 0,
+                    "NoDecode should not be used with global indexes (pid_column_cnt > 0)"
+                );
                 if self.physical_table_id_column_cnt > 0 {
                     self.process_physical_table_id_column(key, columns)?;
                 }
             }
```
</details>

<details>
<summary>🤖 Prompt for AI Agents</summary>

```
Verify each finding against the current code and only fix it if needed.

- 537, Add a defensive debug assertion when entering the NoDecode branch to
ensure the global-index + IntHandle invariant holds: inside the NoDecode arm
(where

copr: add tests and documentation for global index V1 format

Based on PR#19261, with the help from Claude Code to add tests etc.
Add comprehensive test coverage and documentation for V1 global index
format, where partition ID appears in both KEY and VALUE for non-unique
global indexes on non-clustered tables.

V1 Format:
- Key: [indexed_cols][PARTITION_ID_FLAG][partition_id][handle]
- Value: [tail_len][PARTITION_ID_FLAG][partition_id][...] (unchanged)

This dual-location approach minimizes code changes during migration from
legacy format (partition ID in value only) to future V2 format (partition
ID in key only).

Changes:
- Add documentation to decode_int_handle_from_key() explaining V1 format
  and migration strategy
- Add test_non_unique_global_index_v1_partition_in_key_and_value() to
  validate end-to-end V1 key/value handling
- Add test_v1_edge_cases() to test zero and negative partition IDs
- Add test_v1_backward_compatibility() to ensure old format still works

All tests verify that:
1. Partition ID in key doesn't break handle decoding (key skips it)
2. Partition ID from value is correctly extracted (existing code path)
3. All columns are output correctly including partition ID
4. Zero and negative partition IDs work correctly (i64 full range)
5. Legacy format (no partition ID in key) continues to work

removed from VALUE to optimize storage. This reduces each non-unique
global index entry by 9 bytes (1 flag + 8-byte partition ID).

- Handle decoding after skipping partition ID prefix

copr: fix physical_table_id column for V2 global indexes in old collation path

In process_old_collation_kv, the physical_table_id column was
unconditionally populated using decode_table_id(key), which extracts the
index table ID from the key prefix. For V2 global indexes (non-unique,
non-clustered), the partition ID is only in the key and the value is
minimal ([0x00]), routing through the old collation path. This caused
TiDB to receive the wrong physical table ID and look up rows from the
wrong partition, returning 0 records.

Move physical_table_id column population after handle/partition decoding
so that for global indexes the partition ID is used instead, matching the
behavior already present in process_kv_general (new collation path).

Also extend test_global_index_formats with a physical_table_id column
dimension to verify the fix across all V1/V2 combinations.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
Signed-off-by: Mattias Jonsson <mattias.jonsson@pingcap.com>
Merge remote-tracking branch 'origin/master' into support-new-global-idx-format-V2-19262

Signed-off-by: Mattias Jonsson <mattias.jonsson@pingcap.com>

Removed un-needed code

Signed-off-by: Mattias Jonsson <mattias.jonsson@pingcap.com>

copr: fix pid_column_cnt handling for deprecated EXTRA_PARTITION_ID_COL_ID column

When pid_column_cnt > 0 (deprecated EXTRA_PARTITION_ID_COL_ID requested)
and no partition ID is found in the key or value, the pid column must
still receive a value to avoid column length mismatches.

In process_kv_general, decode_pid_columns with DecodePartitionIdOp::Nop
was a no-op, silently skipping the push. In process_old_collation_kv,
pid_column_cnt was not handled at all.

Fix both paths to fall back to the table ID from the key prefix when
no partition ID is available, matching the existing physical_table_id
fallback pattern.

decode_int_handle_and_partition_from_key() with calls to the existing
split_partition_id() helper.

title: storage: fix wrong commit_ts in Get/TableScan when latest write is Op_Lock (#19374) (#19394)
body:
This is an automated cherry-pick of #19394

<!--
Thank you for contributing to TiKV!

If you haven't already, please read TiKV's [CONTRIBUTING](https://github.com/tikv/tikv/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md) document.

### What is changed and how it works?
<!--
Please create an issue first to describe the problem.

There MUST be one line starting with "Issue Number:  " and 
linking the relevant issues via the "close" or "ref".

For more info, check https://github.com/tikv/tikv/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md#linking-issues.
-->

Issue Number: Close #19393

<!--
You could use "commit message" code block to add more description to the final commit message.
For more info, check https://github.com/tikv/tikv/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md#format-of-the-commit-message.
-->
What's Changed:

```commit-message
storage: fix wrong commit_ts in Get/TableScan when latest write is Op_Lock

Problem Summary:
- When `load_commit_ts=true` and a key has newer committed `Op_Lock` versions on top of the visible `Op_Put`, TiKV may return the lock commit_ts instead of the put commit_ts.

What changed:
- Fix `PointGetter` to return the commit_ts of the actual write version loaded by `last_change` shortcut, instead of the current cursor version.
- Fix backward scanner to only update `loaded_commit_ts` when selecting a `Put` version, avoiding ts overwrite from `Lock/Rollback` versions.

Tests:
- Add `test_point_get_load_commit_ts_with_top_lock_versions` in `point_getter.rs`.
- Add `test_load_commit_ts_with_top_lock_versions` in `scanner/backward.rs`.
- Verify existing related tests:
  - `test_point_get_load_commit_ts`
  - `test_scan_with_load_commit_ts`
```

### Related changes

- [ ] PR to update `pingcap/docs`/`pingcap/docs-cn`:
- [ ] Need to cherry-pick to the release branch

### Check List

Tests <!-- At least one of them must be included. -->

Side effects

reviews.body: ## Pull request overview Fixes incorrect `_tidb_commit_ts` being returned by point-get and scans when `load_commit_ts` is enabled and newer committed `Op_Lock` writes exist above the latest visible `Op_Put` (per Close #19393 / ref #19374). **Changes:** - `PointGetter`: track and return the commit_ts of the actual `Write` loaded via the `last_change` shortcut, instead of decoding ts from the cursor’s current (newer) position. - Backward scanner: only set `loaded_commit_ts` when a `Put` version is selected (avoids overwriting with `Lock/Rollback` commit_ts). - Adds targeted regression tests covering top-lock stacks across seek-bound thresholds and delete visibility. ### Reviewed changes Copilot reviewed 3 out of 3 changed files in this pull request and generated no comments. | File | Description | | ---- | ----------- | | `src/storage/mvcc/reader/scanner/mod.rs` | Adds a forward-scan regression test validating `load_commit_ts` correctness with many top `LOCK` writes (both `< SEEK_BOUND` and `>= SEEK_BOUND`) and deleted keys. | | `src/storage/mvcc/reader/scanner/backward.rs` | Fixes reverse-scan `loaded_commit_ts` bookkeeping so commit_ts comes from the returned `PUT`, plus adds a regression test. | | `src/storage/mvcc/reader/point_getter.rs` | Fixes point-get commit_ts derivation when `last_change` triggers a `get_cf` jump, plus adds regression tests for both `<`/`>= SEEK_BOUND` and delete cases. | --- 💡 Add Copilot custom instructions for smarter, more guided reviews. Learn how to get started.

<details>
<summary>🧹 Nitpick comments (1)</summary><blockquote>

<details>
<summary>src/storage/mvcc/reader/scanner/backward.rs (1)</summary><blockquote>

`383-391`: **The seek-loop fix is correct, but the new test does not exercise this code path.**

In `test_load_commit_ts_with_top_lock_versions`, the PUT@ts=1 is the *earliest* entry for key "k". The `prev()` loop starts there (i=0), captures `loaded_commit_ts = Some(1)`, and subsequent iterations only see LOCKs. After the loop, the seek path re-scans ts=REVERSE_SEEK_BOUND+2 down to `last_checked_commit_ts` and terminates with `handle_last_version`, which already carries the correct `loaded_commit_ts` from the `prev()` loop. **Lines 383–386 are never reached** in that test.

The specific scenario these lines guard against is when the `prev()` loop exhausts all `REVERSE_SEEK_BOUND` iterations without finding a PUT (e.g., all encountered versions are LOCKs), and then the seek loop finds a PUT in the range `(last_checked_commit_ts, scan_ts]`. A concrete setup:

```rust
// PUT above last_checked_commit_ts so seek loop hits it directly
must_prewrite_put(&mut engine, b"k", b"v", b"k", 1);
must_commit(&mut engine, b"k", 1, 1);
// enough LOCKs so prev() loop is exhausted without finding the PUT
for ts in 2..=REVERSE_SEEK_BOUND + 2 {
    must_prewrite_lock(&mut engine, b"k", b"k", ts);
    must_commit(&mut engine, b"k", ts, ts);
}
// a second PUT above everything, visible, so seek loop finds it first
must_prewrite_put(&mut engine, b"k", b"v2", b"k", REVERSE_SEEK_BOUND + 3);
must_commit(&mut engine, b"k", REVERSE_SEEK_BOUND + 3, REVERSE_SEEK_BOUND + 3);
// scan at REVERSE_SEEK_BOUND + 5 so the PUT@REVERSE_SEEK_BOUND+3 is between
// last_checked_commit_ts and scan_ts
```

Consider adding a test case that forces this code path.


Would you like me to draft the missing test case for the seek-loop PUT path?

<details>
<summary>🤖 Prompt for AI Agents</summary>

```
Verify each finding against the current code and only fix it i

<!--

For more info, check https://github.com/tikv/tikv/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md#format-of-the-commit-message.
-->
What's Changed:

```commit-message
Add network/io info collection for TopSQL:
1. Introduce resource-metering.enable-network-io-collection config to control whether enable this new feature. Default is disabled.
2. Collect network_in, network_out, logical_read, logical_write execution info and recorded in TopSQL:
    i. Since the LocalStorage of TopSQL recorder is TLS, and only be accessed inside the thread with attached tag. Here, we use GLOBAL_TRACKERS to help record network_in data size.
    ii. For network_out, we can only directly get resp's size for Coprocessor request. Thus we need to collect this data one by one for all requests. Since we only care about requests that potentially generate large response, we bypass some "write requests" whose response only contained "commit_ts" data.
    iii. Use the processed_size(https://github.com/tikv/tikv/blob/1deb3a135dc41c3ca227e3d5a29712526b492a4c/components/tikv_kv/src/stats.rs#L195) as logical read size
    iv. Use the scheduled tasks' write_bytes() as logical write size
```

title: *: Update rustc to update time crate
body:
<!--
Thank you for contributing to TiKV!

If you haven't already, please read TiKV's [CONTRIBUTING](https://github.com/tikv/tikv/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md) document.

If you're unsure about anything, just ask; somebody should be along to answer within a day or two.

PR Title Format:
1. module [, module2, module3]: what's changed
2. *: what's changed
-->

### What is changed and how it works?
<!--
Please create an issue first to describe the problem.

There MUST be one line starting with "Issue Number:  " and 
linking the relevant issues via the "close" or "ref".

For more info, check https://github.com/tikv/tikv/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md#linking-issues.
-->

Issue Number: Close #19355

<!--
You could use "commit message" code block to add more description to the final commit message.
For more info, check https://github.com/tikv/tikv/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md#format-of-the-commit-message.
-->
What's Changed:

```commit-message
Update time crate to fix RUSTSEC-2026-0009.
```


Updating time crate requires:

1.  Update rustc nightly version
  1.1. Requires removing let-chains usage unless edition is set to 2024, `feature(let_chains)` does not work
  1.2. It is more "readable" removing all `let_chains` rather than bump Rust edition to 2024, I recommend we upgrade Rust edition to 2024 separately, and upgrade each module individually. created https://github.com/tikv/tikv/issues/19368 for tracking
2. Update raft-engine serde version, see the comment  here https://github.com/tikv/tikv/issues/19355#issuecomment-3874163894
  2.1 The raft-engine PR is https://github.com/tikv/raft-engine/pull/390

### Can we separate this into multiple smaller PRs:
Yes and No. TiKV CI is failing, any PR requires fixing RUSTSEC, fixing RUSTSEC requires update nightly version, and here we are again...

### Files need attentions:
```
components/compact-log-backup/src/execute/mod.rs
components/raftstore/src/coprocessor/config.rs

reviews.body: ## Pull request overview Adds explicit `Content-Length` headers to S3 multipart-upload related requests in TiKV’s AWS cloud storage implementation to improve compatibility with GCP’s S3-compatible service (which can return HTTP 411 when `Content-Length` is missing). **Changes:** - Customize `create_multipart_upload`, `complete_multipart_upload`, and `abort_multipart_upload` requests to add `Content-Length`. - Update multipart upload tests to include `Content-Length` expectations. - Add a new test covering abort behavior on multipart upload failure. --- 💡 Add Copilot custom instructions for smarter, more guided reviews. Learn how to get started.

**Actionable comments posted: 3**

<details>
<summary>🤖 Fix all issues with AI agents</summary>

```
In `@components/cloud/aws/src/s3.rs`:
- Around line 1069-1089: The mocked abort multipart upload response currently
uses a CompleteMultipartUploadResult XML which is semantically incorrect; locate
the ReplayEvent::new that builds the Request with query
x-id=AbortMultipartUpload (the URI containing
"x-id=AbortMultipartUpload&uploadId=1") and replace the response body in its
http::Response::builder() call to be an empty body (or a minimal empty XML) to
accurately represent AbortMultipartUpload semantics instead of the
CompleteMultipartUploadResult payload.
- Around line 1018-1019: The inline comment above the multipart test referencing
"split magic_contents into 3 parts, so we mock 5 requests here(1 begin + 3 part
+ 1 complete)" is a copy-paste artifact and should be corrected to reflect the
abort path exercised by the test: update the comment to mention "1 abort" (or "1
abort request") instead of "1 complete" and optionally note that the mocked
requests are 1 begin + 3 part + 1 abort; locate the comment near the
magic_contents split in the multipart upload test in s3.rs and change the
wording accordingly.
- Around line 1107-1114: The histogram assertion reads a process-global metric
CLOUD_REQUEST_HISTOGRAM_VEC via
get_metric_with_label_values(...).get_sample_count() and compares to an absolute
value, which is flaky because other tests (e.g., test_s3_storage_multi_part)
also observe "upload_part"; change the test to snapshot the prior count by
calling get_sample_count() before the operation, run the upload/abort steps,
then re-read get_sample_count() and assert the difference (new - old) equals the
expected number of observations ((magic_contents.len() / multi_part_size) as
u64); apply the same delta-assertion pattern to test_s3_storage_multi_part as
well so both tests compare deltas instead of absolute counts.
```

</details>

<details>
<summary>🧹 Nitpick comments (2)

title: pd: report hot read cpu in heartbeat
body:
<!--
Thank you for contributing to TiKV!

If you haven't already, please read TiKV's [CONTRIBUTING](https://github.com/tikv/tikv/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md) document.

If you're unsure about anything, just ask; somebody should be along to answer within a day or two.

PR Title Format:
1. module [, module2, module3]: what's changed
2. *: what's changed
-->

### What is changed and how it works?
<!--
Please create an issue first to describe the problem.

There MUST be one line starting with "Issue Number:  " and 
linking the relevant issues via the "close" or "ref".

For more info, check https://github.com/tikv/tikv/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md#linking-issues.
-->

Issue Number: Close https://github.com/tikv/tikv/issues/19373

<!--
You could use "commit message" code block to add more description to the final commit message.
For more info, check https://github.com/tikv/tikv/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md#format-of-the-commit-message.
-->
What's Changed:

```commit-message
 Improve hotspot read accounting by reporting per-region unified-read CPU in `cpu_stats`, preventing CPU stat loss across region/store heartbeat paths, and fixing Linux thread-name truncation
  matching for read CPU attribution.
- Add `cpu_stats` propagation on the Region heartbeat reporting path (`RegionStat` -> PD request).
  - Keep `cpu_usage` as a compatibility field (deprecated in kvproto) for rolling upgrades.
  - Split region CPU accumulators into two maps:
    - `since_region_heartbeat`
    - `since_store_heartbeat`
    so one heartbeat path no longer drains CPU data required by the other.
  - Include unified-read CPU in Store heartbeat peer_stats, and add CPU into hotspot filtering and TopN selection.
    TopN dimensions are expanded from 3 to 4 (keys/bytes/query/cpu).
  - Do not consume peer deltas / CPU records on fake store heartbeats, preserving full intervals for the next real heartbeat.
  - Clean up both CPU maps when peers are destroyed, and clear orphan CPU records.
  - Add prefix matching utility for Linux 15-char thread-name truncation to fix read CPU attribution for long prefixes (for example `unified-read-pool`).
  - Add unit tests for CPU thresholding, TopN collection, zero-interval handling, map cleanup, and thread-name prefix matching.
  - Update kvproto revision (with cpu_stats-related fields), and add a temporary denylist entry for `RUSTSEC-2026-0009` in `deny.toml` due to current `time` upgrade constraints with raft-engine/serde
  pinning.
```

### Related changes



### Check List

Tests <!-- At least one of them must be included. -->

- [x] Unit test
- [ ] Integration test


### Release note
<!-- 
Compatibility change, improvement, bugfix, and new feature need a release note.

Please refer to [Release Notes Language Style Guide](https://pingcap.github.io/tidb-dev-guide/contribute-to-tidb/release-notes-style-guide.html) to write a quality release note.

If you don't think this PR needs a release note then fill it with None.
If this PR will be picked to release branch, then a release note is probably required.
-->

```release-note
None
```


<!-- This is an auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->
## Summary by CodeRabbit

* **New Features**
  * CPU statistics are now attached to region and store heartbeats, used for hotspot detection, Top-N CPU reporting, and gated reporting.

* **Chores**
  * Dependency for kvproto pinned to a specific revision for reproducible builds.
  * Improved thread-name prefix matching for CPU aggregation.
  * Per-region CPU accounting split to support distinct heartbeat/reporting flows.

* **Tests**
  * Unit tests added for per-region state removal, CPU-threshold reporting, and thread-name prefix behavior.
<!-- end of auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->
author: lhy1024

reviews.body:
## Pull request overview

This PR extends TiKV’s hotspot reporting by including unified read CPU usage in PD heartbeats, and improves internal CPU/thread accounting by matching thread-name prefixes correctly under Linux’s thread-name truncation.

**Changes:**
- Add `CpuStats` reporting (unified read CPU) into region/store heartbeat peer stats and PD client requests.
- Track CPU time separately for region-heartbeat vs store-heartbeat reporting intervals and include CPU in TopN peer reporting.
- Introduce `matches_thread_name_prefix` to correctly match TiKV thread-name prefixes under Linux truncation, and switch existing CPU/thread aggregation to use it.

### Reviewed changes

Copilot reviewed 10 out of 11 changed files in this pull request and generated 3 comments.

<details>
<summary>Show a summary per file</summary>

| File | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |
| `src/read_pool.rs` | Use prefix-based thread-name matching when summing unified read pool per-thread CPU time. |
| `components/tikv_util/src/thread_name_prefix.rs` | Add `matches_thread_name_prefix` helper (with tests) to handle Linux thread-name truncation safely. |
| `components/raftstore/src/store/worker/split_controller.rs` | Switch thread CPU aggregation from substring matching to prefix matching. |
| `components/raftstore/src/store/worker/pd.rs` | Add store-heartbeat peer CPU reporting (unified read) + TopN by CPU + cleanup helpers and tests. |
| `components/raftstore-v2/src/worker/pd/store.rs` | Add store-heartbeat peer CPU reporting (unified read) + TopN by CPU. |
| `components/raftstore-v2/src/worker/pd/region.rs` | Attach `CpuStats` to region heartbeat + add cleanup helper/test. |
| `components/raftstore-v2/src/worker/pd/mod.rs` | Add store-heartbeat CPU record map to Runner state. |
| `components/pd_client/src/lib.rs` | Extend `RegionStat` with `cpu_stats: pdpb::CpuStats`. |
| `components/pd_client/src/client.rs` | Populate `RegionHeartbeatRequest.cpu_stats` from `RegionStat`. |
| `C

**Actionable comments posted: 2**
🤖 Fix all issues with AI agents ``` In `@Cargo.toml`: - Around line 223-224: Remove the temporary kvproto patch in Cargo.toml that points to the personal fork: delete the [patch.'https://github.com/pingcap/kvproto'] section (the kvproto = { git = "https://github.com/lhy1024/kvproto", rev = "b27f7093..." } entry) and either restore the upstream kvproto dependency or leave the default upstream source; if you still need the forked changes, replace the patch with the official kvproto reference once the upstream PR is merged. In `@components/raftstore-v2/src/worker/pd/store.rs`: - Around line 219-226: The cpu usage branch leaves stale entries in self.region_cpu_records_store when interval_seconds == 0; change the logic in the block around cpu_usage calculation (referencing region_cpu_records_store, region_id, interval_seconds, and cpu_usage) to always call self.region_cpu_records_store.remove(region_id) to consume/drain the record even when interval_seconds is zero, then compute cpu_usage (or set it to 0) using the removed value; ensure remove is invoked unconditionally before returning cpu_usage so entries don't accumulate. ```

`478-500`: **Consider adding a negative test case.** The test covers the case where the region exists in all maps. A test for a non-existent `region_id` (verifying it returns `false` and doesn't panic) would improve coverage, though the function is simple enough that this is optional.

🤖 Fix all issues with AI agents ``` In `@components/raftstore/src/store/worker/pd.rs`: - Around line 947-994: The function collect_report_peers_for_store_heartbeat currently removes CPU records for region_ids that exist in region_peers but leaves entries in region_cpu_records_since_store_heartbeat for region_ids not present in region_peers, causing a leak; after the for loop in collect_report_peers_for_store_heartbeat, drain/clear the remaining entries in region_cpu_records_since_store_heartbeat (e.g., iterate and remove any leftover keys or call clear()) so orphaned CPU records are removed even when a region is missing from region_peers. ```

components/tikv_util/src/thread_name_prefix.rs (1)
`158-179`: **Well-implemented utility with a clear rationale.** The function correctly handles the Linux 15-byte `comm` truncation and the ASCII-only invariant is well-documented. One minor robustness consideration: If this function is ever called with a non-ASCII `prefix`, the byte slice at line 178 (`&prefix[..LINUX_THREAD_NAME_MAX_LEN]`) will panic at a non–char-boundary. Since all current prefixes in this file are ASCII, this is safe today. Consider adding a debug assertion to guard against future misuse:

components/raftstore/src/store/worker/pd.rs (2)

title: tici: support versioned lookup via per-key read ts (range_versions)
body:
<!--
Thank you for contributing to TiKV!

If you haven't already, please read TiKV's [CONTRIBUTING](https://github.com/tikv/tikv/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md) document.

If you're unsure about anything, just ask; somebody should be along to answer within a day or two.

PR Title Format:
1. module [, module2, module3]: what's changed
2. *: what's changed
-->

### What is changed and how it works?
<!--
Please create an issue first to describe the problem.

There MUST be one line starting with "Issue Number:  " and 
linking the relevant issues via the "close" or "ref".

For more info, check https://github.com/tikv/tikv/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md#linking-issues.
-->

Issue Number: Close https://github.com/tikv/tikv/issues/19357

<!--
You could use "commit message" code block to add more description to the final commit message.
For more info, check https://github.com/tikv/tikv/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md#format-of-the-commit-message.
-->
What's Changed:

```commit-message
coprocessor: support versioned lookup via per-key read ts (range_versions)

  This introduces a special "versioned lookup" read path for coprocessor DAG requests:

  - When `coppb::Request.range_versions` is empty, behavior is unchanged.
  - When `range_versions` is provided:
    - `ranges.len()` must equal `range_versions.len()`
    - all `ranges` must be point ranges
    - only DAG + TableScan is allowed
    - each `ranges[i]` is read at `range_versions[i]` (per-key read ts), while bypassing txn-related checks:
      - endpoint memory lock checking / max_ts update
      - lock-cf checking in PointGetter
      - RcCheckTs newer-ts conflicts

  Implementation overview:
  - Plumb `range_versions` through Endpoint -> DagHandlerBuilder -> TableScan -> RangesScanner.
  - Add Store/SnapshotStore `get_entry_at_ts` backed by PointGetter with `bypass_txn_check`.
  - Disable coprocessor cache for versioned lookup to avoid unintended cache hits.

  Tests:
  - Add unit tests for validation/guard rails and bypass behaviors.
  - Add executor tests to verify per-range ts usage (including backward scan alignment).
  - Extend coprocessor integration tests for reading older versions.

  Dependencies:
  - Requires kvproto change adding `coppb::Request.range_versions` (kvproto PR: pingcap/kvproto#1356).
    The temporary `[patch]` in `Cargo.toml` can be removed after kvproto is merged.
```

### Related changes

- [ ] PR to update `pingcap/docs`/`pingcap/docs-cn`:
- [ ] Need to cherry-pick to the release branch

### Check List

Tests <!-- At least one of them must be included. -->

- [ ] Unit test
- [ ] Integration test
- [ ] Manual test (add detailed scripts or steps below)
- [ ] No code

Side effects

- [ ] Performance regression: Consumes more CPU
- [ ] Performance regression: Consumes more Memory
- [ ] Breaking backward compatibility

### Release note
<!-- 
Compatibility change, improvement, bugfix, and new feature need a release note.

Please refer to [Release Notes Language Style Guide](https://pingcap.github.io/tidb-dev-guide/contribute-to-tidb/release-notes-style-guide.html) to write a quality release note.

If you don't think this PR needs a release note then fill it with None.
If this PR will be picked to release branch, then a release note is probably required.
-->

```release-note
coprocessor: support versioned lookup via per-key read ts (range_versions) used by tici
```
author: wshwsh12

reviews.body:
## Pull request overview

This PR introduces a “versioned lookup” coprocessor read path that allows supplying per-key (per-point-range) read timestamps via `versioned_ranges`, exposed through a dedicated `VersionedKv` RPC and plumbed through DAG/TableScan down to point-get MVCC reads with txn checks bypassed.

**Changes:**
- Add `VersionedKv.versioned_coprocessor` RPC and reject `versioned_ranges` on the existing coprocessor RPCs.
- Plumb per-range read timestamps (`range_versions`) through Endpoint → DAG runner → TableScan/ScanExecutor → RangesScanner → Storage, and add storage APIs to fetch a key at an explicit ts while bypassing txn checks.
- Add/extend unit and integration tests covering validation/guardrails and per-range ts behavior.

### Reviewed changes

Copilot reviewed 22 out of 23 changed files in this pull request and generated 1 comment.

<details>
<summary>Show a summary per file</summary>

| File | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |
| tests/integrations/coprocessor/test_select.rs | Adds integration test verifying reading an older version via `versioned_ranges`. |
| tests/integrations/coprocessor/test_checksum.rs | Wires new `range_versions` option for checksum path (set to `None`). |
| src/storage/txn/store.rs | Adds `Store::get_entry_at_ts` and implements it for `SnapshotStore` (plus internal helper). |
| src/storage/mvcc/reader/point_getter.rs | Adds `is_versioned_lookup` flag to bypass lock + RcCheckTs newer-ts checks; adds tests. |
| src/server/service/kv.rs | Introduces `VersionedKv` RPC handler; rejects `versioned_ranges` on existing coprocessor RPCs. |
| src/server/server.rs | Registers the new VersionedKv gRPC service. |
| src/coprocessor/statistics/analyze_context.rs | Passes `range_versions: None` to scanner options. |
| src/coprocessor/statistics/analyze.rs | Extends table scan builder calls with `range_versions: None`. |
| src/coprocessor/endpoint.rs | Parses `versioned_ranges`, validates invariants, disables cache, and skips m

txn: support shared lock for pessimistic / prewrite / commit schedulers (#19282)

ref tikv/tikv#19087

This PR implement the acquire_pessimistic_lock / prewrite / commit schedulers for shared lock.

Signed-off-by: you06 <you1474600@gmail.com>

coprocessor: fix panic when _tidb_commit_ts column is placed before PK handle columns (#19300)

close tikv/tikv#19299

fix panic when _tidb_commit_ts column is placed before PK handle columns

Signed-off-by: Chao Wang <cclcwangchao@hotmail.com>

*: support `need_commit_ts` for TiKV command batch_get_command (#19301)

close tikv/tikv#19102

support `need_commit_ts` for TiKV command batch_get_command

Signed-off-by: Chao Wang <cclcwangchao@hotmail.com>

txn: add shared lock handling for all schedulers (#19304)

ref tikv/tikv#19087

Add shared lock handling for all schedulers:

- Implement rollback, cleanup, and lock resolving for shared locks
- Add rollback_shared_lock function for rolling back individual locks within SharedLocks
- Support shared locks in check_txn_status, pessimistic_rollback, and resolve_lock commands
- Extend flashback to handle shared locks during version rollback
- Update scan_lock to properly filter and return shared locks
- Add filter_shared_locks method to SharedLocks for selective lock removal

Signed-off-by: you06 <you1474600@gmail.com>

cdc: remove assertion on the seek_for_valid_write (#19291)

ref tikv/tikv#11187

Use warn instead of assertion on the seek_for_valid_write.
https://github.com/tikv/tikv/pull/16252 should fix the panic by specifying ranges, but it only fixes versions above v8.5.

Signed-off-by: nhsmw <nhsmwk@gmail.com>

txn: avoid xlock starvation by setting slock shrink-only (#19313)

ref tikv/tikv#19087

Avoid exclusive lock starvation by setting shared locks shrink-only:

- `acquire_pessimistic_lock` returns `NotInShrinkMode` when an exclusive lock meets non-shrink-only shared locks.
- `AcquirePessimisticLock.process_write` converts shared locks to shrink-only, persists them, and returns `KeyIsLocked` to the client.
- The scheduler skips in-memory pessimistic lock writes when a shared-lock update is being persisted.

Signed-off-by: you06 <you1474600@gmail.com>
Signed-off-by: zyguan <zhongyangguan@gmail.com>

Co-authored-by: you06 <you1474600@gmail.com>

resolved_ts: fix OOM due to rts tasks pile up on channel (#19294)

fixes tikv/tikv#18359, close tikv/tikv#18359

This pull request fixes #18359 by introducing improvements to memory
quota management and region re-registration in the resolved-ts
component. The changes ensure that when memory usage exceeds the
configured quota, all observed regions are re-registered to free up
memory, and random backoff is applied to avoid thundering herd problems.
Additionally, the observer and endpoint now share a memory quota
instance, and active checks for memory quota are performed periodically.

Signed-off-by: Neil Shen <overvenus@gmail.com>

tracker: enrich write RPC scan_detail (#19325)

close tikv/tikv#18871

endpoint: implement async snapshot retrieval with timeout handling (#19289)

ref tikv/tikv#18491, ref tikv/tikv#19201

endpoint: implement async snapshot retrieval with timeout handling
  - Added  method to retrieve snapshots with a deadline check.
  - Introduced  utility function to handle future timeouts.
  - Updated existing snapshot retrieval calls to use the new timeout-aware method.
  - Added tests for timeout scenarios and successful snapshot retrieval.

author: zyguan

reviews.body:
## Pull request overview

This pull request adds support for shared lock handling in deadlock detection as part of the broader effort to support shared locks for foreign key constraints (issue #19087). The changes enable the deadlock detector to properly track and detect cycles when transactions wait on shared locks, which can have multiple concurrent owners.

**Changes:**
- Extended the `LockInfoExt` trait to provide an iterator over individual lock owners in a shared lock
- Updated deadlock detection logic to register and check wait-for edges for each owner of a shared lock
- Added cleanup logic to remove all wait-for entries when deadlock is detected on a shared lock
- Added comprehensive integration tests for shared lock deadlock scenarios

### Reviewed changes

Copilot reviewed 6 out of 6 changed files in this pull request and generated 1 comment.

<details>
<summary>Show a summary per file</summary>

| File | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |
| components/tikv_util/src/lib.rs | Adds Iterator trait implementation for Either type to support iterating over lock owners |
| components/txn_types/src/lock.rs | Extends LockInfoExt trait with iter_locks() method to iterate over shared lock owners or yield single lock |
| src/server/lock_manager/mod.rs | Removes restriction preventing deadlock detection for shared locks since they must be shrink-only |
| src/server/lock_manager/deadlock.rs | Updates detection and cleanup logic to handle multiple lock owners by iterating through them |
| src/server/lock_manager/waiter_manager.rs | Adds logic to clean up all wait-for entries when deadlock detected on shared lock, handles shared lock shrink updates |
| tests/integrations/server/lock_manager.rs | Adds three comprehensive integration tests covering basic deadlock, partial edge cleanup, and update waiter scenarios |
</details>






---

💡 <a href="/tikv/tikv/new/master/.github/instructions?filename=*.instructions.md" class="Link--inTextBlock" target="_blank" rel="n

**Actionable comments posted: 1**

<details>
<summary>🤖 Fix all issues with AI agents</summary>

```
In `@tests/integrations/server/lock_manager.rs`:
- Around line 71-90: The function kv_shared_pessimistic_lock dereferences
keys[0] without checking emptiness; add an explicit precondition at the top of
kv_shared_pessimistic_lock (e.g., assert!(!keys.is_empty(), "keys must not be
empty") or debug_assert!) to guard against an empty keys Vec and avoid a panic,
then proceed to use primary = keys[0].clone() as before; this change targets the
kv_shared_pessimistic_lock function and the keys[0] usage.
```

</details>

<details>
<summary>🧹 Nitpick comments (3)</summary><blockquote>

<details>
<summary>tests/integrations/server/lock_manager.rs (3)</summary><blockquote>

`486-489`: **Prefer a `recv_timeout` assertion over a fixed sleep.**

Line 488 uses a sleep to “ensure waiting.” This can be racy on slow CI. Using a timeout assert makes the test deterministic.

<details>
<summary>Proposed fix</summary>

```diff
     let rx_txn10_k2 = async_pessimistic_lock_resumable(client.clone(), ctx.clone(), key2, 10);
-    thread::sleep(Duration::from_millis(100));
+    assert_eq!(
+        rx_txn10_k2
+            .recv_timeout(Duration::from_millis(100))
+            .unwrap_err(),
+        RecvTimeoutError::Timeout
+    );
```
</details>

---

`523-579`: **Use a shared, longer timeout to reduce flakiness.**

Several 100ms timeouts in this test are tight. A shared `wait` duration (e.g., 500ms) makes failures less likely on loaded CI.

<details>
<summary>Proposed fix</summary>

```diff
     let (client, ctx) = build_leader_client(&mut cluster, key_a);
     let client = Arc::new(client);
+    let wait = Duration::from_millis(500);
@@
-    assert_eq!(
-        rx_txn20_kb
-            .recv_timeout(Duration::from_millis(100))
-            .unwrap_err(),
-        RecvTimeoutError::Timeout
-    );
+    assert_eq!(
+        rx_txn20_kb.recv_timeout(wait).unwrap_err(),
+        RecvTimeoutErr

body:

<!--
You could use "commit message" code block to add more description to the final commit message.
For more info, check https://github.com/tikv/tikv/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md#format-of-the-commit-message.
-->
What's Changed:

```commit-message
Add network/io info collection for TopSQL:
1. Introduce resource-metering.enable-network-io-collection config to control whether enable this new feature. Default is disabled.
2. Collect network_in, network_out, logical_read, logical_write execution info and recorded in TopSQL:
    i. Since the LocalStorage of TopSQL recorder is TLS, and only be accessed inside the thread with attached tag. Here, we use GLOBAL_TRACKERS to help record network_in data size.
    ii. For network_out, we can only directly get resp's size for Coprocessor request. Thus we need to collect this data one by one for all requests. Since we only care about requests that potentially generate large response, we bypass some "write requests" whose response only contained "commit_ts" data.
    iii. Use the processed_size(https://github.com/tikv/tikv/blob/1deb3a135dc41c3ca227e3d5a29712526b492a4c/components/tikv_kv/src/stats.rs#L195) as logical read size
    iv. Use the scheduled tasks' write_bytes() as logical write size
```

title: metrics: optimize perf_context metric in Grafana dashboard
body:
### What is changed and how it works?

Issue Number: ref #15990

This PR reorders and hides the `store_perf_context` metric in the "Perf Context duration" panel.

**Problem:**
The `tikv_raftstore_store_perf_context_time_duration_secs` metric was placed above `tikv_raftstore_apply_perf_context_time_duration_secs`. Since TiKV defaults to raft-engine (`raft_engine.enable = true`), this metric produces no data. Users often edit the first expression expecting to see changes, but nothing happens because they're editing the wrong (empty) metric.

**Solution:**
- Move `store_perf_context` below `apply_perf_context`
- Hide `store_perf_context` by default (still available for users using RocksDB)

### Related changes

- [ ] PR to update `pingcap/docs`/`pingcap/docs-cn`:
- [ ] Need to cherry-pick to the release branch

- [x] No code

Side effects

- [ ] Performance regression: Consumes more Memory
- [ ] Breaking backward compatibility

None
```
author: hhwyt

commits.message:
metrics: hide store_perf_context metric in Grafana dashboard

TiKV defaults to raft-engine for storing raft logs since raft_engine.enable
is true by default. The tikv_raftstore_store_perf_context_time_duration_secs
metric only reports data when using RocksDB as the raft log storage engine.

Since most deployments use raft-engine, this metric produces no data. Hide
it by default in the Perf Context duration panel while keeping it available
for users who still use RocksDB.

Signed-off-by: Wei Yang <weiyang@pingcap.com>
Signed-off-by: hhwyt <hhwyt1@gmail.com>

title: tracker: enrich write RPC scan_detail
body:
### What is changed and how it works?

Issue Number: close #18871

What's Changed:
- Propagate MVCC scan statistics collected in txn scheduler into the per-request tracker for txn write RPCs (prewrite/commit/pessimistic lock/...) and merge them into `ExecDetailsV2.scan_detail_v2`.
- No kvproto/protobuf changes; existing perf fields in scan_detail (snapshot time, RocksDB block/cache counters) are preserved.

Slow log output comparison (extracted from `tidb-slow.log`):

```
baseline:
  slowest_prewrite.scan_detail: get_snapshot_time: 5.45us, rocksdb: {block: {}}

patched:
  slowest_prewrite.scan_detail: total_keys: 126, get_snapshot_time: 18.5us, rocksdb: {block: {}}
```

Why `processed_versions` / `processed_versions_size` are often 0 for write RPCs:
- `ScanDetailV2.processed_versions` maps to `Statistics.write.processed_keys` ("user-visible keys"), and `processed_versions_size` maps to `Statistics.processed_size`.
- Txn write commands mainly read write CF for conflict/lock checking, which increments op counters (`get/seek/next` -> `total_versions/total_keys`) but usually doesn't count as "processed keys" nor "processed size", so only `total_versions/total_keys` is expected to be non-zero in most SQL workloads.

### Related changes

- [ ] PR to update `pingcap/docs`/`pingcap/docs-cn`:
- [ ] Need to cherry-pick to the release branch

### Check List

Tests <!-- At least one of them must be included. -->

- [x] Unit test
- [x] Integration test
- [x] Manual test (add detailed scripts or steps below)
- [ ] No code

Manual test:
- Compares baseline vs patched slow log scan_detail.

Side effects

- [ ] Performance regression: Consumes more CPU
- [ ] Performance regression: Consumes more Memory
- [ ] Breaking backward compatibility

### Release note

```release-note
Enhance write-path transaction RPC scan_detail in slow logs by reporting MVCC scan statistics (e.g. total_keys).
```

reviews.body:
## Pull request overview

This pull request adds comprehensive shared lock handling across all TiKV schedulers and transaction commands. Shared locks enable multiple transactions to hold pessimistic locks on the same key concurrently, improving concurrency for read-modify-write workloads.

**Changes:**
- Implements rollback, cleanup, and lock resolving for shared locks across all transaction commands
- Adds `rollback_shared_lock` function for rolling back individual locks within SharedLocks
- Updates scan_lock to properly filter and return shared locks with `filter_shared_locks` method

### Reviewed changes

Copilot reviewed 22 out of 22 changed files in this pull request and generated 4 comments.

<details>
<summary>Show a summary per file</summary>

| File | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |
| tests/failpoints/cases/test_storage.rs | Adds integration tests for shared lock resolution and scan_lock filtering by max_ts |

title: load based compaction
body:
This change implements the load based compaction.  Changes
1. Added the code to monitor mvcc on a region
2. Use mvcc reads to calculate compaction score 

<!--
Thank you for contributing to TiKV!

You could use "commit message" code block to add more description to the final commit message.
For more info, check https://github.com/tikv/tikv/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md#format-of-the-commit-message.
-->
What's Changed:

```commit-message

```

### Related changes

- [ ] PR to update `pingcap/docs`/`pingcap/docs-cn`:
- [ ] Need to cherry-pick to the release branch

### Check List

Tests <!-- At least one of them must be included. -->

- [X] Unit test
- [X] Integration test
- [ ] Manual test (add detailed scripts or steps below)
- [ ] No code
unit test steps
1. set this config
```
mvcc-read-aware-enabled = true
mvcc-scan-threshold = 1
mvcc-read-weight = 1000.0
redundant-rows-threshold = 1000000
redundant-rows-percent-threshold = 100
```
2. run sysbench with one hot key write
`sysbench oltp_read_only --mysql-ignore-errors=1213,1020,1205,1105 --point_selects=0 --range_selects=true --range_size=2 --simple_ranges=1 --sum_ranges=0 --order_ranges=0 --distinct_ranges=0 --distinct_ranges=0 --skip_trx=true --tables=1 --table_size=1 --threads=64 --mysql-host=127.0.0.1 --mysql-user=root --mysql-db=test --mysql-port=4000 --report-interval=10 --time=3000 run
`
3. run sysbench with one hot key read
`sysbench oltp_update_non_index --non_index_updates=1 --skip_trx=true --tables=1 --table_size=1 --threads=64 --mysql-host=127.0.0.1 --mysql-user=root --mysql-db=test --mysql-port=4000 --report-interval=10 --time=3000 run`

4. compaction should be triggered by mvcc read scans since rows threshold is set so high. seen these log messages
```
[[2026/01/06 01:02:13.419 -08:00] [INFO] [compaction_runner.rs:383] ["top compaction candidate"] [mvcc_versions_scanned=2] [tikv_rows=1576] [rocksdb_tombstones=0] [tikv_estimated_discardable=346896] [total_entries=437536] [score=2000] [reg      ion_id=96] [rank=1] [thread_id=117]
[2026/01/06 01:02:13.419 -08:00] [INFO] [compaction_runner.rs:399] ["collected 1 compaction candidates"] [thread_id=117]
[2026/01/06 01:02:14.668 -08:00] [INFO] [compaction_filter.rs:681] ["Compaction filter reports"] [filtered=253256] [total=254819] [thread_id=157]
[2026/01/06 01:02:14.737 -08:00] [INFO] [compaction_runner.rs:487] ["compacted candidate"] [duration_ms=1317] [processed_count=1] [score=2000] [region_id=96] [thread_id=117]
 [2026/01/06 01:07:13.426 -08:00] [INFO] [compaction_runner.rs:399] ["collected 0 compaction candidates"] [thread_id=117]
````


Side effects

- [ ] Performance regression: Consumes more CPU
- [ ] Performance regression: Consumes more Memory
- [ ] Breaking backward compatibility

### Release note
<!-- 
Compatibility change, improvement, bugfix, and new feature need a release note.

Please refer to [Release Notes Language Style Guide](https://pingcap.github.io/tidb-dev-guide/contribute-to-tidb/release-notes-style-guide.html) to write a quality release note.

If you don't think this PR needs a release note then fill it with None.
If this PR will be picked to release branch, then a release note is probably required.
-->

A new option is added to enable load based compaction
**mvcc-read-aware-enabled**: bool - Enable MVCC-read-aware compaction prioritization. When enabled, regions with high MVCC version scanning during reads will be prioritized for compaction
**mvcc-scan-threshold** - Minimum MVCC versions scanned per request to consider region hot Regions where reads encounter this many or more MVCC versions pe request will get higher compaction priority
 **mvcc-read-weight** - Weight multiplier for MVCC read activity in scoring Higher values give more priority to regions with MVCC read activity.

body:
This is a cherry-pick of #19230

- The Get / BatchGet will return the commit_ts for each value if `need_commit_ts` is specified.
- coprocessor: support return _tidb_commit_ts column for TableScan coprocessor request

This is also a cherry-pick of #19300

- coprocessor: fix panic when _tidb_commit_ts column is placed before PK handle columns

This is also a cherry-pick of #19301

- *: support need_commit_ts for TiKV command batch_get_command

If you haven't already, please read TiKV's [CONTRIBUTING](https://github.com/tikv/tikv/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md) document.

If you're unsure about anything, just ask; somebody should be along to answer within a day or two.

PR Title Format:

2. *: what's changed
-->

### What is changed and how it works?
<!--
Please create an issue first to describe the problem.

There MUST be one line starting with "Issue Number:  " and 
linking the relevant issues via the "close" or "ref".

For more info, check https://github.com/tikv/tikv/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md#linking-issues.
-->

Issue Number: Close #19312

<!--
You could use "commit message" code block to add more description to the final commit message.
For more info, check https://github.com/tikv/tikv/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md#format-of-the-commit-message.
-->
What's Changed:

```commit-message
support returning commit ts for Get / BatchGet / Coprocessor
```

#### Review guidelines

This PR supports returning the commit timestamp for the following cases:

- When GetRequest / BatchGetRequest has `need_commit_ts == true`. It will return a non-zero `commit_ts`  in GetResponse / BatchGetResponse.
- For coprocessor request, when `TableScan.columns` has a column with id `EXTRA_COMMIT_TS_COL_ID` which is `-5`, it fills the commit ts of the row.

Please review this PR and pay close attention to the following points:

- When `need_commit_ts` is `false` or `EXTRA_COMMIT_TS_COL_ID` is not used, the behavior should be exactly the same as the previous implementation. And the commit_ts field should be zero in `GetResponse / BatchGetResponse`
- When `need_commit_ts` is `true` or `EXTRA_COMMIT_TS_COL_ID` one of the columns in the coprocessor request, the right commit ts should be returned. Please take care of some corner cases, for example, when `resolved_locks` or `committed_locks` is set in the rpc requests.

## Pull request overview

This PR adds support for returning commit timestamps in Get/BatchGet operations and introduces the `_tidb_commit_ts` column for TableScan coprocessor requests. The implementation introduces new type `ValueEntry` that wraps a value with an optional commit timestamp, and extends the storage layer, MVCC readers, and coprocessor executors to propagate this information.

**Changes:**
- Added `ValueEntry` and `KvPairEntry` types to wrap values with optional commit timestamps
- Extended Get/BatchGet APIs to accept `need_commit_ts`/`load_commit_ts` parameters
- Added support for `EXTRA_COMMIT_TS_COL_ID` column in TableScan executor

### Reviewed changes

Copilot reviewed 30 out of 31 changed files in this pull request and generated 2 comments.

title: crossbeam-skiplist: fix RefRange iterator memory leak (#19284)
body:
This is an automated cherry-pick of #19284

### What is changed and how it works?

Issue Number: Close #19285

What's Changed:

Fix a memory leak in `crossbeam-skiplist`'s `RefRange` iterator.

`RefRange::next()` and `next_back()` were using `clone_from()` to update `self.head`/`self.tail`. Since `RefEntry` has no `Drop` implementation (by design - callers must explicitly call `release()`), the old entry was dropped without decrementing its refcount, causing permanent memory leaks.
Note that the the comment of `RefEntry` says
```
/// You *must* call `release` to free this type, otherwise the node will be
/// leaked. This is because releasing the entry requires a `Guard`.
```

This bug affects any code using `SkipMap::range()` iterators. In TiKV, this manifests as memory leaks in `LockTable` when `check_range()` or `find_first()` are called.

```rust
// Before (buggy):
self.head.clone_from(&next_head);  // Old entry dropped without decrement!

// After (fixed):
if let Some(e) = mem::replace(&mut self.head, next_head.clone()) {
    unsafe { e.node.decrement(guard); }  // Properly decrement old entry
}
```

The fix matches the pattern already used in `RefIter::next()`.

Stress test results (10 seconds, range iteration + insert/remove cycle):

```
before fix:
t=10s ops=11710 len=3422 alloc=613MB
after fix:
t=10s ops=11786 len=7530 alloc=3MB
```


```commit-message
Fix RefRange::next() and next_back() to properly decrement old self.head/self.tail refcount.
```

### Related changes

- [ ] PR to update `pingcap/docs`/`pingcap/docs-cn`:
- [x] Need to cherry-pick to the release branch

### Check List

Tests

- [ ] Unit test
- [x] Integration test
- [ ] Manual test (add detailed scripts or steps below)
- [ ] No code

Side effects

- [ ] Performance regression: Consumes more CPU
- [ ] Performance regression: Consumes more Memory
- [ ] Breaking backward compatibility

### Release note

```release-note
Fix a memory leak in crossbeam-skiplist's RefRange iterator that caused LockTable memory to grow unboundedly when using range queries.
```
author: ti-chi-bot

commits.message:
crossbeam-skiplist: fix RefRange iterator memory leak

RefRange::next() and next_back() were using clone_from() to update
self.head/self.tail. Since RefEntry has no Drop implementation,
the old entry was dropped without decrementing its refcount, causing
permanent memory leaks.

Fix by using mem::replace() and manually calling decrement() on the
old entry, matching the pattern used in RefIter::next().

Signed-off-by: ekexium <eke@fastmail.com>
