title: crossbeam-skiplist: fix RefRange iterator memory leak
body:
### What is changed and how it works?

Issue Number: Close #19285

What's Changed:

Fix a memory leak in `crossbeam-skiplist`'s `RefRange` iterator.

`RefRange::next()` and `next_back()` were using `clone_from()` to update `self.head`/`self.tail`. Since `RefEntry` has no `Drop` implementation (by design - callers must explicitly call `release()`), the old entry was dropped without decrementing its refcount, causing permanent memory leaks.
Note that the the comment of `RefEntry` says
```
/// You *must* call `release` to free this type, otherwise the node will be
/// leaked. This is because releasing the entry requires a `Guard`.
```

This bug affects any code using `SkipMap::range()` iterators. In TiKV, this manifests as memory leaks in `LockTable` when `check_range()` or `find_first()` are called.

```rust
// Before (buggy):
self.head.clone_from(&next_head);  // Old entry dropped without decrement!

// After (fixed):
if let Some(e) = mem::replace(&mut self.head, next_head.clone()) {
    unsafe { e.node.decrement(guard); }  // Properly decrement old entry
}
```

The fix matches the pattern already used in `RefIter::next()`.

Stress test results (10 seconds, range iteration + insert/remove cycle):

```
before fix:
t=10s ops=11710 len=3422 alloc=613MB
after fix:
t=10s ops=11786 len=7530 alloc=3MB
```


```commit-message
Fix RefRange::next() and next_back() to properly decrement old self.head/self.tail refcount.
```

### Related changes

- [ ] PR to update `pingcap/docs`/`pingcap/docs-cn`:
- [x] Need to cherry-pick to the release branch

### Check List

Tests

- [ ] Unit test
- [x] Integration test
- [ ] Manual test (add detailed scripts or steps below)
- [ ] No code

Side effects

- [ ] Performance regression: Consumes more CPU
- [ ] Performance regression: Consumes more Memory
- [ ] Breaking backward compatibility

### Release note

```release-note
Fix a memory leak in crossbeam-skiplist's RefRange iterator that caused LockTable memory to grow unboundedly when using range queries.
```
author: ekexium

reviews.body: ## Pull request overview This PR fixes a critical memory leak in the `crossbeam-skiplist` crate's `RefRange` iterator implementation. The issue occurred because `RefRange::next()` and `next_back()` were using `clone_from()` to update internal state, which dropped old `RefEntry` objects without decrementing their reference counts, leading to memory leaks. **Changes:** - Fixed memory leak in `RefRange::next()` by properly decrementing old entry refcount - Fixed memory leak in `RefRange::next_back()` by properly decrementing old entry refcount - Added regression test `range_iter_refcount_leak` to prevent future regressions ### Reviewed changes Copilot reviewed 2 out of 2 changed files in this pull request and generated no comments. | File | Description | | ---- | ----------- | | components/crossbeam-skiplist/src/base.rs | Fixed `RefRange::next()` and `next_back()` to use `mem::replace()` with explicit `decrement()` instead of `clone_from()` | | components/crossbeam-skiplist/tests/map.rs | Added regression test that exercises range iteration to detect memory leaks | --- 💡 Add Copilot custom instructions for smarter, more guided reviews. Learn how to get started.
Should we impl Drop for `RefRange` and call `drop_impl`?
LGTM

commits.message:
crossbeam-skiplist: fix RefRange iterator memory leak

RefRange::next() and next_back() were using clone_from() to update
self.head/self.tail. Since RefEntry has no Drop implementation,
the old entry was dropped without decrementing its refcount, causing
permanent memory leaks.

Fix by using mem::replace() and manually calling decrement() on the
old entry, matching the pattern used in RefIter::next().

Signed-off-by: ekexium <eke@fastmail.com>

It is initialized to be false, and will be updated when we use tikv-ctl to change the "resource-metering.enable-network-io-collection" config value dynamically. 
However, when we set this config value to true in tikv config file, and restart tikv-server, the global variable will not be set to true due to no changes detected.
So, in this PR, we update the global variable value when tikv-server initialization using config value.

copr: improve observability of total suspend time (#19178)

close tikv/tikv#19179

Changes:
- Include coprocessor suspend time in the existing copr wait time metric
- Add panels for semaphore waiting time and the number of copr tasks
  waiting on the semaphore.

Background: 
- `total_suspend_time` measures how long a coprocessor task is not
actually being processed. This can be prolonged for several reasons,
including YATP scheduling wait and waiting for the concurrency limiter
semaphore. Previously, `total_suspend_time` was only visible in TiKV’s
slow query logs.
- Semaphore waiting is one of the main contributors to long suspend
times. The concurrency limiter exists to prioritize completing a limited
number of heavy tasks rather than spreading work thin across too many
tasks. In this mechanism, when a copr task runs for more than 5ms, it
must acquire a semaphore permit before continuing. The total number of 
permits defaults to the number of CPU cores.

Signed-off-by: Bisheng Huang <hbisheng@gmail.com>

title: crossbeam-skiplist: fix RefRange iterator memory leak (#19284)
body:
This is an automated cherry-pick of #19284

### What is changed and how it works?

Issue Number: Close #19285

What's Changed:

Fix a memory leak in `crossbeam-skiplist`'s `RefRange` iterator.

`RefRange::next()` and `next_back()` were using `clone_from()` to update `self.head`/`self.tail`. Since `RefEntry` has no `Drop` implementation (by design - callers must explicitly call `release()`), the old entry was dropped without decrementing its refcount, causing permanent memory leaks.
Note that the the comment of `RefEntry` says
```
/// You *must* call `release` to free this type, otherwise the node will be
/// leaked. This is because releasing the entry requires a `Guard`.
```

This bug affects any code using `SkipMap::range()` iterators. In TiKV, this manifests as memory leaks in `LockTable` when `check_range()` or `find_first()` are called.

```rust
// Before (buggy):
self.head.clone_from(&next_head);  // Old entry dropped without decrement!

// After (fixed):
if let Some(e) = mem::replace(&mut self.head, next_head.clone()) {
    unsafe { e.node.decrement(guard); }  // Properly decrement old entry
}
```

The fix matches the pattern already used in `RefIter::next()`.

Stress test results (10 seconds, range iteration + insert/remove cycle):

```
before fix:
t=10s ops=11710 len=3422 alloc=613MB
after fix:
t=10s ops=11786 len=7530 alloc=3MB
```


```commit-message
Fix RefRange::next() and next_back() to properly decrement old self.head/self.tail refcount.
```

### Related changes

- [ ] PR to update `pingcap/docs`/`pingcap/docs-cn`:
- [x] Need to cherry-pick to the release branch

### Check List

Tests

- [ ] Unit test
- [x] Integration test
- [ ] Manual test (add detailed scripts or steps below)
- [ ] No code

Side effects

- [ ] Performance regression: Consumes more CPU
- [ ] Performance regression: Consumes more Memory
- [ ] Breaking backward compatibility

### Release note

```release-note
Fix a memory leak in crossbeam-skiplist's RefRange iterator that caused LockTable memory to grow unboundedly when using range queries.
```
author: ti-chi-bot

commits.message:
crossbeam-skiplist: fix RefRange iterator memory leak

RefRange::next() and next_back() were using clone_from() to update
self.head/self.tail. Since RefEntry has no Drop implementation,
the old entry was dropped without decrementing its refcount, causing
permanent memory leaks.

Fix by using mem::replace() and manually calling decrement() on the
old entry, matching the pattern used in RefIter::next().

author: gengliqi

reviews.body: ## Pull request overview This PR fixes incorrect TIMESTAMP handling in the `to_packed_u64` method that was caused by applying timezone offset twice during the packing process. The method previously used `try_from_chrono_datetime` which includes timezone validation via `check`, but this validation was incorrectly applied to UTC values that had already been converted from local time. **Changes:** - Replaced `try_from_chrono_datetime` with `unchecked_new` in `to_packed_u64` to avoid double timezone validation - Added test cases for timestamps near Unix epoch boundary to verify the fix --- 💡 Add Copilot custom instructions for smarter, more guided reviews. Learn how to get started.

reviews.body:
## Pull request overview This PR adds support for shared locks in the lock wait table, enabling multiple waiters for shared locks to be woken up simultaneously when a conflicting lock is released. This is preparatory work for full shared lock support. **Key changes:** - Extended `AcquirePessimisticLock` command to accept 3-tuple keys (key, should_not_exist, is_shared_lock) - Modified lock wait queue to group and wake up consecutive shared lock waiters together - Added detection and handling for shared lock commands to disable in-memory pessimistic lock optimization - Excluded shared locks from deadlock detection ### Reviewed changes Copilot reviewed 9 out of 9 changed files in this pull request and generated 3 comments.
Show a summary per file | File | Description | | ---- | ----------- | | `src/storage/txn/commands/acquire_pessimistic_lock.rs` | Updated key tuple format to include is_shared_lock flag | | `src/storage/lock_manager/lock_waiting_queue.rs` | Implemented group wake-up logic for consecutive shared lock waiters | | `src/storage/txn/scheduler.rs` | Added shared lock command detection and modified wake-up handling to process multiple entries | | `src/server/lock_manager/mod.rs` | Excluded shared locks from deadlock detection | | `src/storage/mod.rs` | Updated test helper functions to support new 3-tuple format | | `src/storage/txn/commands/mod.rs` | Updated protocol buffer conversion to extract shared lock flag | | `tests/failpoints/cases/test_storage.rs` | Updated test cases to use new 3-tuple format | | Multiple test files | Added is_shared_lock field initialization in test code |
--- 💡 Add Copilot custom instructions for smarter, more guided reviews.

title: tikv_util: Centralize all thread name prefixes for both V1 and V2
body:
### What is changed and how it works?

Issue Number: Close https://github.com/tikv/tikv/issues/19175

What's Changed:

```commit-message
Centralize all thread name prefixes into a tikv_util file for both V1 and V2.
```

See the issue for context. Changes I made here:

- Add a file `components/tikv_util/src/thread_name_prefix.rs` as the centralized location, and move all thread name string literals there.
- Sort all constant string literals by when the corresponding threads are created during server init. This makes it easier for us to look up, organize and manage threads.
- Rename some confusing (usually due to excessive abbreviation) threads:
  - `rtkp` -> `runtime-keeper `
  - `inc-scan` -> `resolved-ts-scanner`
  - `impwkr` -> `import-sst-worker`
  - `bkwkr` -> `backup-worker`
- Fix typo in thread name
  - `refreash-config-worker`

There are some thread names unique to API V1 (e.g. "raftstore") or V2 (e.g. "rs"). Regardless, they are all put into the centralized file.

How I tested it works:

Intentionally rename all string constants (extremely easy to do with any text editor) in the tikv_util file to add suffix "-oh-yeah". Run LLDB to verify that all thread names now become like "sched-worker-pool-oh-yeah-0", "steady-timer-oh-yeah", "gc-manager-oh-yeah" etc.

reviews.body: ## Pull request overview This PR enables TiKV to use a local clock-based synthetic timestamp when PD (Placement Driver) is unavailable during backup stream flushing operations. This prevents metadata loss in scenarios where PD connectivity is interrupted. **Key Changes:** - Added synthetic timestamp generation using local clock and UUID suffix when flush_ts is zero (indicating PD unavailability) - Modified `flush_backup_metadata` to return a list of created metadata files for better observability - Reduced verbose logging in checkpoint manager when checkpoint values remain unchanged ### Reviewed changes Copilot reviewed 2 out of 2 changed files in this pull request and generated 6 comments. | File | Description | | ---- | ----------- | | components/backup-stream/src/router.rs | Implements synthetic timestamp generation with UUID-based collision avoidance, updates function signature to return metadata file paths, enhances flush logging, and adds comprehensive unit tests | | components/backup-stream/src/checkpoint_manager.rs | Reduces log verbosity by preventing duplicate warnings when checkpoint values are identical (common during transaction pending states) | --- 💡 Add Copilot custom instructions for smarter, more guided reviews. author: 3pointer

reviews.body: ## Pull request overview This PR addresses a critical concurrency issue in the log backup system by reducing the lifetime of a DashMap lock during the flush operation. The original code held a DashMap reference guard across asynchronous await points, which could potentially cause the log backup to get stuck. **Key Changes:** - Refactored `do_flush` method to use `get_task_handler` helper method that clones the Arc before returning, releasing the DashMap lock immediately - Added clippy lint configuration to detect and prevent similar issues in the future where DashMap references are held across await points ### Reviewed changes Copilot reviewed 2 out of 2 changed files in this pull request and generated no comments. | File | Description | | ---- | ----------- | | components/backup-stream/src/router.rs | Refactored `do_flush` to use `get_task_handler` method instead of directly accessing `self.tasks`, reducing lock lifetime by releasing DashMap guard before await points | | clippy.toml | Added await-holding-invalid-types lint configuration to detect holding DashMap::Ref across await points | | tests/.disk_latency_inspector.tmp | Accidentally committed temporary file that should be removed | --- 💡 Add Copilot custom instructions for smarter, more guided reviews. Learn how to get started.

## Pull request overview Copilot reviewed 2 out of 2 changed files in this pull request and generated no new comments. --- 💡 Add Copilot custom instructions for smarter, more guided reviews. Learn how to get started.

author: you06

reviews.body:
## Pull request overview

This PR implements encode/decode functionality for shared locks in TiKV's MVCC system. The implementation adds support for a new `LockType::Shared` variant that can contain multiple transaction locks, enabling shared locking semantics.

- Introduces `SharedLocks` struct to represent multiple transaction locks under a single shared lock
- Updates `parse_lock()` to return `Either<Lock, SharedLocks>` to handle both lock types
- Adds serialization/deserialization logic for the new shared lock format with embedded transaction info segments

### Reviewed changes

Copilot reviewed 38 out of 39 changed files in this pull request and generated 3 comments.

<details>
<summary>Show a summary per file</summary>

| File | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |
| components/txn_types/src/lock.rs | Core implementation: adds `LockType::Shared` enum variant, `SharedLocks` struct with encoding/decoding, `parse_lock()` function, and helper methods |
| components/txn_types/src/lib.rs | Exports new `LockOrSharedLocks` type alias and `parse_lock()` function |
| components/txn_types/src/write.rs | Maps `LockType::Shared` to `None` for WriteType conversion |
| components/tikv_util/src/lib.rs | Adds `PartialEq` derive to `Either` enum |
| components/tikv_util/src/memory.rs | Implements `HeapSize` for `Either` and refactors tuple `HeapSize` implementations using macro |
| src/storage/types.rs | Maps `LockType::Shared` to `kvrpcpb::Op::SharedLock` in MvccInfo |
| src/storage/txn/store.rs | Handles `Either` return type from `parse_lock()` with unimplemented for SharedLocks case |
| src/storage/txn/commands/*.rs | Updates lock loading sites to handle `Either<Lock, SharedLocks>` return type |
| src/storage/txn/actions/*.rs | Updates transaction action handlers to pattern match on Either type |
| src/storage/mvcc/reader/*.rs | Updates all MVCC readers and scanners to handle new lock type with Either pattern matching |
| src/storage/mvcc/*.rs | Updates MVCC modu

reviews.body: ## Pull request overview This PR implements conflict resolution logic for the compact-log-backup component to handle cases where multiple writes to the same key are encountered during compaction. This addresses issue #18300 where compaction would previously fail with an assertion error when encountering duplicate keys with different values. **Key Changes:** - Added a `resolve` function that attempts to intelligently choose which record to keep when conflicts are detected - Replaced the strict assertion that failed on any duplicate with conflict resolution logic that handles known patterns (e.g., rollback to put with collapsed rollback, protected vs unprotected rollbacks) - Added comprehensive test coverage for various conflict scenarios ### Reviewed changes Copilot reviewed 3 out of 4 changed files in this pull request and generated 7 comments. | File | Description | | ---- | ----------- | | components/compact-log-backup/src/compaction/exec.rs | Added conflict resolution logic via new `resolve` function, refactored `update_checksum_diff` and `process_input` to support conflict resolution, added extensive test cases | | components/compact-log-backup/src/util.rs | Added `redact` utility function for safely logging potentially sensitive key/value data | | components/compact-log-backup/Cargo.toml | Added `log_wrappers` and `rand` dependencies for logging and conflict ID generation | | Cargo.lock | Updated with new dependency entries | --- 💡 Add Copilot custom instructions for smarter, more guided reviews. Learn how to get started.

author: YuJuncen

reviews.body: ## Pull request overview This PR fixes a bug where temporary SST files could be left behind when downloads fail in the `sst_importer` component. The fix ensures that cleanup happens on all exit paths (both success and error) by using Rust's `defer!` macro pattern. **Key Changes:** - Implemented automatic cleanup of temporary files using the `defer!` macro to ensure cleanup occurs on both success and error paths - Added a new failpoint `download_files_ext_after_download` to enable testing of the cleanup behavior when errors occur after downloads complete - Added comprehensive test coverage to verify temporary files are properly cleaned up on error ### Reviewed changes Copilot reviewed 2 out of 2 changed files in this pull request and generated 1 comment. | File | Description | | ---- | ----------- | | components/sst_importer/src/sst_importer.rs | Modified `download_files_ext` to use `defer!` for guaranteed cleanup of temporary files, added failpoint for testing, and included a new test `test_download_files_ext_cleanup_temp_files_on_error` | | components/sst_importer/Cargo.toml | Added `tikv/failpoints` dependency to the failpoints feature to enable failpoint testing | --- 💡 Add Copilot custom instructions for smarter, more guided reviews. Learn how to get started.

author: lcwangchao

reviews.body:
## Pull request overview

This PR adds support for returning commit timestamps with Get/BatchGet/Coprocessor operations, enabling TiDB to retrieve both values and their commit timestamps in a single request. This is a cherry-pick of PRs #19103 and #19166 to resolve issue #19102.

**Key changes:**
- Introduces `ValueEntry` and `KvPairEntry` types to carry both values and optional commit timestamps
- Adds `load_commit_ts` parameters throughout the storage stack to control commit timestamp loading
- Updates Get/BatchGet RPC handlers to return commit timestamps when `need_commit_ts` is set
- Adds support for `_tidb_commit_ts` virtual column in TableScan coprocessor requests

### Reviewed changes

Copilot reviewed 30 out of 30 changed files in this pull request and generated 9 comments.

<details>
<summary>Show a summary per file</summary>

| File | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |
| components/txn_types/src/types.rs | Defines new `ValueEntry` and `KvPairEntry` types to encapsulate values with optional commit timestamps |
| src/storage/txn/store.rs | Extends Store trait with `get_entry` and `incremental_get_entry` methods, updates `batch_get` and `scanner` signatures to support commit timestamp loading |
| src/storage/mvcc/reader/point_getter.rs | Adds `get_entry` method and updates lock handling to skip access_locks when commit_ts is required |
| src/storage/mvcc/reader/scanner/forward.rs | Updates `LatestKvPolicy` to return `ValueEntry` and load commit timestamps from write CF keys |
| src/storage/mvcc/reader/scanner/backward.rs | Updates `BackwardKvScanner` to track and return commit timestamps with values |
| src/storage/mod.rs | Adds `get_entry` method that wraps existing `get` to support commit timestamp loading |
| src/server/service/kv.rs | Updates Get and BatchGet RPC handlers to check `need_commit_ts` flag and populate response commit timestamps |
| src/storage/errors.rs | Adds `map_kv_pair_entries` function and updates mapping logic to handle comm

*: support to return commit timestamp for Get / BatchGet requests (#19103)

- `fn on_split_region_check_tick` (by SplitReason_SIZE)
  - ~~`fn split_check(auto_split: true) -> SplitCheckTask{auto_split: true}`~~
  - `fn split_check(reason: SIZE) -> SplitCheckTask{reason: SIZE}`
    - ~~`fn check_split_and_bucket(auto_split: true) -> fn ask_split(source: "split checker") -> CasualMessage::SplitRegion{source: "split checker"}`~~
    - `fn check_split_and_bucket(reason: SIZE) -> fn ask_split(source: "split_checker_by_size") -> CasualMessage::SplitRegion{source: "split_checker_by_size"}`
      - ~~`fn on_prepare_split_region(source: "split checker") -> PdTask::AskBatchSplit{}`~~
      - `fn on_prepare_split_region(source: "split_checker_by_size") -> PdTask::AskBatchSplit{reason: SIZE}`
        - ~~`fn handle_ask_batch_split(task: "batch_split")`~~
        - `fn handle_ask_batch_split(reason: SIZE)`

- `fn load_base_split` (by SplitReason_LOAD)
  - `Task::AutoSplit`
    - ~~case 1: `fn handle_ask_batch_split(task: "auto_split")`~~
    - case 1: `fn handle_ask_batch_split(reason: LOAD)`
    - case 2: `CasualMessage::HalfSplitRegion{source: "auto_split"}`
      - ~~`fn on_schedule_half_split_region -> fn split_check_key_range(auto_split: false) -> SplitCheckTask{auto_split: false}`~~
      - `fn on_schedule_half_split_region -> fn split_check_key_range(reason: LOAD) -> SplitCheckTask{reason: LOAD}`
        - ~~`fn check_split_and_bucket(auto_split: true) -> fn ask_split(source: "split checker") -> CasualMessage::SplitRegion{source: "split checker"}`~~

            - ~~`fn handle_ask_batch_split(task: "batch_split")`~~
            - `fn handle_ask_batch_split(reason: LOAD)`

- tidb grpc (by SplitReason_ADMIN)

    - ~~`fn on_prepare_split_region(source: ctx.peer()) -> PdTask::AskBatchSplit{}`~~

- pd gRPC (by SplitReason_ADMIN)
  - case 1: `CasualMessage::SplitRegion{source: "pd"}`
    - ~~`fn on_prepare_split_region(source: "pd") -> PdTask::AskBatchSplit{}`~~

<!--
You could use "commit message" code block to add more description to the final commit message.
For more info, check https://github.com/tikv/tikv/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md#format-of-the-commit-message.
-->
What's Changed:

```commit-message
raftstore: use SplitReason in SplitCheckerHost
```

In #19013, both `SplitReason::Load` and `SplitReason::Size` were treated as `auto_split` in `SplitCheckerHost`. This contradicts the expected behavior of `SplitCheckerHost`, which should only treat `SplitReason::Size` as `auto_split`. To avoid ambiguity, `SplitReason` was used to replace `auto_split` in `SplitCheckerHost`.

test report is https://github.com/tikv/tikv/issues/19220#issuecomment-3669304240

title: cdc: add watchdog for cdc conn (#18757)
body:
This is an automated cherry-pick of #18757

<!--
Thank you for contributing to TiKV!

If you haven't already, please read TiKV's [CONTRIBUTING](https://github.com/tikv/tikv/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md) document.

If you're unsure about anything, just ask; somebody should be along to answer within a day or two.

PR Title Format:
1. module [, module2, module3]: what's changed
2. *: what's changed
-->

### What is changed and how it works?
<!--
Please create an issue first to describe the problem.

There MUST be one line starting with "Issue Number:  " and 
linking the relevant issues via the "close" or "ref".

For more info, check https://github.com/tikv/tikv/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md#linking-issues.
-->


Issue Number: Close #18169, close #18915

The main intent is to resolve a problem where the CDC sink's memory quota increases indefinitely after a downstream TiCDC node disconnects. This leak persists until the TiKV instance is manually restarted. The underlying cause is suspected to be dangling connections that are not properly released after the downstream becomes unavailable.

In summary, this PR resolves the issue through the following steps:

1. Introduce Watchdog: A background task is created specifically for monitoring.

2. Register Connections: New CDC connections are registered with the watchdog upon creation.

3. Periodic Checks: The watchdog periodically checks the last_flush_time of each connection against a timeout.

4. Release Connections: If a connection is determined to be stuck, the watchdog forcibly disconnects it, releasing its associated memory and resources.

This mechanism ensures that dangling connections are promptly cleaned up even if a downstream TiCDC node becomes unresponsive, preventing the unbounded memory growth and improving the system's stability.

<!--
You could use "commit message" code block to add more description to the final commit message.
For more info, check https://github.com/tikv/tikv/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md#format-of-the-commit-message.
-->
What's Changed:

```commit-message

```

### Related changes

- [ ] PR to update `pingcap/docs`/`pingcap/docs-cn`:
- [x] Need to cherry-pick to the release branch

### Check List

Tests <!-- At least one of them must be included. -->

- [ ] Unit test
- [x] Integration test
- [x] Manual test (add detailed scripts or steps below)

1. Make a tikv with failpoint enable. 
```
FAIL_POINT=1 make build
```
2. Start a cluster with this tikv-server.
3. Create 3 changefeeds, and they are normal.
4. Inject the failpoints.
```
curl -X PUT "http://127.0.0.1:20180/fail/cdc_idle_deregister_threshold" -d "return(true)"

curl -X PUT "http://127.0.0.1:20180/fail/cdc_sleep_after_sink_flush"    -d "return(true)"
```
5. It can be seen that all changefeed got stuck and.
6. After 30 seconds, the cdc connections were closed, and the downstream cdc will try to create a new connection 
```
[2025/07/31 16:27:48.884 +08:00] [ERROR] [service.rs:568] ["cdc connection idle for too long, aborting connection"] [seconds_since_last_flush=40] [conn_id=ConnId(217)] [downstream=ipv4:127.0.0.1:49452] [thread_id=170]
[2025/07/31 16:27:48.884 +08:00] [ERROR] [service.rs:568] ["cdc connection idle for too long, aborting connection"] [seconds_since_last_flush=40] [conn_id=ConnId(216)] [downstream=ipv4:127.0.0.1:49452] [thread_id=170]
[2025/07/31 16:27:48.902 +08:00] [ERROR] [service.rs:568] ["cdc connection idle for too long, aborting connection"] [seconds_since_last_flush=40] [conn_id=ConnId(219)] [downstream=ipv4:127.0.0.1:49453] [thread_id=170]
[2025/07/31 16:27:48.902 +08:00] [ERROR] [service.rs:568] ["cdc connection idle for too long, aborting connection"] [seconds_since_last_flush=40] [conn_id=ConnId(218)] [downstream=ipv4:127.0.0.1:49453] [thread_id=170]
[2025/07/31 16:27:49.887 +08:00] [INFO] [service.rs:448] ["cdc connection created"] [conn_id=ConnId(228)] [features="[\"stream-multiplexing\"]"] [downstream=ipv4:127.0.0.1:49737] [thread_id=9]
[2025/07/31 16:27:49.889 +08:00] [INFO] [service.rs:448] ["cdc connection created"] [conn_id=ConnId(229)] [features="[\"stream-multiplexing\"]"] [downstream=ipv4:127.0.0.1:49737] [thread_id=9]
[2025/07/31 16:27:49.904 +08:00] [INFO] [service.rs:448] ["cdc connection created"] [conn_id=ConnId(230)] [features="[\"stream-multiplexing\"]"] [downstream=ipv4:127.0.0.1:49740] [thread_id=10]
[2025/07/31 16:27:49.905 +08:00] [INFO] [service.rs:448] ["cdc connection created"] [conn_id=ConnId(231)] [features="[\"stream-multiplexing\"]"] [downstream=ipv4:127.0.0.1:49740] [thread_id=10]
```
7. Delete all failpoints.
```
curl -X DELETE "http://127.0.0.1:20180/fail/cdc_sleep_after_sink_flush"
curl -X DELETE "http://127.0.0.1:20180/fail/cdc_idle_deregister_threshold"
```
8. Changefeeds resume to normal.

Side effects

- [ ] Performance regression: Consumes more Memory
- [ ] Breaking backward compatibility

title: coprocessor: fix the indexlookup push down local lookup range may exceeds the region end
body:
<!--
Thank you for contributing to TiKV!

If you haven't already, please read TiKV's [CONTRIBUTING](https://github.com/tikv/tikv/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md) document.

If you're unsure about anything, just ask; somebody should be along to answer within a day or two.

PR Title Format:
1. module [, module2, module3]: what's changed
2. *: what's changed
-->

### What is changed and how it works?
<!--
Please create an issue first to describe the problem.

There MUST be one line starting with "Issue Number:  " and 
linking the relevant issues via the "close" or "ref".

For more info, check https://github.com/tikv/tikv/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md#linking-issues.
-->

Issue Number: Close #19215

<!--
You could use "commit message" code block to add more description to the final commit message.
For more info, check https://github.com/tikv/tikv/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md#format-of-the-commit-message.
-->
What's Changed:

```commit-message
The int handle will use the `convert_to_prefix_next` as the end of the point-get range. However, the prefix next may exceeds the region end. In the previous implementation, at this time we use the region end as the table scan range end. However, the region range end is in MVCC format, we must decode it to raw format first.
```

### Related changes

- [ ] PR to update `pingcap/docs`/`pingcap/docs-cn`:
- [ ] Need to cherry-pick to the release branch

### Check List

Tests <!-- At least one of them must be included. -->

- [x] Unit test
- [ ] Integration test
- [ ] Manual test (add detailed scripts or steps below)
- [ ] No code

Side effects

- [ ] Performance regression: Consumes more CPU
- [ ] Performance regression: Consumes more Memory
- [ ] Breaking backward compatibility

### Release note
<!-- 
Compatibility change, improvement, bugfix, and new feature need a release note.

Please refer to [Release Notes Language Style Guide](https://pingcap.github.io/tidb-dev-guide/contribute-to-tidb/release-notes-style-guide.html) to write a quality release note.

If you don't think this PR needs a release note then fill it with None.
If this PR will be picked to release branch, then a release note is probably required.
-->

```release-note
None
```
author: lcwangchao

reviews.body: ## Pull request overview This PR fixes a bug in the index lookup executor where local lookup ranges may exceed the region end boundary. The issue occurs because integer handles use `convert_to_prefix_next` to calculate the point-get range end, which can exceed the region end. The fix ensures that when the region end is used to cap the range, it is properly decoded from MVCC format to raw format before being assigned to the key range. Key changes: - Decode region end keys from MVCC format to raw format before using them in TableTask key ranges - Add error handling for decoding failures with fallback to TiDB-side lookup - Update documentation to clarify that TableTask key ranges contain raw keys without MVCC encoding - Update tests to properly decode region end keys when constructing expected ranges --- 💡 Add Copilot custom instructions for smarter, more guided reviews. Learn how to get started.

commits.message:
coprocessor: fix the indexlookup push down local lookup range may exceeds the region end

Signed-off-by: Chao Wang <cclcwangchao@hotmail.com>

body:
<!--

Issue Number: close https://github.com/pingcap/tidb/issues/64949

<!--
You could use "commit message" code block to add more description to the final commit message.
For more info, check https://github.com/tikv/tikv/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md#format-of-the-commit-message.
-->
What's Changed:

- change `KVPair` to `KvPairEntry` to include the commit ts information
- pass `load_commit_ts` from top side down
- no protocol change,  for coprocessor request, DAG contains the executors like TableScan, and also column info are available, there is an extra EXTRA_COMMIT_TS_COL_ID = -5 column for the _tidb_commit_ts
- In table scan executor, when it detects EXTRA_COMMIT_TS_COL_ID, pass `load_commit_ts = true` to get the extra commit ts, fill data to that `_tidb_commit_ts` column
- In MVCC scanner, return commit_ts by ValueEntry


```commit-message
coprocessor: support return _tidb_commit_ts column for TableScan coprocessor request

The fix adds two strategic calls to `on_check_peer_complete_apply_logs()` to ensure the state is properly checked and cleared during hibernation scenarios. - Adds `busy_on_apply` check after updating leader committed index to catch hibernating peers that receive updates - Adds `busy_on_apply` check before responding to hibernate requests to ensure proper state cleanup before entering hibernation --- 💡 Add Copilot custom instructions for smarter, more guided reviews. Learn how to get started.

commits.message:
raftstore: fix the corner case if entering hiberate state without correctly clearing `busy_on_apply` state.

Add more uts.

title: raftstore: optimize hibernate condition (#19082)
body: This is an automated cherry-pick of #19082 ### What is changed and how it works? Issue Number: Close #19070 What's Changed: ```commit-message 1. Hibernate condition optimization: leader no need wait for all peers' hibernate votes if any non-voter peer is down and votes reach majority. 2. If non hibernate vote peers are all unreachable, leader can skip broadcasting hibernate request. This can reduce the number of messages before down peer is detected (10min by default). ``` As noted in #19070, when a TiKV peer becomes unavailable, region leaders on other nodes are prevented from entering hibernation due to the current condition logic—which requires the leader to receive votes from all peers before hibernating. This requirement is overly strict. In practice, the leader only needs to confirm that it has received votes from all alive peers(referencing the down_peers field) and that a majority has been reached for raft voters. Before Modification After Modification ### Related changes - [ ] PR to update `pingcap/docs`/`pingcap/docs-cn`: - [ ] Need to cherry-pick to the release branch ### Check List Tests - [ ] Unit test - [x] Integration test - [x] Manual test (add detailed scripts or steps below) - [ ] No code Side effects - [ ] Performance regression: Consumes more CPU - [ ] Performance regression: Consumes more Memory - [ ] Breaking backward compatibility ### Release note ```release-note Hibernate condition optimization: leader no need wait for all peers' hibernate votes if any non-voter peer is down and votes reach majority. ```

title: copr: improve observability of total suspend time (#19178)
body: This is an automated cherry-pick of #19178 ### What is changed and how it works? Issue Number: Close #19179 What's Changed: ```commit-message Changes: - Include coprocessor suspend time in the existing copr wait time metric - Add panels for semaphore waiting time and the number of copr tasks waiting on the semaphore. Background: - `total_suspend_time` measures how long a coprocessor task is not actually being processed. This can be prolonged for several reasons, including YATP scheduling wait and waiting for the concurrency limiter semaphore. Previously, `total_suspend_time` was only visible in TiKV’s slow query logs. - Semaphore waiting is one of the main contributors to long suspend times. The concurrency limiter exists to prioritize completing a limited number of heavy tasks rather than spreading work thin across too many tasks. In this mechanism, when a copr task runs for more than 5ms, it must acquire a semaphore permit before continuing. The total number of permits defaults to the number of CPU cores. ``` Grafana example: ### Related changes - [ ] PR to update `pingcap/docs`/`pingcap/docs-cn`: - [x] Need to cherry-pick to the release branch ### Check List Tests - [ ] Unit test - [ ] Integration test - [x] Manual test (add detailed scripts or steps below) - [ ] No code Side effects - [ ] Performance regression: Consumes more CPU - [ ] Performance regression: Consumes more Memory - [ ] Breaking backward compatibility ### Release note ```release-note None ```

body: ### What is changed and how it works? Issue Number: Close #19179 What's Changed: ```commit-message Changes: - Include coprocessor suspend time in the existing copr wait time metric - Add panels for semaphore waiting time and the number of copr tasks waiting on the semaphore. Background: - `total_suspend_time` measures how long a coprocessor task is not actually being processed. This can be prolonged for several reasons, including YATP scheduling wait and waiting for the concurrency limiter semaphore. Previously, `total_suspend_time` was only visible in TiKV’s slow query logs. - Semaphore waiting is one of the main contributors to long suspend times. The concurrency limiter exists to prioritize completing a limited number of heavy tasks rather than spreading work thin across too many tasks. In this mechanism, when a copr task runs for more than 5ms, it must acquire a semaphore permit before continuing. The total number of permits defaults to the number of CPU cores. ``` Grafana example: ### Related changes - [ ] PR to update `pingcap/docs`/`pingcap/docs-cn`: - [x] Need to cherry-pick to the release branch ### Check List Tests - [ ] Unit test - [ ] Integration test - [x] Manual test (add detailed scripts or steps below) - [ ] No code Side effects - [ ] Performance regression: Consumes more CPU - [ ] Performance regression: Consumes more Memory - [ ] Breaking backward compatibility ### Release note ```release-note None ```
author: hbisheng

reviews.body: LGTM

commits.message:
copr: improve observability of total suspend time

What’s changed?
- Include coprocessor suspend time in the existing copr wait time metric
- Add metrics for semaphore waiting time and the number of copr tasks
  waiting on the semaphore.

Background:
- `total_suspend_time` measures how long a coprocessor task is not
actually being processed. This can be prolonged for several reasons,
including YATP scheduling wait and waiting for the concurrency limiter
semaphore. Previously, `total_suspend_time` was only visible in TiKV’s
slow query logs.
- Semaphore waiting is one of the main contributors to long suspend
times. The concurrency limiter exists to prioritize completing a limited
number of heavy tasks rather than spreading work thin across too many
tasks, which could cause all of them to make slow progress. In this
mechanism, when a copr task runs for more than 5 ms, it must acquire a
semaphore permit before continuing. The total number of permits defaults
to the number of CPU cores.

Signed-off-by: Bisheng Huang <hbisheng@gmail.com>

fix lint

Signed-off-by: Bisheng Huang <hbisheng@gmail.com>

title: raftstore: optimize hibernate condition
body: ### What is changed and how it works? Issue Number: Close #19070 What's Changed: ```commit-message 1. Hibernate condition optimization: leader no need wait for all peers' hibernate votes if any non-voter peer is down and votes reach majority. 2. If non hibernate vote peers are all unreachable, leader can skip broadcasting hibernate request. This can reduce the number of messages before down peer is detected (10min by default). ``` As noted in #19070, when a TiKV peer becomes unavailable, region leaders on other nodes are prevented from entering hibernation due to the current condition logic—which requires the leader to receive votes from all peers before hibernating. This requirement is overly strict. In practice, the leader only needs to confirm that it has received votes from all alive peers(referencing the down_peers field) and that a majority has been reached for raft voters. Before Modification After Modification ### Related changes - [ ] PR to update `pingcap/docs`/`pingcap/docs-cn`: - [ ] Need to cherry-pick to the release branch ### Check List Tests - [ ] Unit test - [x] Integration test - [x] Manual test (add detailed scripts or steps below) - [ ] No code Side effects - [ ] Performance regression: Consumes more CPU - [ ] Performance regression: Consumes more Memory - [ ] Breaking backward compatibility ### Release note ```release-note Hibernate condition optimization: leader no need wait for all peers' hibernate votes if any non-voter peer is down and votes reach majority. ```
author: jiadebin

reviews.body: LGTM

## Pull request overview

This PR optimizes the hibernate condition logic in TiKV's raftstore to allow region leaders to enter hibernation even when some peers are down, as long as a quorum of alive peers is reached and all alive peers have voted for hibernation. Previously, leaders required votes from all peers before hibernating, which was overly strict and prevented hibernation when any peer was unavailable.

Key changes:
- Leader can now hibernate when down peers exist, provided alive voters reach quorum and all alive peers are synchronized
- Optimization to skip broadcasting hibernate requests when all non-responding peers are unreachable (in probe state with heartbeat timeout)
- Enhanced quorum checking in `check_before_tick` to only require matched peers to form a quorum, excluding down peers

### Reviewed changes

Copilot reviewed 5 out of 5 changed files in this pull request and generated 16 comments.

<details>
<summary>Show a summary per file</summary>

| File | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |
| tests/integrations/raftstore/test_hibernate.rs | Adds three comprehensive integration tests covering hibernation with down voters, down learners, and the case where peers are down but not yet detected |
| components/test_raftstore/src/util.rs | Speeds up down peer detection in tests by reducing `max_peer_down_duration` and `pd_heartbeat_tick_interval` to 5 seconds |
| components/raftstore/src/store/peer.rs | Adds helper methods to check peer heartbeat timeout and unreachability; updates `check_before_tick` to accept down_peer_ids and verify quorum among matched alive peers |
| components/raftstore/src/store/hibernate_state.rs | Refactors `maybe_hibernate` to accept a quorum-checking closure and down_peer_ids; implements logic to allow hibernation when quorum is reached among alive peers even if not all peers voted |
| components/raftstore/src/store/fsm/peer.rs | Integrates down peer tracking into the hibernate flow; adds optimization to skip broadcastin

What's Changed:


**cherry-pick: https://github.com/tikv/tikv/issues/18813**

- Introduce a new trait: `RegionStorageAccessor` to access the snapshot for secondary regions in cop-task
- Add a new function `seek_region` to `Engine` so that it can seek regions when executor is running.
- Introduce `SecondarySnapStoreAccessor` to impl  `RegionStorageAccessor`.
- Make the `ReqContext` a immutable value that can be shared accross threads safely


**cherry-pick: https://github.com/tikv/tikv/pull/18869**

coprocessor: provide BatchIndexLookUpExecutor to do index lookup in TiKV side (#18869)

- Introduce `BatchIndexLookUpExecutor` to support index look-up in TiKV side.
- Rename `SecondarySnapStoreAccessor` to `ExtraSnapStoreAccessor` to make its name easier to understand.
    
**cherry-pick: https://github.com/tikv/tikv/pull/18943**

- Add two methods `intermediate_schema` and `take_intermediate_results` to support outputting intermediate results. 
- `BatchExecutorsRunner` supports to parse `IndexLookUp` and driving it for intermediate and final results.
- Add some tests
- Rename some struct name from `SecondaryRegionXXXProvider` to `ExtraRegionXXXProvider`, which are left to rename in the previous PR.

**cherry-pick: https://github.com/tikv/tikv/pull/18997**

coprocessor: check memory locks in `ExtraSnapStoreAccessor` (#18997)
        
Check the memory locks in `ExtraSnapStoreAccessor:: get_local_region_storage` to make sure the `IndexLookUp` can get the consistency rows for 1pc or async commit.

author: lcwangchao

reviews.body:
## Pull request overview

This PR fixes a critical bug in the TopN executor where extra common handle keys were being accessed with incorrect indices. The bug occurred because the code was directly using `logical_row_index` to access the physical column keys, when it should have been using `logical_rows[logical_row_index]` to properly map from logical to physical row indices.

### Key Changes
- Fixed TopN heap to correctly access extra common handle keys using the proper logical-to-physical row mapping
- Extended projection executor to properly handle and propagate extra common handle keys instead of returning errors
- Added comprehensive test coverage across multiple executors (TopN, Selection, Projection, Limit) to verify correct handling of extra common handle keys

### Reviewed changes

Copilot reviewed 8 out of 8 changed files in this pull request and generated no comments.

<details>
<summary>Show a summary per file</summary>

| File | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |
| components/tidb_query_executors/src/util/top_n_heap.rs | Fixed bug: use `logical_rows[logical_row_index]` instead of `logical_row_index` when accessing extra common handle keys |
| components/tidb_query_executors/src/util/mock_executor.rs | Added helper method `set_extra_common_handle_keys()` for test setup |
| components/tidb_query_executors/src/top_n_executor.rs | Added comprehensive test `test_extra_common_handle_keys()` verifying TopN correctly handles extra common handle keys with logical rows |
| components/tidb_query_executors/src/selection_executor.rs | Added test `test_extra_common_handle_keys()` verifying Selection executor preserves extra keys |
| components/tidb_query_executors/src/projection_executor.rs | Removed error on extra keys, added logic to properly propagate and remap extra common handle keys based on logical rows for both column reference and expression evaluation paths |
| components/tidb_query_executors/src/limit_executor.rs | Added test `test_extra_common_ha
LGTM

title: flow-control: fix compaction slowdown caused by increased flow-control thresholds. (#18710)
body:
This is an automated cherry-pick of #18710

<!--
Thank you for contributing to TiKV!

If you haven't already, please read TiKV's [CONTRIBUTING](https://github.com/tikv/tikv/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md) document.

If you're unsure about anything, just ask; somebody should be along to answer within a day or two.

PR Title Format:
1. module [, module2, module3]: what's changed
2. *: what's changed
-->

### What is changed and how it works?
<!--
Please create an issue first to describe the problem.

There MUST be one line starting with "Issue Number:  " and 
linking the relevant issues via the "close" or "ref".

For more info, check https://github.com/tikv/tikv/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md#linking-issues.
-->

Issue Number: Close #18708

<!--
You could use "commit message" code block to add more description to the final commit message.
For more info, check https://github.com/tikv/tikv/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md#format-of-the-commit-message.
-->
What's Changed:

```commit-message
This PR addresses performance stability issues caused by increasing
storage.flow-control.l0-file-threshold and
storage.flow-control.soft-pending-compaction-bytes-limit. Previously,
raising these values could reduce the effectiveness of RocksDB’s compaction
speed-up mechanism, because the RocksDB internal thresholds
(level0-slowdown-writes-trigger and soft-pending-compaction-bytes-limit)
would be overridden, delaying compaction acceleration.

Key improvements:
1. Conditional override of RocksDB thresholds:
  - level0-slowdown-writes-trigger is overridden by l0-file-threshold
only if it is smaller.
  - soft-pending-compaction-bytes-limit is overridden only if it is
smaller than storage.flow-control.soft-pending-compaction-bytes-limit.
This ensures that increasing flow-control settings does not weaken
compaction acceleration, while user-configured RocksDB thresholds that
are larger than the flow-control limits are overriden, allowing compaction
speed-up to trigger before write flow control.
flow control.
3. Updated write stall check:
  - ingest_maybe_slowdown_writes now uses level0-stop-writes-trigger
instead of level0-slowdown-writes-trigger to determine whether ingest
may trigger a write stall.
  - This keeps the original behavior, since `l0-file-threshold` overrides
`level0-stop-writes-trigger`, just like the previous behavior with
`level0-slowdown-writes-trigger`. Ideally, flow-control settings would
be used directly to determine write stalls, but
`ingest_maybe_slowdown_writes` cannot access the flow-control module
configuration because this function resides inside the Engine module。

After this change, write control effectively has three stages:
1. Compaction acceleration: triggered when RocksDB thresholds are reached.
2. Flow control: triggered at storage.flow-control.l0-file-threshold and
storage.flow-control.soft-pending-compaction-bytes-limit.
4. Stop writes: triggered at storage.flow-control.hard-pending-compaction-bytes-limit. 
```

### Related changes

- [ ] PR to update `pingcap/docs`/`pingcap/docs-cn`:
        TODO
- [ ] Need to cherry-pick to the release branch

### Check List

Tests <!-- At least one of them must be included. -->

- [ ] Integration test
- [ ] Manual test (add detailed scripts or steps below)
- [ ] No code

Side effects

- [ ] Performance regression: Consumes more CPU
- [ ] Performance regression: Consumes more Memory
- [ ] Breaking backward compatibility

### Release note
<!-- 
Compatibility change, improvement, bugfix, and new feature need a release note.

Please refer to [Release Notes Language Style Guide](https://pingcap.github.io/tidb-dev-guide/contribute-to-tidb/release-notes-style-guide.html) to write a quality release note.

If you don't think this PR needs a release note then fill it with None.
If this PR will be picked to release branch, then a release note is probably required.
-->

```release-note
When storage.flow-control.enable is set to true, we only override rocksdb.level0_slowdown_writes_trigger to l0-file-threshold only when it is larger than l0-file-threshold and only override rocksdb.soft-pend-compaction-bytes-limit to storage.flow-control.soft-pending-compaction-bytes-limit only when it is larger than storage.flow-control.soft-pending-compaction-bytes-limit.
```

title: flow-control: fix compaction slowdown caused by increased flow-control thresholds.
body:
<!--
Thank you for contributing to TiKV!

If you haven't already, please read TiKV's [CONTRIBUTING](https://github.com/tikv/tikv/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md) document.

If you're unsure about anything, just ask; somebody should be along to answer within a day or two.

PR Title Format:
1. module [, module2, module3]: what's changed
2. *: what's changed
-->

### What is changed and how it works?
<!--
Please create an issue first to describe the problem.

There MUST be one line starting with "Issue Number:  " and 
linking the relevant issues via the "close" or "ref".

For more info, check https://github.com/tikv/tikv/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md#linking-issues.
-->

Issue Number: Close #18708

<!--
You could use "commit message" code block to add more description to the final commit message.
For more info, check https://github.com/tikv/tikv/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md#format-of-the-commit-message.
-->
What's Changed:

```commit-message
This PR addresses performance stability issues caused by increasing
storage.flow-control.l0-file-threshold and
storage.flow-control.soft-pending-compaction-bytes-limit. Previously,
raising these values could reduce the effectiveness of RocksDB’s compaction
speed-up mechanism, because the RocksDB internal thresholds
(level0-slowdown-writes-trigger and soft-pending-compaction-bytes-limit)
would be overridden, delaying compaction acceleration.

Key improvements:
1. Conditional override of RocksDB thresholds:
  - level0-slowdown-writes-trigger is overridden by l0-file-threshold
only if it is smaller.
  - soft-pending-compaction-bytes-limit is overridden only if it is
smaller than storage.flow-control.soft-pending-compaction-bytes-limit.
This ensures that increasing flow-control settings does not weaken
compaction acceleration, while user-configured RocksDB thresholds that
are larger than the flow-control limits are overriden, allowing compaction
speed-up to trigger before write flow control.
flow control.
3. Updated write stall check:
  - ingest_maybe_slowdown_writes now uses level0-stop-writes-trigger
instead of level0-slowdown-writes-trigger to determine whether ingest
may trigger a write stall.
  - This keeps the original behavior, since `l0-file-threshold` overrides
`level0-stop-writes-trigger`, just like the previous behavior with
`level0-slowdown-writes-trigger`. Ideally, flow-control settings would
be used directly to determine write stalls, but
`ingest_maybe_slowdown_writes` cannot access the flow-control module
configuration because this function resides inside the Engine module。

After this change, write control effectively has three stages:
1. Compaction acceleration: triggered when RocksDB thresholds are reached.
2. Slowdown writes: triggered at storage.flow-control.l0-file-threshold and
storage.flow-control.soft-pending-compaction-bytes-limit.
4. Throttle writes: triggered at storage.flow-control.hard-pending-compaction-bytes-limit. 
```

### Related changes

- [ ] PR to update `pingcap/docs`/`pingcap/docs-cn`:
        TODO
- [ ] Need to cherry-pick to the release branch

### Check List

Tests <!-- At least one of them must be included. -->

- [x] Unit test
- [ ] Integration test
- [ ] Manual test (add detailed scripts or steps below)
- [ ] No code

Side effects

- [ ] Performance regression: Consumes more CPU
- [ ] Performance regression: Consumes more Memory
- [ ] Breaking backward compatibility

### Release note
<!-- 
Compatibility change, improvement, bugfix, and new feature need a release note.

Please refer to [Release Notes Language Style Guide](https://pingcap.github.io/tidb-dev-guide/contribute-to-tidb/release-notes-style-guide.html) to write a quality release note.

If you don't think this PR needs a release note then fill it with None.
If this PR will be picked to release branch, then a release note is probably required.
-->

```release-note
When storage.flow-control.enable is set to true, we only override rocksdb.level0_slowdown_writes_trigger to l0-file-threshold only when it is larger than l0-file-threshold and only override rocksdb.soft-pend-compaction-bytes-limit to storage.flow-control.soft-pending-compaction-bytes-limit only when it is larger than storage.flow-control.soft-pending-compaction-bytes-limit.
```

title: raftstore: fix unreasonable snapshot cancel checking (#18873)
body: This is an automated cherry-pick of #18873 ### What is changed and how it works? Issue Number: Close #18872 What's Changed: ```commit-message Adjust cancel generating snapshot index from `entry_cache_compact_idx` to `raftengine_compact_index`. This prevents overly aggressive cache compaction that, after a forced GC on the leader, could continuously cancel snapshot generation. ``` As mentioned in issue #18872 , if a node is unable to synchronize logs due to a network partition, other nodes will implicitly mark it as a "down peer" (specifically, if `truncated_idx > replicated_idx`). However, the advancement of `entry_cache_compact_idx` does not take into account the `replicated_idx` of the "down peer". As other active peers continue to write and synchronize, the cache compaction idx will continue to advance, causing the `snapshot_idx` to be older than the `entry_cache_compact_idx` , resulting in frequent cancellation of snapshot generation. Therefore, we should use `raftengine_compact_index` as the checker idx, which considers the `replicated_idx `of all peers. **Before Modification** **After Modification** ### Related changes - [ ] PR to update `pingcap/docs`/`pingcap/docs-cn`: - [ ] Need to cherry-pick to the release branch ### Check List Tests - [ ] Unit test - [ ] Integration test - [x] Manual test (add detailed scripts or steps below) - [ ] No code Side effects - [ ] Performance regression: Consumes more CPU - [ ] Performance regression: Consumes more Memory - [ ] Breaking backward compatibility ### Release note ```release-note fix unreasonable snapshot cancel checking ```

author: lcwangchao

reviews.body:
## Pull request overview

This PR adds support for common handles in the index lookup executor, enabling TiKV to handle tables with composite primary keys during index scan and lookup operations. Previously, the index lookup executor only supported integer handles (single-column primary keys).

**Key Changes:**
- Introduced `CommonHandle` type alongside existing `IntHandle` to support composite primary keys
- Added `extra_common_handle_keys` field to `LazyBatchColumnVec` for passing handle data between executors
- Refactored `RowHandle::from_lazy_batch_column_vec()` to process multiple rows at once

### Reviewed changes

Copilot reviewed 9 out of 9 changed files in this pull request and generated 2 comments.

<details>
<summary>Show a summary per file</summary>

| File | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |
| components/tidb_query_datatype/src/codec/batch/lazy_column_vec.rs | Added `extra_common_handle_keys` field to store common handle keys for passing between executors |
| components/tidb_query_datatype/src/codec/table.rs | Refactored `RowHandle` trait to process batches; added `CommonHandle` implementation; renamed `encode_common_handle_for_test` to `encode_common_handle` for production use |
| components/tidb_query_executors/src/index_scan_executor.rs | Added `fill_extra_common_handle_key` parameter to populate common handle keys during index scans |
| components/tidb_query_executors/src/index_lookup_executor.rs | Refactored to use generic `Handle` type parameter and process handles in batches |
| components/tidb_query_executors/src/runner.rs | Added `is_executor_under_parent_tp()` to detect executor hierarchy; updated builder to choose between `IntHandle` and `CommonHandle` based on table schema |
| components/tidb_query_executors/src/projection_executor.rs | Added error handling to reject queries with common handle keys (not yet supported) |
| components/tidb_query_executors/src/util/top_n_heap.rs | Added logic to copy common handle keys when building
LGTM

copr: support common handle for index_lookup_executor

Signed-off-by: Chao Wang <cclcwangchao@hotmail.com>

title: raft-client: Implement health check inspection for TiKV stores (#18798)
body:
ref tikv/tikv#18797

Implement health check inspection for TiKV stores

- Added `inspect_network_interval` configuration option to `Config` struct for network latency inspection.
- Introduced `HEALTH_CHECK_DURATION_HISTOGRAM` and `HEALTH_CHECK_STATUS_COUNTER` metrics for monitoring health check durations and statuses.
- Enhanced `ConnectionPool` to manage connections with a new `ConnectionInfo` struct, allowing for channel updates and state management.
- Created `Inspector` struct to handle health check inspections for TiKV stores, including starting and stopping inspection tasks.
- Implemented health check logic in the `Inspector` to periodically check the health of each store and log results.
- Updated `RaftClient` to initialize the `Inspector` and start network inspections upon creation.
- Modified server initialization to include health check inspections for TiKV stores.

title: Unsafe Recovery: Remove a learner if it is in unsafe recovery demotion list (#18760)
body:
This is an automated cherry-pick of #18760

<!--
Thank you for contributing to TiKV!

If you haven't already, please read TiKV's [CONTRIBUTING](https://github.com/tikv/tikv/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md) document.

If you're unsure about anything, just ask; somebody should be along to answer within a day or two.

PR Title Format:
1. module [, module2, module3]: what's changed
2. *: what's changed
-->

### What is changed and how it works?
<!--
Please create an issue first to describe the problem.

There MUST be one line starting with "Issue Number:  " and 
linking the relevant issues via the "close" or "ref".

For more info, check https://github.com/tikv/tikv/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md#linking-issues.
-->

Issue Number: Close #18458

<!--
You could use "commit message" code block to add more description to the final commit message.
For more info, check https://github.com/tikv/tikv/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md#format-of-the-commit-message.
-->
What's Changed:

```commit-message
Remove a learner if it is in unsafe recovery demotion list.

If a TiFlash learner is the most up-to-date peer during unsafe recovery, it will be tombstoned, and another
peer in the group will be forced as a leader. If we don't remove TiFlash learner from leader's Raft group's
member list, the learner will be considered as "down" thus need to be replaced. However, if there is only
one TiFlash node, this replacement will stuck since PD has 0 candidate node. To resolve this, we simply need
to remove the learner from the membership list.
```

### Related changes

- [ ] PR to update `pingcap/docs`/`pingcap/docs-cn`:

title: raftstore: support network dimension for SlowScore
body:
<!--
Thank you for contributing to TiKV!

If you haven't already, please read TiKV's [CONTRIBUTING](https://github.com/tikv/tikv/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md) document.

If you're unsure about anything, just ask; somebody should be along to answer within a day or two.

### What is changed and how it works?
<!--

linking the relevant issues via the "close" or "ref".

For more info, check https://github.com/tikv/tikv/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md#linking-issues.
-->

Issue Number: Ref https://github.com/tikv/tikv/issues/18797

<!--
You could use "commit message" code block to add more description to the final commit message.
For more info, check https://github.com/tikv/tikv/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md#format-of-the-commit-message.
-->
What's Changed:

- Add `network_factors` to `UnifiedSlowScore`
- Get the duration every `inspect_network_interval` and record it in `SlowScore`
- Calculate score every `NETWORK_ROUND_TICKS` records
- Because the number of tikvs will change, the store_id in `UnifiedSlowScore` will be adjusted according to the store_id information in the obtained duration
- To simplify, we will ensure that the `last_tick_id` of different Network SlowScore are the same.

```commit-message
raftstore: support network dimension for SlowScore

title: sst_service: avoid trigger false-positive manual compact in `add_force_partition_range`
body:
<!--
Thank you for contributing to TiKV!

If you haven't already, please read TiKV's [CONTRIBUTING](https://github.com/tikv/tikv/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md) document.

If you're unsure about anything, just ask; somebody should be along to answer within a day or two.

PR Title Format:
1. module [, module2, module3]: what's changed
2. *: what's changed
-->

### What is changed and how it works?
<!--
Please create an issue first to describe the problem.

There MUST be one line starting with "Issue Number:  " and 
linking the relevant issues via the "close" or "ref".

For more info, check https://github.com/tikv/tikv/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md#linking-issues.
-->

Issue Number: Close #19120, Close #19106, close pingcap/tidb#64488

<!--
You could use "commit message" code block to add more description to the final commit message.
For more info, check https://github.com/tikv/tikv/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md#format-of-the-commit-message.
-->
What's Changed:

NOTE: this PR also close #19106 as it includes the change in (https://github.com/tikv/rocksdb/pull/424)

```commit-message
This PR include the new change in tikv/rocksdb#421 that introduce a new config `check_range_overlap_on_bottom_level`. By set this config to true, the manual compaction while use range overlap instead of key overlap on the bottom level for selecting candidate SST files. This can ensure the manual compact can split large range SST files at the bottom level which blocks SST ingesting to the bottom level.
```

### Related changes

- [ ] PR to update `pingcap/docs`/`pingcap/docs-cn`:
- [ ] Need to cherry-pick to the release branch

### Check List

Tests <!-- At least one of them must be included. -->

- [x] Unit test
- [ ] Integration test
- [ ] Manual test (add detailed scripts or steps below)
  - We have test this change in 2 internal test cluster, before this change, when running DDL with other online traffic the `add_force_partition_range` can easily trigger long run compactions; After this change, there are not compaction triggered anymore except the first call.
- [ ] No code

Side effects

- [ ] Performance regression: Consumes more CPU
- [ ] Performance regression: Consumes more Memory
- [ ] Breaking backward compatibility

### Release note
<!-- 
Compatibility change, improvement, bugfix, and new feature need a release note.

Please refer to [Release Notes Language Style Guide](https://pingcap.github.io/tidb-dev-guide/contribute-to-tidb/release-notes-style-guide.html) to write a quality release note.

If you don't think this PR needs a release note then fill it with None.
If this PR will be picked to release branch, then a release note is probably required.
-->

```release-note
Fix the panic caused by rocksdb's memory table data race in ARM arch environemnt.
```

<!--

Issue Number: Close #19120, Close #19106

<!--
You could use "commit message" code block to add more description to the final commit message.
For more info, check https://github.com/tikv/tikv/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md#format-of-the-commit-message.
-->
What's Changed:

NOTE: this PR also close #19106 as it includes the change in (https://github.com/tikv/rocksdb/pull/424)

```commit-message
This PR include the new change in tikv/rocksdb#421 that introduce a new config `check_range_overlap_on_bottom_level`. By set this config to true, the manual compaction while use range overlap instead of key overlap on the bottom level for selecting candidate SST files. This can ensure the manual compact can split large range SST files at the bottom level which blocks SST ingesting to the bottom level.
```

### Related changes

- [ ] PR to update `pingcap/docs`/`pingcap/docs-cn`:
- [ ] Need to cherry-pick to the release branch

### Check List

Tests <!-- At least one of them must be included. -->

- [x] Unit test
- [ ] Integration test
- [ ] Manual test (add detailed scripts or steps below)
  - We have test this change in 2 internal test cluster, before this change, when running DDL with other online traffic the `add_force_partition_range` can easily trigger long run compactions; After this change, there are not compaction triggered anymore except the first call.
- [ ] No code

Side effects

- [ ] Performance regression: Consumes more Memory
- [ ] Breaking backward compatibility

title: raftstore: fix unreasonable snapshot cancel checking
body: ### What is changed and how it works? Issue Number: Close #18872 What's Changed: ```commit-message Adjust cancel generating snapshot index from `entry_cache_compact_idx` to `raftengine_compact_index`. This prevents overly aggressive cache compaction that, after a forced GC on the leader, could continuously cancel snapshot generation. ``` As mentioned in issue #18872 , if a node is unable to synchronize logs due to a network partition, other nodes will implicitly mark it as a "down peer" (specifically, if `truncated_idx > replicated_idx`). However, the advancement of `entry_cache_compact_idx` does not take into account the `replicated_idx` of the "down peer". As other active peers continue to write and synchronize, the cache compaction idx will continue to advance, causing the `snapshot_idx` to be older than the `entry_cache_compact_idx` , resulting in frequent cancellation of snapshot generation. Therefore, we should use `raftengine_compact_index` as the checker idx, which considers the `replicated_idx `of all peers. **Before Modification** **After Modification** ### Related changes - [ ] PR to update `pingcap/docs`/`pingcap/docs-cn`: - [ ] Need to cherry-pick to the release branch ### Check List Tests - [ ] Unit test - [ ] Integration test - [x] Manual test (add detailed scripts or steps below) - [ ] No code Side effects - [ ] Performance regression: Consumes more CPU - [ ] Performance regression: Consumes more Memory - [ ] Breaking backward compatibility ### Release note ```release-note fix unreasonable snapshot cancel checking ```
author: exit-code-1

reviews.body: Overall LGTM

So, before this PR, we cancel snapshot generation if snapshot's idx is older than "live peers' replication idx", so that some snapshots that are generated for "down peers" are mistakenly cancelled. Do I understand it correctly? Can you give some examples in that PR description? Overall LGTM

title: copr: improve like perf
body:
<!--
Thank you for contributing to TiKV!

If you haven't already, please read TiKV's [CONTRIBUTING](https://github.com/tikv/tikv/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md) document.

If you're unsure about anything, just ask; somebody should be along to answer within a day or two.

PR Title Format:

2. *: what's changed
-->

### What is changed and how it works?
<!--
Please create an issue first to describe the problem.

There MUST be one line starting with "Issue Number:  " and 
linking the relevant issues via the "close" or "ref".

For more info, check https://github.com/tikv/tikv/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md#linking-issues.
-->

Issue Number: Close #19037 

#### Key Optimizations  
##### Optimize Backtracking Logic for Patterns Starting with "%"  
When a pattern starts with `%`, restart backtracking directly from the characters after `%` (instead of restarting from the `%` itself). 
#### Benchmark 
To eliminate interference and facilitate comparison, single concurrency was configured with the following setting:
`set tidb_distsql_scan_concurrency=1;`
For the query (under TPCH 10G data, tested on a MacBook Pro with an M2 Pro CPU):
`SELECT COUNT(*) FROM orders WHERE o_comment LIKE ?;`
The comparison of execution time before and after optimization is as follows (unit: seconds):
| PATTERN | Collation | Before Optimization | After Optimization | Percentage Reduction |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `"%中国%"` | utf8mb4_0900_ai_ci | 4.0 | 3.7 | 8% |
| `"%中国%"` | utf8mb4_bin | 2.5 | 2.2 | 12% |
| `"%special%requests%" (From TPCH Q13)` | utf8mb4_0900_ai_ci | 3.6 | 3.4 | 6% |
| `"%special%requests%" (From TPCH Q13)` | utf8mb4_bin | 2.6 | 2.3 | 12% |

title: Raftstore: Close some background schedulers before shutting down
body:
<!--
Thank you for contributing to TiKV!

If you haven't already, please read TiKV's [CONTRIBUTING](https://github.com/tikv/tikv/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md) document.

If you're unsure about anything, just ask; somebody should be along to answer within a day or two.

PR Title Format:
1. module [, module2, module3]: what's changed
2. *: what's changed
-->

### What is changed and how it works?
<!--
Please create an issue first to describe the problem.

There MUST be one line starting with "Issue Number:  " and 
linking the relevant issues via the "close" or "ref".

For more info, check https://github.com/tikv/tikv/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md#linking-issues.
-->

Issue Number: Ref #18434

<!--
You could use "commit message" code block to add more description to the final commit message.
For more info, check https://github.com/tikv/tikv/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md#format-of-the-commit-message.
-->
What's Changed:

```commit-message
Close some background schedulers before shutting down.

We need to restart both the KV engine and the Raft engine in a test to set some non-online configs.
e.g. turning off Titan.
These background workers have references to either the KV engine or the Raft engine, and they are also
self-referencing, causing the KV engine and Raft engine never get closed on shutting down. We need this
to interrupt the infinite loop, and release the DBs' references.

The best way to resolve this would be using tokio's unbound channel which allows downgrade to weak ptr.
However, TiKV just has too many Arc circular dependencies, it is nearly impossible to detangle it.
```

### Related changes

- [ ] PR to update `pingcap/docs`/`pingcap/docs-cn`:
- [ ] Need to cherry-pick to the release branch

title: raft-client: Implement health check inspection for TiKV stores (#18798)
body:
This is an automated cherry-pick of #18798 ### What is changed and how it works? Issue Number: Ref https://github.com/tikv/tikv/issues/18797 What's Changed: ```commit-message Implement health check inspection for TiKV stores - Added `inspect_network_interval` configuration option to `Config` struct for network latency inspection. - Introduced `HEALTH_CHECK_DURATION_HISTOGRAM` and `HEALTH_CHECK_STATUS_COUNTER` metrics for monitoring health check durations and statuses. - Enhanced `ConnectionPool` to manage connections with a new `ConnectionInfo` struct, allowing for channel updates and state management. - Created `Inspector` struct to handle health check inspections for TiKV stores, including starting and stopping inspection tasks. - Implemented health check logic in the `Inspector` to periodically check the health of each store and log results. - Updated `RaftClient` to initialize the `Inspector` and start network inspections upon creation. - Modified server initialization to include health check inspections for TiKV stores. ``` The setting of dynamically modifying configuration will be in the next pr ### Related changes - [ ] PR to update `pingcap/docs`/`pingcap/docs-cn`: - [ ] Need to cherry-pick to the release branch ### Check List Tests - [x] Unit test - [x] Integration test - [x] Manual test (add detailed scripts or steps below) performance test The following data is obtained after 5 performance tests(3 tikv 3 times, 10 tikv 2 times). title: raftstore: enhance the detection to cover I/O jitters on kvdb. (#18439)
body:
This is an automated cherry-pick of #18439

<!--
Thank you for contributing to TiKV!

If you haven't already, please read TiKV's [CONTRIBUTING](https://github.com/tikv/tikv/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md) document.

If you're unsure about anything, just ask; somebody should be along to answer within a day or two.

PR Title Format:
1. module [, module2, module3]: what's changed
2. *: what's changed
-->

### What is changed and how it works?
<!--
Please create an issue first to describe the problem.

There MUST be one line starting with "Issue Number:  " and 
linking the relevant issues via the "close" or "ref".

For more info, check https://github.com/tikv/tikv/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md#linking-issues.
-->

Issue Number: Close https://github.com/tikv/tikv/issues/18463

<!--
You could use "commit message" code block to add more description to the final commit message.
For more info, check https://github.com/tikv/tikv/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md#format-of-the-commit-message.
-->
What's Changed:

In previous work https://github.com/tikv/tikv/pull/17801, TiKV has introduced the detection mechanism for kvdb disk to detect I/O hang issues. 

However, recent customer feedback highlighted the need to extend detection coverage to I/O jitters, ensuring TiKV can automatically recover from abnormal states caused by KVDB I/O jitters.

Therefore, this ticket is built to tracks the development efforts to enhance TiKV’s I/O jitter detection and recovery mechanism. And the majority parts of this change are listed as followings show:
- for configurations:
  - `raftstore.inspect_kvdb_interval`: 2s -> 100ms
- for detection mechanism on kvdb:
  - `SlowScore::ratio_thresh`：60% -> 10%

```commit-message
Enhances the detection mechanism to cover the I/O jitters on kvdb disk if deploys with separated mount paths.
```
