title: cdc: adjust assertion when merge locks (#19047)
body:
This is an automated cherry-pick of #19047

<!--
Thank you for contributing to TiKV!

If you haven't already, please read TiKV's [CONTRIBUTING](https://github.com/tikv/tikv/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md) document.

If you're unsure about anything, just ask; somebody should be along to answer within a day or two.

PR Title Format:
1. module [, module2, module3]: what's changed
2. *: what's changed
-->

### What is changed and how it works?
<!--
Please create an issue first to describe the problem.

There MUST be one line starting with "Issue Number:  " and 
linking the relevant issues via the "close" or "ref".

For more info, check https://github.com/tikv/tikv/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md#linking-issues.
-->

Issue Number: Close #19048 

<!--
You could use "commit message" code block to add more description to the final commit message.
For more info, check https://github.com/tikv/tikv/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md#format-of-the-commit-message.
-->
What's Changed:

```commit-message
fix potential panic which may happen when subscribe the region and meet rollback and prewrite entry
```

Similar to the #19025, but the region is subscribed after the rollback prewrite entry stored into the disk and before the prewrite the same key.
```

   → CDC calls push_lock for T2

The bug may occurs when:
1. T1 prewrites and commits key K (start_ts=100, commit_ts=110)
    → CF_WRITE[K@110] contains T1's committed write

2. T2 prewrites key K as a SECONDARY key (start_ts=111)
   → CF_LOCK[K] contains T2's lock

3. cdc subscribe the region
   → CF_LOCK[K@111] scanned out when doing incremental scan by the `scan_locks_from_storage`
   → CDC incremental scan finished and set to the resolver by the `finish_scan_locks`

4. T2 rolls back key K
   → Finds overlapped_write from T1 at commit_ts=110
   → Since K is NOT T2's primary: protected=false
   → make_rollback() returns None (no CF_WRITE entry!)
   → Only deletes from CF_LOCK
   → CDC ignores the DELETE operation

5. T3 prewrites key K
   → Triggers resolve_lock which removes T2's stale lock from CF_LOCK
   → No CDC notification

6. CDC processes T3's prewrite
   → Calls push_lock for T3
   → Finds T2's stale lock still in lock_tracker
   → Assertion fails: different start_ts
```

### Related changes

- [ ] PR to update `pingcap/docs`/`pingcap/docs-cn`:
- [ ] Need to cherry-pick to the release branch

Tests <!-- At least one of them must be included. -->

- [ ] Unit test
- [ ] Integration test
- [ ] Manual test (add detailed scripts or steps below)
- [ ] No code

Side effects

- [ ] Performance regression: Consumes more CPU
- [ ] Performance regression: Consumes more Memory
- [ ] Breaking backward compatibility

Please refer to [Release Notes Language Style Guide](https://pingcap.github.io/tidb-dev-guide/contribute-to-tidb/release-notes-style-guide.html) to write a quality release note.

title: cdc: fixing a TiKV assertion panic that can happen when CDC is enabled (#19025)
body:
This is an automated cherry-pick of #19025

Issue Number: Close #18498 

What's Changed:

```
This assertion failure happens due to a discrepancy between Raft engine state machine and how CDC is processing Raft commands. When there are overlapped writes with rollbacks which happens between primary and secondary keys, CDC ended-up having duplicate locks in its lock tracker when there isn't a PUT command on the WRITE_CF to release the lock.
```

```
The bug occurs when:
1. T1 prewrites and commits key K (start_ts=100, commit_ts=110)
    → CF_WRITE[K@110] contains T1's committed write
2. T2 prewrites key K as a SECONDARY key (start_ts=111)
   → CF_LOCK[K] contains T2's lock
   → CDC calls push_lock for T2
3. T2 rolls back key K
   → Finds overlapped_write from T1 at commit_ts=110
   → Since K is NOT T2's primary: protected=false
   → make_rollback() returns None (no CF_WRITE entry!)
   → Only deletes from CF_LOCK
   → CDC ignores the DELETE operation
4. T3 prewrites key K
   → Triggers resolve_lock which removes T2's stale lock from CF_LOCK
   → No CDC notification
5. CDC processes T3's prewrite
   → Calls push_lock for T3
   → Finds T2's stale lock still in lock_tracker
   → Assertion fails: different start_ts
```

### Related changes

- [ ] PR to update `pingcap/docs`/`pingcap/docs-cn`:
- [x] Need to cherry-pick to the release branch

### Check List

Tests

- [ ] Unit test
- [x] Integration test
- [x] Manual test (add detailed scripts or steps below)
- [ ] No code

Following is a load test that encountered this issue, still non-deterministic, but happened often enough to verify.

```
SHOW CREATE TABLE test.mockBootstrap;
+---------------+-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
| Table         | Create Table                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                    |
+---------------+-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
| mockBootstrap | CREATE TABLE `mockBootstrap` (
  `id` varchar(150) NOT NULL,
  `strCol` varchar(150) DEFAULT NULL,
  `dateCol` bigint DEFAULT NULL,
  PRIMARY KEY (`id`) /*T![clustered_index] CLUSTERED */,
  KEY `strCol` (`strCol`),
  KEY `dateCol` (`dateCol`)
) ENGINE=InnoDB DEFAULT CHARSET=utf8mb4 COLLATE=utf8mb4_bin |
+---------------+-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
```

The load was generated using the [k6 framework](https://grafana.com/docs/loki/latest/send-data/k6/), with the following code (rest of the code is omitted for brevity). The key to hitting this was very high frequency inserts + secondary indexes, which caused overlapping writes with rollbacks.

```
export default function() {
    const timestamp = Date.now();
    const id = `urn:li:bootstrap:${Date.now()}`;
    const randomString = getRandomString(8);

    const sql = `INSERT INTO mockBootstrap (id, strCol, dateCol) `
        + `values (?, ?, ?) `
        + `ON DUPLICATE KEY UPDATE id = ?, strCol = ?, dateCol = ?`;

    db_config.executeSql(sql, [id, randomString, timestamp, id, randomString, timestamp]);
}
```
Side effects

- [ ] Performance regression: Consumes more CPU
- [ ] Performance regression: Consumes more Memory
- [ ] Breaking backward compatibility

### Release note

```release-note
None
```

body:
Issue Number: Close #18498 

What's Changed:

```

When there are overlapped writes with rollbacks which happens between primary and secondary keys, CDC ended-up having duplicate locks in its lock tracker when there isn't a PUT command on the WRITE_CF to release the lock.
```

```
The bug occurs when:
1. T1 prewrites and commits key K (start_ts=100, commit_ts=110)
```

title: Unsafe Recovery: Remove a learner if it is in unsafe recovery demotion list
body:
<!--
Thank you for contributing to TiKV!

If you haven't already, please read TiKV's [CONTRIBUTING](https://github.com/tikv/tikv/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md) document.

If you're unsure about anything, just ask; somebody should be along to answer within a day or two.

PR Title Format:
1. module [, module2, module3]: what's changed
2. *: what's changed
-->

### What is changed and how it works?
<!--
Please create an issue first to describe the problem.

There MUST be one line starting with "Issue Number:  " and 
linking the relevant issues via the "close" or "ref".

For more info, check https://github.com/tikv/tikv/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md#linking-issues.
-->

Issue Number: Close #18458

<!--
You could use "commit message" code block to add more description to the final commit message.
For more info, check https://github.com/tikv/tikv/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md#format-of-the-commit-message.
-->
What's Changed:

```commit-message
Remove a learner if it is in unsafe recovery demotion list.

If a TiFlash learner is the most up-to-date peer during unsafe recovery, it will be tombstoned, and another
peer in the group will be forced as a leader. If we don't remove TiFlash learner from leader's Raft group's
member list, the learner will be considered as "down" thus need to be replaced. However, if there is only
one TiFlash node, this replacement will stuck since PD has 0 candidate node. To resolve this, we simply need
to remove the learner from the membership list.
```

### Related changes

- [ ] PR to update `pingcap/docs`/`pingcap/docs-cn`:
- [ ] Need to cherry-pick to the release branch

-->

```commit-message
The default value of config `rocksdb.max-manifest-file-size` is too small for large clusters with more than 100k sst files. It can cause long ingest latency when the effective manifest file size exceed the threshold. So this PR adjust the default value to 256MiB which should be a large enough value.
```

title: config: make default settings for gRPC adaptive to CPU quota. (#18735)
body:
This is an automated cherry-pick of #18735

<!--
Thank you for contributing to TiKV!

If you haven't already, please read TiKV's [CONTRIBUTING](https://github.com/tikv/tikv/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md) document.

If you're unsure about anything, just ask; somebody should be along to answer within a day or two.

PR Title Format:

-->

### What is changed and how it works?
<!--
Please create an issue first to describe the problem.

There MUST be one line starting with "Issue Number:  " and 
linking the relevant issues via the "close" or "ref".

For more info, check https://github.com/tikv/tikv/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md#linking-issues.
-->

Issue Number: ref https://github.com/tikv/tikv/issues/18613

<!--
You could use "commit message" code block to add more description to the final commit message.
For more info, check https://github.com/tikv/tikv/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md#format-of-the-commit-message.
-->
What's Changed:

This PR makes default gRPC settings adaptive to CPU quota instead of using fixed values. The change improves resource utilization by scaling gRPC connection numbers and concurrency based on available CPU resources.
- Updates `grpc_raft_conn_num` to scale with CPU quota (CPU cores / 8, clamped between 1-4)
- Updates `grpc_concurrency` to be calculated as `grpc_raft_conn_num` * 3 + 2

With this change, the default setting for common envs can be reviewed as following lists:
| CPU Quota | grpc_raft_conn_num | grpc_concurrency |
| --- | --- | --- |
| 8c | 1 (same with nightly) | 5 (same with nightly) |
| 16c | 2 | 8 |
| >= 32c | 4 (limited to 4) | 14 (limited to 14) | 

```commit-message
Make the default value of relevant gRPC thread num adaptive to CPU quota.
```

title: log-backup: Keep the order of observation IDs consistent with the order in which they were received (#18290)
body:
This is an automated cherry-pick of #18290

<!--
Thank you for contributing to TiKV!

If you haven't already, please read TiKV's [CONTRIBUTING](https://github.com/tikv/tikv/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md) document.

If you're unsure about anything, just ask; somebody should be along to answer within a day or two.

PR Title Format:
1. module [, module2, module3]: what's changed
2. *: what's changed
-->

### What is changed and how it works?
<!--
Please create an issue first to describe the problem.

There MUST be one line starting with "Issue Number:  " and 
linking the relevant issues via the "close" or "ref".

For more info, check https://github.com/tikv/tikv/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md#linking-issues.
-->

Issue Number: Close #18243

<!--
You could use "commit message" code block to add more description to the final commit message.
For more info, check https://github.com/tikv/tikv/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md#format-of-the-commit-message.
-->
What's Changed:

```commit-message
Keep the order of observation IDs consistent with the order in which they were received
```

### Additional notes

There are always three observe operations (Stop[Pre-Candidate], Stop[Candidate] and Start[Leader]) generated when any peer becomes leader. But the observe operation `Start[Leader]` may lost due to no task registered yet. Besides, when a log backup task is being registered, the endpoint will send a observe operation `Start[Scanned]` for a leader.

Case 1: If the observe operation `Start[Leader]` is ignored because the task is not registered yet. 
We can make sure the endpoint must get the region when a new task is being registered. We have the following execution order:
```
1. RaftStoreEvent::RoleChange -> region_info_accessor.scheduler
2. Start[Leader] -> backup_stream::Endpoint.scheduler [IGNORED]
3. register task ranges so that any observe operation won't be ignored
4. RegionInfoQuery::SeekRegion -> region_info_accessor.scheduler
```
In this case, the step 2 is already done, so we can make sure that the region update query is already in the queue of `region_info_accessor.scheduler` when the endpoint sends `RegionInfoQuery::SeekRegion` to the `region_info_accessor`. Therefore, the endpoint can get the region from `seek_region`.

Case2: If the endpoint can not get the region from `seek_region`.
We can make sure the observe operation `Start[Leader]` is not ignored. We have the following execution order:
```
1. register task ranges so that any observe operation won't be ignored
2. RegionInfoQuery::SeekRegion -> region_info_accessor.scheduler
3. RaftStoreEvent::RoleChange -> region_info_accessor.scheduler
4. Start[Leader] -> backup_stream::Endpoint.scheduler
```
In this case, the step 2 is already done, so we can make sure that the task range is registered. Therefore, the step 4 is not ignored and the observe operation `Start[Leader]` is scheduled.

In summary, the `region_operator` may meet the `Start[Scanned] -> Stop[Pre-Candidate] -> Stop[Candidate] -> Start[Leader]` and repeat scanning the region. But it won't lost the region if it is leader.

title: raftstore: Remove redundant initial Raft state write for split regions
body:
<!--
Thank you for contributing to TiKV!

If you haven't already, please read TiKV's [CONTRIBUTING](https://github.com/tikv/tikv/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md) document.

If you're unsure about anything, just ask; somebody should be along to answer within a day or two.

PR Title Format:
1. module [, module2, module3]: what's changed
2. *: what's changed
-->

### What is changed and how it works?
<!--
Please create an issue first to describe the problem.

There MUST be one line starting with "Issue Number:  " and 
linking the relevant issues via the "close" or "ref".

For more info, check https://github.com/tikv/tikv/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md#linking-issues.
-->

Issue Number: ref #18256

<!--
You could use "commit message" code block to add more description to the final commit message.
For more info, check https://github.com/tikv/tikv/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md#format-of-the-commit-message.
-->
What's Changed:

```commit-message
When creating new regions via split, writing an initial RaftLocalState
is unnecessary. Regardless of whether this state is persisted, callers
always observe the fixed initial state (last_index = 5, term = 5, commit = 5).
The RaftLocalState will be updated and persisted on subsequent raft log
appends, commit index advances, votes, or elections.

This change is safe under various scenarios:

1. On restart after calling init_raft_state, recovery is correct as the
   initial state is fixed.
2. If a log is appended, raft state and log are persisted atomically
   within the same write batch.
3. Peer removal via applying snapshot deletes raft state directly;
   reads are not performed.
4. Region splits or merges require prior commit, with raft state already
   updated and persisted.

Removing this redundant write improves tail latency without impacting
correctness.
```

### Check List

Tests <!-- At least one of them must be included. -->

- [ ] Integration test

### Release note
<!-- 
Compatibility change, improvement, bugfix, and new feature need a release note.

Please refer to [Release Notes Language Style Guide](https://pingcap.github.io/tidb-dev-guide/contribute-to-tidb/release-notes-style-guide.html) to write a quality release note.

If you don't think this PR needs a release note then fill it with None.
If this PR will be picked to release branch, then a release note is probably required.
-->

```release-note
None
```
author: overvenus

reviews.body:
## Pull Request Overview

This PR removes redundant initial Raft state writes when creating new regions through split operations. The change eliminates unnecessary persistence of initial RaftLocalState since callers always observe the fixed initial state values (last_index = 5, term = 5, commit = 5) regardless of persistence, and the state will be updated and persisted during subsequent Raft operations.

- Removes the write operation and associated metrics collection from `init_raft_state` function
- Adds comprehensive integration tests to verify recovery scenarios work correctly without the initial write
- Removes the unused metrics histogram for peer creation Raft write duration

### Reviewed Changes

Copilot reviewed 3 out of 3 changed files in this pull request and generated 2 comments.

| File | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |
| components/raftstore/src/store/peer_storage.rs | Removes redundant initial Raft state persistence and metrics collection |
| components/raftstore/src/store/metrics.rs | Removes unused peer creation Raft write duration histogram |
| tests/integrations/raftstore/test_split_region.rs | Adds integration tests verifying Raft state recovery scenarios |


<details>
<summary>Comments suppressed due to low confidence (1)</summary>

**tests/integrations/raftstore/test_split_region.rs:1559**
* The filter logic is inverted from what the comment suggests. Either the comment should be 'Allow only prevote messages' or the condition should be `msg_type == MessageType::MsgRequestPreVote`
```
            msg_type != MessageType::MsgRequestPreVote
```
</details>

commits.message:
raftstore: Remove redundant initial Raft state write for split regions

When creating new regions via split, writing an initial RaftLocalState
is unnecessary. Regardless of whether this state is persisted, callers
always observe the fixed initial state (last_index = 5, term = 5, commit = 5).
The RaftLocalState will be updated and persisted on subsequent raft log
appends, commit index advances, votes, or elections.

This change is safe under various scenarios:

1. On restart after calling init_raft_state, recovery is correct as the
   initial state is fixed.
2. If a log is appended, raft state and log are persisted atomically
   within the same write batch.
3. Peer removal via applying snapshot deletes raft state directly;
   reads are not performed.
4. Region splits or merges require prior commit, with raft state already
   updated and persisted.

Removing this redundant write improves tail latency without impacting
correctness.

Signed-off-by: Neil Shen <overvenus@gmail.com>

Add two tests

Signed-off-by: Neil Shen <overvenus@gmail.com>

title: titan: Estimate raw blob size based on Titan metrics (#18628)
body:
This is an automated cherry-pick of #18628

<!--
Thank you for contributing to TiKV!

If you haven't already, please read TiKV's [CONTRIBUTING](https://github.com/tikv/tikv/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md) document.

If you're unsure about anything, just ask; somebody should be along to answer within a day or two.

PR Title Format:
1. module [, module2, module3]: what's changed
2. *: what's changed
-->

### What is changed and how it works?
<!--
Please create an issue first to describe the problem.

There MUST be one line starting with "Issue Number:  " and 
linking the relevant issues via the "close" or "ref".

For more info, check https://github.com/tikv/tikv/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md#linking-issues.
-->

Issue Number: Close #18600

<!--
You could use "commit message" code block to add more description to the final commit message.
For more info, check https://github.com/tikv/tikv/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md#format-of-the-commit-message.
-->
What's Changed:

```commit-message
This PR introduces a mechanism to estimate Titan blob raw size based on
metrics collected from Titan during flush. 

A sliding window of recent (raw, compressed) size samples is used to 
compute an average expansion factor. This factor is then used to 
estimate the raw size of a blob when building SST table properties. 
This approach assumes that the compression ratio remains relatively 
stable for the workload.

Apparently this is not a perfect solution, but a workaround. A more 
direct solution—recording the uncompressed size in the blob index 
format—was previously proposed[1]. However, changing the data format 
would break backward compatibility and prevent rollbacks, which can be 
risky.

[1] https://github.com/tikv/titan/pull/334#issuecomment-3026813148
```

title: GC: Estimate compaction effectiveness based on stats and GC safe point (#18670)
body:
This is an automated cherry-pick of #18670

<!--
Thank you for contributing to TiKV!

If you haven't already, please read TiKV's [CONTRIBUTING](https://github.com/tikv/tikv/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md) document.

If you're unsure about anything, just ask; somebody should be along to answer within a day or two.

PR Title Format:
1. module [, module2, module3]: what's changed
2. *: what's changed
-->

### What is changed and how it works?
<!--
Please create an issue first to describe the problem.

There MUST be one line starting with "Issue Number:  " and 
linking the relevant issues via the "close" or "ref".

For more info, check https://github.com/tikv/tikv/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md#linking-issues.
-->

Issue Number: Close #18697

<!--
You could use "commit message" code block to add more description to the final commit message.
For more info, check https://github.com/tikv/tikv/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md#format-of-the-commit-message.
-->
What's Changed:

```commit-message
Add stale versions' newest ts and oldest ts as SST properties.
  - oldest_stale_versions_ts, newest_stale_versions_ts for estimate number of MVCC version.
    imagine some rows are updated.
  - oldest_delete_ts, newest_delete_ts for estimate number of TiKV MVCC deletes.
    imagine some rows are deleted, thus can be physically deleted once past GC safe point
  - I introduced 4 (2 by 2) new stats, because deletes and stale versions are different "dimensions"
  - This is backward compatible. As stale_version_ts, delete_ts will be instantiated with (all entries') min_ts and max_ts if not set in the  SST files created by older TiKV versions.
Estimate num discardable versions based on them and GC safe point, assuming uniform distribution.

An example, assuming:
- Each row got inserted once, updated once, and delete once
- Each operation is 10 secs apart

We can have:
[{r1_t01, v1}, {r1_t11, v2}, {r1_t21, del}, {r2_t02, v1}, {r1_t12, v2}, {r1_t22, del}, {r3_t03, v1}, {r3_t13, v2}, {r1_t23, del}]
oldest_stale_versions_ts: t01, newest_stale_version_ts: t13
oldest_delete_ts: t21, newest_delete_ts: t23
If GC safe point is t22, we know number of discardable stale versions is 2 * 3, while number of discardable deletes is just 1
```

### Related changes

- [ ] PR to update `pingcap/docs`/`pingcap/docs-cn`:
- [x] Need to cherry-pick to the release branch

- [ ] Manual test (add detailed scripts or steps below)
- [ ] No code

- [ ] Performance regression: Consumes more Memory
- [ ] Breaking backward compatibility

Please refer to [Release Notes Language Style Guide](https://pingcap.github.io/tidb-dev-guide/contribute-to-tidb/release-notes-style-guide.html) to write a quality release note.

If you don't think this PR needs a release note then fill it with None.
If this PR will be picked to release branch, then a release note is probably required.
-->


    imagine some rows are updated.
  - oldest_delete_ts, newest_delete_ts for estimate number of TiKV MVCC deletes.
    imagine some rows are deleted, thus can be physically deleted once past GC safe point
  - I introduced 4 (2 by 2) new stats, because deletes and stale versions are different "dimensions"
  - This is backward compatible. As stale_version_ts, delete_ts will be instantiated with (all entries') min_ts and max_ts if not set in the  SST files created by older TiKV versions.
Estimate num discardable versions based on them and GC safe point, assuming uniform distribution.

An example, assuming:

We can have:
[{r1_t01, v1}, {r1_t11, v2}, {r1_t21, del}, {r2_t02, v1}, {r1_t12, v2}, {r1_t22, del}, {r3_t03, v1}, {r3_t13, v2}, {r1_t23, del}]

title: GC: remove compact on split (#18500)
body:
This is an automated cherry-pick of #18500

<!--
Thank you for contributing to TiKV!

If you haven't already, please read TiKV's [CONTRIBUTING](https://github.com/tikv/tikv/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md) document.

If you're unsure about anything, just ask; somebody should be along to answer within a day or two.

PR Title Format:
1. module [, module2, module3]: what's changed
2. *: what's changed
-->

### What is changed and how it works?
<!--
Please create an issue first to describe the problem.

There MUST be one line starting with "Issue Number:  " and 
linking the relevant issues via the "close" or "ref".

For more info, check https://github.com/tikv/tikv/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md#linking-issues.
-->

Issue Number: Close #18451

<!--
You could use "commit message" code block to add more description to the final commit message.
For more info, check https://github.com/tikv/tikv/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md#format-of-the-commit-message.
-->
What's Changed:

```commit-message
Remove compaction scheduling on split key not found error. 

Split key not found error is usually returned when a region only has a few keys but a large number of stale MVCC versions.
This was the incentive of scheduling a compaction on split error. However, this solution brings a more serious problem, i.e. worker threads starvation.
We don't really need this extra compaction, we expect that, after the compaction optimization is done, this MVCC accumulation issue would be mitigated. So, once the "split key not found" occurs, we just rely on the CheckThenCompact to resolve it. 
```

### Related changes

- [ ] PR to update `pingcap/docs`/`pingcap/docs-cn`:
- [ ] Need to cherry-pick to the release branch

### Check List

commits.message:
GC: remove compact on split (#18500)

close tikv/tikv#18451

Remove compaction scheduling on split key not found error.

Split key not found error is usually returned when a region only has a few keys but a large number of stale MVCC versions.
This was the incentive of scheduling a compaction on split error. However, this solution brings a more serious problem, i.e. worker threads starvation.
We don't really need this extra compaction, we expect that, after the compaction optimization is done, this MVCC accumulation issue would be mitigated. So, once the "split key not found" occurs, we just rely on the CheckThenCompact to resolve it.

Signed-off-by: Yang Zhang <yang.zhang@pingcap.com>

title: raftstore: move the handling of `CompactedEvent` to split-check worker. (#18565)
body:
This is an automated cherry-pick of #18565

<!--
Thank you for contributing to TiKV!

If you haven't already, please read TiKV's [CONTRIBUTING](https://github.com/tikv/tikv/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md) document.

If you're unsure about anything, just ask; somebody should be along to answer within a day or two.

PR Title Format:
1. module [, module2, module3]: what's changed
2. *: what's changed
-->

### What is changed and how it works?
<!--
Please create an issue first to describe the problem.

There MUST be one line starting with "Issue Number:  " and 
linking the relevant issues via the "close" or "ref".

For more info, check https://github.com/tikv/tikv/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md#linking-issues.
-->

Issue Number: Close https://github.com/tikv/tikv/issues/18532

<!--
You could use "commit message" code block to add more description to the final commit message.
For more info, check https://github.com/tikv/tikv/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md#format-of-the-commit-message.
-->
What's Changed:

As the issue https://github.com/tikv/tikv/issues/18532 mentioned, this performance bottleneck occurs when processing `StoreMsg::CompactedEvent` after RocksDB compactions, where updating region sizes in `store.meta` requires a global lock and linear traversal of all regions. 

In the current implementation, the most time-cosumping part is `calc_ranges_declined_bytes` operation, which scales poorly with high region counts, causing significant Raftstore stalls during the mandatory post-compaction updates.
![image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/bdc0a808-2bf4-4fe6-a117-ec884087ae5b)

This PR moves the handling of `StoreMsg::CompactedEvent` out of Raftstore's `StoreMsg` processing by converting it to a `SplitCheckTask::CompactedEvent` and offloading it to the `split-check` worker. This change delegates the compaction event processing to the dedicated `split-check` worker thread.

```commit-message
Optimize the handling of `CompactedEvent` in raftstore by moving it to `split-check` worker.
```

title: titan: Estimate raw blob size based on Titan metrics
body:
<!--
Thank you for contributing to TiKV!

If you haven't already, please read TiKV's [CONTRIBUTING](https://github.com/tikv/tikv/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md) document.

If you're unsure about anything, just ask; somebody should be along to answer within a day or two.

PR Title Format:

2. *: what's changed
-->

### What is changed and how it works?
<!--
Please create an issue first to describe the problem.

There MUST be one line starting with "Issue Number:  " and 
linking the relevant issues via the "close" or "ref".

For more info, check https://github.com/tikv/tikv/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md#linking-issues.
-->

Issue Number: Close #18600

<!--
You could use "commit message" code block to add more description to the final commit message.
For more info, check https://github.com/tikv/tikv/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md#format-of-the-commit-message.
-->
What's Changed:

```commit-message
This PR introduces a mechanism to estimate Titan blob raw size based on
metrics collected from Titan during flush. 

A sliding window of recent (raw, compressed) size samples is used to 
compute an average expansion factor. This factor is then used to 
estimate the raw size of a blob when building SST table properties. 
This approach assumes that the compression ratio remains relatively 
stable for the workload.

Apparently this is not a perfect solution, but a workaround. A more 
direct solution—recording the uncompressed size in the blob index 
format—was previously proposed[1]. However, changing the data format 
would break backward compatibility and prevent rollbacks, which can be 
risky.

[1] https://github.com/tikv/titan/pull/334#issuecomment-3026813148
```

This PR introduces a mechanism to estimate Titan blob raw size based on
metrics collected from Titan during compaction and flush.

A sliding window of recent (raw, compressed) size samples is used to
compute an average expansion factor. This factor is then used to
estimate the raw size of a blob when building SST table properties.
This approach assumes that the compression ratio remains relatively
stable for the workload.

This is not a perfect solution, but a practical workaround. A more

format—was previously proposed[1]. However, changing the data format
would break backward compatibility and prevent rollbacks, which can be
risky.

reviews.body: Overall LGTM

## Pull Request Overview

This PR moves GC oriented compaction functionality from the raftstore module to the gc_worker module, implementing a new auto compaction system. The primary purpose is to consolidate compaction logic in a single location while using region_info_provider for more efficient range collection.

Key changes include:
- Disabling the old raftstore compaction system in favor of the new gc_worker auto compaction
- Implementing a new CompactionRunner that continuously evaluates and compacts regions based on MVCC statistics
- Adding comprehensive configuration options and monitoring metrics for the auto compaction system

title: GC: Estimate compaction effectiveness based on stats and GC safe point
body:
<!--
Thank you for contributing to TiKV!

If you haven't already, please read TiKV's [CONTRIBUTING](https://github.com/tikv/tikv/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md) document.

If you're unsure about anything, just ask; somebody should be along to answer within a day or two.

PR Title Format:
1. module [, module2, module3]: what's changed
2. *: what's changed
-->

### What is changed and how it works?
<!--
Please create an issue first to describe the problem.

There MUST be one line starting with "Issue Number:  " and 
linking the relevant issues via the "close" or "ref".

For more info, check https://github.com/tikv/tikv/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md#linking-issues.
-->

Issue Number: Close #18697

<!--
You could use "commit message" code block to add more description to the final commit message.
For more info, check https://github.com/tikv/tikv/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md#format-of-the-commit-message.
-->
What's Changed:

```commit-message
Add stale versions' newest ts and oldest ts as SST properties.
  - oldest_stale_versions_ts, newest_stale_versions_ts for estimate number of MVCC version.
    imagine some rows are updated.
  - oldest_delete_ts, newest_delete_ts for estimate number of TiKV MVCC deletes.
    imagine some rows are deleted, thus can be physically deleted once past GC safe point
  - I introduced 4 (2 by 2) new stats, because deletes and stale versions are different "dimensions"
  - This is backward compatible. As stale_version_ts, delete_ts will be instantiated with (all entries') min_ts and max_ts if not set in the  SST files created by older TiKV versions.
Estimate num discardable versions based on them and GC safe point, assuming uniform distribution.

An example, assuming:
- Each row got inserted once, updated once, and delete once
- Each operation is 10 secs apart

We can have:
[{r1_t01, v1}, {r1_t11, v2}, {r1_t21, del}, {r2_t02, v1}, {r1_t12, v2}, {r1_t22, del}, {r3_t03, v1}, {r3_t13, v2}, {r1_t23, del}]
oldest_stale_versions_ts: t01, newest_stale_version_ts: t13
oldest_delete_ts: t21, newest_delete_ts: t23
If GC safe point is t22, we know number of discardable stale versions is 2 * 3, while number of discardable deletes is just 1
```

reviews.body:
Bug: Mismatched Version Count in Compact Test

 The `raftstore::compact::CheckThenCompactTopN:CheckRange3` failpoint in `components/raftstore/src/store/worker/compact.rs` contains an incorrect condition. It checks for `range_stats.num_versions == 20` for 'range 3', but the test setup in `test_compact_top_n.rs` creates 15 put versions (3 put operations per key × 5 keys) for this range. Since `range_stats.num_versions` excludes delete entries, the condition should be `num_versions == 15` to match the actual data and the accompanying log message "Found candidate with 15 versions (range 3)". This inconsistency can lead to debugging confusion.

tests/failpoints/cases/test_compact_top_n.rs#L486-L491 https://github.com/tikv/tikv/blob/6d2395fc32a7c4275eff5c3c666e3f056535abc9/tests/failpoints/cases/test_compact_top_n.rs#L486-L491

title: raftstore: asynchronously destroy peer
body:
<!--
Thank you for contributing to TiKV!

If you haven't already, please read TiKV's [CONTRIBUTING](https://github.com/tikv/tikv/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md) document.

If you're unsure about anything, just ask; somebody should be along to answer within a day or two.

PR Title Format:
1. module [, module2, module3]: what's changed
2. *: what's changed
-->

### What is changed and how it works?
<!--
Please create an issue first to describe the problem.

There MUST be one line starting with "Issue Number:  " and 
linking the relevant issues via the "close" or "ref".

For more info, check https://github.com/tikv/tikv/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md#linking-issues.
-->

Issue Number: Close https://github.com/tikv/tikv/issues/18536

<!--
You could use "commit message" code block to add more description to the final commit message.
For more info, check https://github.com/tikv/tikv/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md#format-of-the-commit-message.
-->
What's Changed:

Previously, `destroy_peer` operations executed synchronously on raftstore threads would:
- Perform blocking writes to both raft-engine and RocksDB
- Cause unpredictable latency spikes during peer destruction
- Impact service stability (as reported by customers)


To address this issue, this PR refactors peer destruction into an asynchronous workflow:
- ***Phase 1***: trigger an asynchronous task to clear metadata
   - Initiates destruction by sending `RegionTask::ClearPeerMeta` to region worker
   - Region worker handles all I/O operations:
         - Clears metadata from raft-engine
         - Removes data from RocksDB
   - Returns `SignificantMessage::ReadyToDestroyPeer` upon completion to the raftstore thread
- ***Phase 2***: `ready_to_destroy`
  - `PeerFSM` processes the completion message
  - Finalizes peer destruction without blocking I/O

```commit-message
Moving peer destruction to a background worker prevents I/O operations from causing latency jitters in the main service path.
```

### Related changes

- [ ] PR to update `pingcap/docs`/`pingcap/docs-cn`:
- [ ] Need to cherry-pick to the release branch

If you don't think this PR needs a release note then fill it with None.

Hopefully it’s also useful for other reviewers:

title: coprocessor: provide BatchIndexLookUpExecutor to do index lookup in TiKV side
body: ### What is changed and how it works? Issue Number: Close #18868 What's Changed: ```commit-message - Introduce `BatchIndexLookUpExecutor` to support index look-up in TiKV side. - Rename `SecondarySnapStoreAccessor` to `ExtraSnapStoreAccessor` to make its name easier to understand. ``` The process in BatchIndexLookUpExecutor has two steps: - `IndexScan`: In this phase, it scans the source table and puts the scanned results into an internal buffer. If the src is drain or the buffer size exceeds 256, steps to the `TableLookUp` phase. When in `IndexScan`, all the output results is empty. - `TableLookUp`: In this phase, it builds a `TableTaskIterator` to iterate over the `TableTask`. Each `TableTask` indicates one region. If one TableTask is get, it uses TableTask::build_table_scan_executor to build the `BatchTableScanExecutor` to fetch the final rows. The state machine is: ### Related changes - [ ] PR to update `pingcap/docs`/`pingcap/docs-cn`: - [ ] Need to cherry-pick to the release branch ### Check List Tests - [x] Unit test - [ ] Integration test - [ ] Manual test (add detailed scripts or steps below) - [ ] No code Side effects - [ ] Performance regression: Consumes more CPU - [ ] Performance regression: Consumes more Memory - [ ] Breaking backward compatibility ### Release note ```release-note None ```

What's Changed:

After introducing https://github.com/tikv/tikv/pull/18304, there exists a error on rule-checker with the following error:
```
2025/04/14 14:54:35 failed to replace alert expression: 77:11: group "alert.rules", rule 7, "TiKV_maybe_write_stall": could not parse expression: 1:5: parse error: expected type instant vector in aggregation expression, got range vector
```

This PR is used to correct the this expression.

```commit-message
Fix incorrect aggregation expression with range vector on checking whether
there exists `maybe_write_stall` issues when enabling `flow-control`.
```

reviews.body:
Copilot reviewed 1 out of 1 changed files in this pull request and generated no comments.


<details>
<summary>Comments suppressed due to low confidence (3)</summary>

**metrics/alertmanager/tikv.rules.yml:77**
* The removal of the range selector [10m] ensures that an instant vector is provided for aggregation. Please confirm that this change does not remove desirable smoothing behavior over time.
```
expr: max(tikv_scheduler_l0_avg) by (instance) > 80
```
**metrics/alertmanager/tikv.rules.yml:82**
* Ensure that the adjustment from a range vector to an instant vector is intentional for both occurrences of the alert expression. This change corrects the parsing error but may alter the metric behavior if smoothing over time was needed.
```
expr:  max(tikv_scheduler_l0_avg) by (instance) > 80
```
**metrics/alertmanager/tikv.rules.yml:86**
* [nitpick] The summary text has been updated to reflect that an average value is now evaluated instead of a count. Please verify that this accurately describes the alert’s intent.
```
summary: TiKV the average number of L0 files exceeds 80
```
</details>

title: cdc: add watchdog for cdc conn (#18757)
body:
This is an automated cherry-pick of #18757

<!--
Thank you for contributing to TiKV!

If you haven't already, please read TiKV's [CONTRIBUTING](https://github.com/tikv/tikv/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md) document.

If you're unsure about anything, just ask; somebody should be along to answer within a day or two.

PR Title Format:
1. module [, module2, module3]: what's changed
2. *: what's changed
-->

### What is changed and how it works?
<!--
Please create an issue first to describe the problem.

There MUST be one line starting with "Issue Number:  " and 
linking the relevant issues via the "close" or "ref".

For more info, check https://github.com/tikv/tikv/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md#linking-issues.
-->


Issue Number: Close #18169 

The main intent is to resolve a problem where the CDC sink's memory quota increases indefinitely after a downstream TiCDC node disconnects. This leak persists until the TiKV instance is manually restarted. The underlying cause is suspected to be dangling connections that are not properly released after the downstream becomes unavailable.

In summary, this PR resolves the issue through the following steps:

1. Introduce Watchdog: A background task is created specifically for monitoring.

2. Register Connections: New CDC connections are registered with the watchdog upon creation.

3. Periodic Checks: The watchdog periodically checks the last_flush_time of each connection against a timeout.

4. Release Connections: If a connection is determined to be stuck, the watchdog forcibly disconnects it, releasing its associated memory and resources.

This mechanism ensures that dangling connections are promptly cleaned up even if a downstream TiCDC node becomes unresponsive, preventing the unbounded memory growth and improving the system's stability.

<!--
You could use "commit message" code block to add more description to the final commit message.
For more info, check https://github.com/tikv/tikv/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md#format-of-the-commit-message.
-->
What's Changed:

```commit-message

```

### Related changes

- [ ] PR to update `pingcap/docs`/`pingcap/docs-cn`:
- [x] Need to cherry-pick to the release branch

### Check List

Tests <!-- At least one of them must be included. -->

- [ ] Unit test
- [x] Integration test
- [x] Manual test (add detailed scripts or steps below)

1. Make a tikv with failpoint enable. 
```
FAIL_POINT=1 make build
```
2. Start a cluster with this tikv-server.
3. Create 3 changefeeds, and they are normal.
4. Inject the failpoints.
```
curl -X PUT "http://127.0.0.1:20180/fail/cdc_idle_deregister_threshold" -d "return(true)"

curl -X PUT "http://127.0.0.1:20180/fail/cdc_sleep_after_sink_flush"    -d "return(true)"
```
5. It can be seen that all changefeed got stuck and.
6. After 30 seconds, the cdc connections were closed, and the downstream cdc will try to create a new connection 
```
[2025/07/31 16:27:48.884 +08:00] [ERROR] [service.rs:568] ["cdc connection idle for too long, aborting connection"] [seconds_since_last_flush=40] [conn_id=ConnId(217)] [downstream=ipv4:127.0.0.1:49452] [thread_id=170]
[2025/07/31 16:27:48.884 +08:00] [ERROR] [service.rs:568] ["cdc connection idle for too long, aborting connection"] [seconds_since_last_flush=40] [conn_id=ConnId(216)] [downstream=ipv4:127.0.0.1:49452] [thread_id=170]
[2025/07/31 16:27:48.902 +08:00] [ERROR] [service.rs:568] ["cdc connection idle for too long, aborting connection"] [seconds_since_last_flush=40] [conn_id=ConnId(219)] [downstream=ipv4:127.0.0.1:49453] [thread_id=170]
[2025/07/31 16:27:48.902 +08:00] [ERROR] [service.rs:568] ["cdc connection idle for too long, aborting connection"] [seconds_since_last_flush=40] [conn_id=ConnId(218)] [downstream=ipv4:127.0.0.1:49453] [thread_id=170]
[2025/07/31 16:27:49.887 +08:00] [INFO] [service.rs:448] ["cdc connection created"] [conn_id=ConnId(228)] [features="[\"stream-multiplexing\"]"] [downstream=ipv4:127.0.0.1:49737] [thread_id=9]
[2025/07/31 16:27:49.889 +08:00] [INFO] [service.rs:448] ["cdc connection created"] [conn_id=ConnId(229)] [features="[\"stream-multiplexing\"]"] [downstream=ipv4:127.0.0.1:49737] [thread_id=9]
[2025/07/31 16:27:49.904 +08:00] [INFO] [service.rs:448] ["cdc connection created"] [conn_id=ConnId(230)] [features="[\"stream-multiplexing\"]"] [downstream=ipv4:127.0.0.1:49740] [thread_id=10]
[2025/07/31 16:27:49.905 +08:00] [INFO] [service.rs:448] ["cdc connection created"] [conn_id=ConnId(231)] [features="[\"stream-multiplexing\"]"] [downstream=ipv4:127.0.0.1:49740] [thread_id=10]
```
7. Delete all failpoints.
```
curl -X DELETE "http://127.0.0.1:20180/fail/cdc_sleep_after_sink_flush"
curl -X DELETE "http://127.0.0.1:20180/fail/cdc_idle_deregister_threshold"
```
8. Changefeeds resume to normal.

- [ ] Performance regression: Consumes more Memory
- [ ] Breaking backward compatibility

title: [Backport] Resource Group Level CPU Control
body:
<!--
Thank you for contributing to TiKV!

If you haven't already, please read TiKV's [CONTRIBUTING](https://github.com/tikv/tikv/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md) document.

If you're unsure about anything, just ask; somebody should be along to answer within a day or two.

PR Title Format:
1. module [, module2, module3]: what's changed
2. *: what's changed
-->

### What is changed and how it works?
<!--
Please create an issue first to describe the problem.

There MUST be one line starting with "Issue Number:  " and 
linking the relevant issues via the "close" or "ref".

For more info, check https://github.com/tikv/tikv/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md#linking-issues.
-->
This is a cherry-pick of https://github.com/tidbcloud/cloud-storage-engine/pull/4343 from dedicated branch.  
Issue Number: Close #xxx

<!--
You could use "commit message" code block to add more description to the final commit message.
For more info, check https://github.com/tikv/tikv/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md#format-of-the-commit-message.
-->
What's Changed:
## What's Changed

- Added resource-group-level CPU control for coprocessor DAG requests by introducing a global CPU token bucket together with per-resource-group buckets derived from RU quotas.  
- Throttled requests before execution and supported runtime token top-up for long-running requests, with proper timeout/error propagation when CPU tokens cannot be acquired before the request deadline.  
- Added adaptive per-request CPU estimation, per-resource-group estimate overrides, optional burst behavior, and optional throttling for the default resource group.  
- Added a unified read-pool CPU monitor with sliding-window accounting for both global CPU usage and per-resource-group DAG CPU usage, and used it to drive dynamic refill-rate adjustment.  
- Made CPU throttle settings online-updatable and kept resource-group quota/bucket state synchronized when configs or resource group metadata change.  
- Improved observability with config validation/template updates, structured logs, new Prometheus metrics, and Grafana panels for token allocations, bucket availability/capacity, refill rates, CPU usage ratios, and wait/collection latency.  
- Included follow-up comment and compile/test fixes for the new CPU control path.  

[Test Report](https://pingcap.feishu.cn/wiki/UMq2wHFv8ioCwAknsnzcCv2Knmf)  

### Related changes

title: release-8.5.5: backport hot read cpu reporting
body:
### What is changed and how it works?

Issue Number: ref tikv/tikv#19373

What's Changed:

```commit-message
Release-8.5.5 conflict resolution:
- `Cargo.lock`: kept ours (kvproto already updated).
- `components/tikv_util/src/thread_name_prefix.rs`: new file that does not exist on release-8.5.5; accepted as-is and registered the module in `tikv_util/src/lib.rs`.
- `components/raftstore/src/store/worker/split_controller.rs` / `src/read_pool.rs`: import ordering differences plus new `thread_name_prefix` imports.
- `components/resource_metering/src/model.rs`: core difference — `RawRecord` on release-8.5.5 only has 3 fields (no `network_in_bytes`, etc.). Manually added `ThreadPoolType`, `RegionCpuRecord`, and the `unified_read_cpu_time` / `scheduler_cpu_time` fields plus `add_cpu_time()` method on top of the ours version.
- `components/resource_metering/src/recorder/sub_recorder/cpu.rs`: merged imports (added `ThreadPoolType`, deduplicated `RawRecords`).
- `components/resource_metering/src/reporter/mod.rs`: added `..Default::default()` in test code; dropped `test_reporter_region_data` test (depends on topsql network/io functionality not present on release-8.5.5).
- Cleaned up unused imports: `STATS_MONITOR_THREAD`, `GRPC_SERVER_THREAD`, `UNIFIED_READ_POOL_THREAD`.
```
